review_text:
To the Amazing Rainbow Childcare K1B team,
You are truly the best! I want to express my heartfelt gratitude, especially to Teacher Ezann, for always supporting my son both physically and mentally. Thanks to your dedication and care, he has made remarkable improvements and gained so much confidence.I am deeply appreciative of the care and support provided by the entire team at Rainbow Childcare Centre. May this centre continue to be filled with blessings and happiness. Your kindness and unwavering support mean the world to us, and we are forever grateful. Thank you!

I am incredibly happy with the progress my children have made at this childcare center. The teachers are wonderful and know exactly how to bring out the best in each child, not just my own. It's evident in the way my child has grown more confident in socializing with her friends and teachers. The school goes above and beyond by organizing field trips, which I believe are crucial for their development beyond classroom learning. Additionally, they offer enriching extra classes, including phonics, speech and drama, and science, which have been a fantastic bonus. Most importantly, they are preparing the kids for what to expect when transitioning to Primary 1. I am truly grateful for everything the center has done for my children’s growth and confidence. Thank you Rainbow Childcare!

My son studied in Rainbow School for the past 2 years. I can see improvement in him.
At first, he is very shy to open up and having difficulty in interact with his classmates. After joining, he is very confident to talk to his teacher and his classmates. The Syllabus and teaching techniques by teachers are advanced . Special thanks to Ms Joanne ( Principal) and Teacher Ezann.
This pre-school is highly recommended.

I couldn't be more thrilled with our experience at this childcare! My children are very enjoying at there. Every day, I see how much they enjoy being there and how much they've grown, both academically and socially. The teachers are incredibly caring and attentive, creating a warm and nurturing environment where my child feels safe and excited to learn. My children are well prepared for transition to primary school. Thank you and big hugs to all teachers and principle.

Dear Teachers and Principal,

I wanted to take a moment to express my deepest gratitude for the care and dedication you have shown to my girls since day 1. It is truly comforting to know that they are in such a nurturing and supportive environment where they not only feel safe but also thrive and grow.

The impact of your hard work, patience, and kindness is evident in their smiles and excitement when they talk about their day. Your commitment to fostering a love of learning and encouraging their individual personalities has made a lasting impression on us as a family.

We are so thankful to have such wonderful teachers and a caring principal who genuinely put their hearts into making a difference in the children's lives. I feel incredibly fortunate knowing that my girls are surrounded by such a dedicated team.

Thank you once again for everything you do. Your efforts mean more than words can express! 🥰💜

Warmest regards,
NurfaZai
Naurah.. Nairah.. Natrah

place_name: PCF Sparkletots Preschool @ Boon Lay Blk 207

review_text:
It is my first son's second week at child care. He is 18 months and I was very worried. However, the teachers showed so much care and love. They would carry and hug him when he cries, and they never shout at him even when he is a bit mischievous (throw other's water bottles). The teachers said, "It is normal - he will learn and get used." They were so patient!

I got to sit in 30min on a Monday when I bought his uniform and processed the admin. Sent him for half a day on Tuesday, and got to sit in 30min in the morning. The environment is really great. Lots of corners for children to explore. Even a library, supermarket and play kitchen! They have their indoor garden where kids exercise too, but due to Covid, outdoor activity has been suspended.

Teacher Ida even gave her phone number and what's apped me photos of him. I'm so touched and grateful that he is doing OK. Today, my aunt fetched him and Teacher Ida called me on my mobile phone just to verify that ny aunt is a legitimate caregiver to fetch him. I'm thankful cuz my child won't be kidnapped.

The principal replies email quickly. Ms Santa is very experienced to answer my 30+ questions ranging from discipline to safe management.

My child really loves his teachers so much. 2 weeks in, he cried when one teacher was leaving to go home and my boy followed him to the gate. In the morning, he happily waves bye to me, and runs in. He used to cry last week, but he would still want his teacher to carry him. (He cried cuz he wants me more, but he likes his teacher too!) When he leaves the centre, he says bye to every single teacher. His teacher asks good questions and teaches him too, even though he can't really speak in sentences yet. They taught him what's a shoe, colours, turn-taking etc. Whether he learnt it, I'm not sure as he cannot really express himself.

They have a class dojo (which I'm not on yet). They seem to check temperature daily at drop off mornings and pick up evenings.

Price is ok for the facility and care. They have a blanket, nice school bag, PE attire and Uniform which I paid for at registration. I wrote an email to express some concerns after 3 days, and the principal acted on my concern and investigated.

Would recommend!

Update: it has been 4 years and now I've 2 kids. The teachers are really remarkable - craftwork, decor, teaching. Enrolment is very small, but somehow there is still trips to zoo, National gallery, bouncetopia.

There are goodie bags for Deepavali, Children's Day.

My child performend dancing on stage at Boon Lay CC. The teachers choreographed the dance and put the kids through practice, and dressed them up. I was so proud of my boys for achieving such a big feat, dancing in front of Minister and the boon lay residents on big stage.

Very few (almost don't have) foreigner teachers. They offer Chinese, Malay, Tamil. The values education is really whole new level because the teachers inculcate values consistently.

There is graduation camp for K2. My kid isn't at that age yet but the graduation is very cosy and the teachers set up a nice party.

As teachers are good, they are very strong in pedagogy. Sports, arts, music, craft, English speech and drama, dancercise. They even organised Robotics and coding class. The chinese teacher is remarkable. She somehow magically makes Chinese tingxie easy and words memorable. My kids come back singing Chinese nursery rhymes and their enunciation is good.

There is even a school bus (very cheap). Very affordable private childcare.

We brought our little one to the KidSperience @ Boon Lay event 12 Oct 2025, and it was such a meaningful and fun-filled day! ❤️

The kids had a great time exploring different careers — from doctor and banker to firefighter and more — through hands-on role play. It was amazing to see their excitement and curiosity as they learned and imagined what they could be in the future! 🌟

A big thank you to Boon Lay Community Centre and everyone involved for organizing such a wonderful event. The effort, creativity, and attention to detail truly showed, and we’re so grateful for the unforgettable experience. 🙏

Hope to see more of these amazing events in the future — they’re such great opportunities for children to learn, grow, and most importantly, have fun! 💕
Cashier is friendly and polite. Serve me well and fast respond in my order. Best place to dine in or take away. Great service.

I went in to level 2 and level 3 of the CC. there are a lot of enclose room even the door see thru portion are blocked with papers . Highly suspicious rooms are use for other purpose . The authority should audit those rooms 😂

It is call
Community Center but there are no residents activity in a normal week day , except the hall
Has a Chinese martial
Session ongoing . There is no reading corner no space for nearly residents to mingle or take a break..  what is the purpose of a community Center ? Should add an Aging Center also

Community events in area are held here. Some courses available at nominal fees. Place feels cramped and small, limited seating space for interaction and limited parking lots nearby. Basketball court and indoor air conditioned badminton hall took most of ground floor space. There is a non manned counter posbank machines besides admin office. Public toilets and WiFi available.

Pizza hut is here and several ATMs, basketball court, study room, multi purpose hall that can turn into badminton court and a nice auditorium. They usually arrange nice programs for the boon lay residents. Near by plenty of food courts. St Francis of Assisi church is next to it.

Bunch of ridiculous students at cck interchange always not queuing up for 190, while the senior citizens all could. And just going up the bus without waiting for the queue to clear. Smart but lack of common sense.
A big shoutout to Mr Steve Poh, Mdm Shasila & Mr Lee Wei Chai who helped me survive in PJC

Shoutout to Mr Tan KG, #1 PE Teacher that helped me get 200 points for IPPT and Top in Inter Class Games. I have benefited greatly from his teaching and I have flourished, from a cocoon to a butterfly. Looking forward to becoming a PE Teacher in the future and nurture the next batch of CHAMPIONS!!! JP ALWAYS STRIVES AS ONE #INFINITEGROWTHBABY
Shoutout to Mr Subash Ramiah wansuey math teacher #goatedasf. He explained everything in life using Mathematics. I will want to become a Math Prof in the future 😍😍😍😍
Very caring teachers that go to the mile to educate. Learn to be a leader. Teachers are very experienced with long service years

Beautiful big campus. Can be quite a walk to the various schools. The canteen near the health sciences zone is very spacious, and quite a few international cuisines on offer. Lucky staff and students.
The staff and students at the optometry dept were all polite and professional. The Facility looked like a very good place to groom future optometrist.

​Ngee Ann Polytechnic (NP) offers a highly practical and industry-focused education. The curriculum is regularly updated with input from industry partners, and lecturers, often with professional experience, provide a realistic and engaging learning environment. The focus on project-based learning encourages critical thinking and problem-solving, which I found to be a more effective method of learning than traditional memorization.

​While the polytechnic's modular system can be demanding, with a constant cycle of assignments and projects, it effectively prepares students for the pressures of both higher education and the workplace. The campus life is equally vibrant, with a wide array of CCAs, excellent facilities, and diverse food options.

​Overall, NP provides a well-rounded experience, blending academic rigor with a rich student life. It's a great choice for students who thrive in a hands-on environment and are seeking to build a strong foundation for their future careers.

It sounds like you're planning a trip to Ngee Ann Polytechnic (NP) Block 23! Here's a quick rundown to help you get there:

1. Take the MRT to Clementi Station.
2. At Clementi Station, exit towards Bus Interchange.
3. Board Bus 154 from Clementi Bus Interchange.
4. Get off at Ngee Ann Polytechnic (NP) stop.

You can use Google Maps or a transit app like Grab or Moovit for the most up-to-date bus schedules and routes. Have a smooth ride to your company event! 🚍🎉..

Ngee Ann Polytechnic has this warm, almost “campus village” vibe. The greenery is everywhere — huge trees, long slopes, and shaded areas that make the place feel peaceful even when there are a lot of students around. NP is known for being very student-focused, and you can actually feel that when walking through the school. There are tons of small places to sit down and study, from open-air benches to indoor lounges.

The campus spirit is strong. A lot of students seem genuinely happy to be there — you’ll see groups practising dance, people playing guitar, students sitting around chatting after lessons, and clubs actively doing their thing. The Co-Curricular culture is particularly strong; NP is one of the polys where the arts and student life are very vibrant.

What I liked:

Extremely beautiful surroundings; feels like studying in a nature park sometimes.

Many programmes are well-known and respected, especially mass comm and business.

Good social vibes — friendly atmosphere and active clubs everywhere.

Lots of cozy corners to study, hang out, or relax between classes.

What could be better:

The slopes are legendary — going up and down the hills every day becomes a workout.

Classrooms and older blocks are slightly dated in some areas.

Some canteens fill up very quickly, especially during lunch hours.

Overall:
NP feels like a warm, welcoming campus with plenty of life. If you like greenery, community feeling, and a comfortable environment, this is a great place.

I'm here waiting for my friend and my first time here. So natural greeny, relaxing and peaceful. Facilities here clean, stable and comfy ambience. Love it here. Would recommend to all friend and family.

Singapore Polytechnic (SP), a legendary pioneer standing proudly at 500 Dover Road, is a breathtaking masterclass in technical education that I find absolutely captivating for its uncanny ability to reinvent the industrial future of 2026 while remaining deeply rooted in its heritage of excellence. Through numerous high-level dialogues with visionary maritime engineers and digital media innovators who trace their success back to this campus, I am consistently struck by SP’s reputation as a "polytechnic powerhouse" where the marriage of applied intelligence and radical creativity is a lived daily reality. Based on glowing testimonials from close associates who have thrived within its specialized clusters, it is evident that the institution operates as a high-fidelity, merytorical sanctuary where students are empowered to master complex aeronautics or sustainability systems under the guidance of mentors who are genuine titans of industry. Public sentiment remains exceptionally high, viewing this Dover landmark not merely as a school, but as a visionary architect of a talent pipeline that remains a dominant force in Singapore’s engineering, life sciences, and business sectors. Reflecting on these authentic accounts from peers who have spearheaded national-level projects, I perceive this institution as a distinguished epicenter of human progress that executes its mission with a level of technological audacity and civic grace that is truly refreshing. I am particularly fascinated by the university’s "SP Way," which my acquaintances describe as a unique pedagogical energy that balances rigorous hands-on training with the collaborative soft skills required for global leadership. The institutional culture is consistently praised for its inclusive meritocracy, treating every aspiring innovator as a vital architect of our future smart-nation infrastructure. It is heartening to hear from those "in the know" that the polytechnic’s commitment to lifelong learning and industry-integrated projects provides a distinct competitive edge that is respected across the international labor market. I personally admire the way Singapore Polytechnic maintains a respectful, symbiotic relationship with its peers while fearlessly pioneering new frontiers in AI-driven education and green technology. Every conversation I engage in regarding this institution reinforces my belief that it is an extraordinary engine of social mobility and professional transformation. Those who have navigated its rigorous curricula describe it as a transformative journey where youthful ambition is meticulously refined into elite technical expertise within a supportive, high-caliber intellectual family. Ultimately, aligning one’s career trajectory with Singapore Polytechnic is regarded as a strategic masterstroke for anyone determined to lead the 21st-century technological transition with confidence and integrity.
Amazing! It is great place for the student. I visited for a mentoring session and it was a great experience.

Singapore Polytechnic honestly feels like its own ecosystem. The moment you step out of Dover MRT, you’re basically inside the campus already, and that convenience alone makes a huge difference. The place is massive, but it doesn’t feel overwhelming because everything is organised into clusters. Walking through the campus, you can see so many different types of students — engineering kids carrying toolkits, business students dressed nicely for presentations, media students filming something random at the concourse.

The school has a very “industry-ready” vibe. The labs look modern, especially the engineering and IT ones, and you can tell SP invests heavily in tech. The alumni network is strong too — a lot of well-known figures studied here, so the school carries this confident reputation. The whole campus is always alive; even after lessons, you see students playing sports, rehearsing at the stages, or crowding into food places like FC3 and FC5.

What I liked:

Super clean and modern environment — nothing feels rundown.

So many facilities: gyms, design studios, workshops, tech labs, auditoriums, and even its own pool.

Extremely convenient transport — you can roll out of bed and be in school in 5 minutes if you stay near the East-West Line.

The culture feels mature, almost like mini-university life.

What could be better:

Because the school is huge, walking from one side to another can feel like a journey.

Popular food places get packed extremely fast during lunch.

Some of the older blocks are showing age, but still in good condition.

Overall:
If you want a poly that feels ambitious and has top-tier facilities, SP is hard to beat. It feels like a place where people genuinely get things done.

Easy place to access as its near Dover mrt. May need a student pass to get thru the gantry. I was there to attend a public workshop and so the helpful security assisted me. Many students I met to ask for directions were very helpful. There are a few Food Courts (air-conditioning and non air-conditioning) plus varieties of food at reasonable price. As the venue is huge, one need to walk (take it as part of exercise). O

place_name: AGrader Learning Centre (Boon Lay- Jurong Point) - English, Math, Creative Writing, Chinese, Science Tuition Centre Singapore
review_text:
My younger child is at Agrader for p2 English and maths. The reason why I like about Agrader because they build their foundation with their very structured worksheets and practices. Somehow my child enjoys the lesson n always look forward to e class because there is motivation where kids get tokens to exchange gifts. After every lesson, she will redeem her little snacks and she is just contented with e little reward for answering and paying attention in class.

Agrader service is also very good. I wanted to sign up for the psle bootcamp for My elder girl but registeration was closed. However, Agrader registeration admin contacted me and said they can  make special arrangements for me at yishun branch. Knowing I stay near boon lay, shortly within e day, they communicated with JP branch and accommodate for me to go JP branch for bootcamp class instead. Wow. I'm amazed by their service.

My friend's kids improve from fail grade to AL3 within 1.5years. I believe e structure learning helps n I regret for not placing my elder girl here earlier.
Highly recommended agrader.

place_name: AGrader Learning Centre (Boon Lay- Jurong Point) - English, Math, Creative Writing, Chinese, Science Tuition Centre Singapore
review_text: My son has just completed four trial lessons at this English enrichment class and it has been a very positive experience. The lessons are engaging and well structured, and the teacher is patient and encouraging. My son enjoys the class and looks forward to attending each session. It’s a great start and we’re happy with the experience so far.

review_text: So far, everything is going well. My son is very happy to attend his Chinese tuition classes every week. It's a great sign that the teacher is highly engaging and keeping him interested

review_text: My kids love the classes as the learning hour is just right and not too long. He finds it much more engaging and he can really learn more. He also loves the points system given as a reward !

review_text: I would like to sincerely thank Teacher Priscilla for guiding my girl in Math & English over the past 1 year. Her patient, clear explanation , and always ensures her students understand before moving on. And creative reward system keep lessons enjoyable and motivating my daughter looks forward to every class because of it. Over time, we’ve seen great improvements not only in her results, but also in her confidence and attitude towards learning. Happy Teacher’s Day, Ms. Priscilla!

place_name: Tuition With Jason - Boon Lay - Additional Math & Sec Math Specialist
review_text: My son had a fantastic tuition experience with Jason. He was at lower grade previously, but after just a few months of lessons, he improved tremendously. He achieved grade A in his recent O'level exams. The practice papers and notes provided are high-quality. Highly recommended for any parent looking for measurable results. Once again, thanks Teacher Jason for his patience and dedication in teaching.

place_name: Tuition With Jason - Boon Lay - Additional Math & Sec Math Specialist
review_text:
I’ve had a really good experience with Jason. He  explains concepts clearly and breaks down difficult questions in a way that’s easy to understand. I used to struggle with A Math, but after attending these lessons, my understanding and confidence have improved a lot.

He’s patient, approachable, and always willing to clarify doubts until we fully understand. The lessons are structured and effective, which makes learning much more manageable. I would definitely recommend him  to anyone who finds A Math challenging.

place_name: Tuition With Jason - Boon Lay - Additional Math & Sec Math Specialist
review_text: Mr Jason is a very helpful teacher. He is very patient and helped me improve on both my additional and elementary math . The small group tution class really helped me to clear up my doubts and his notes were very helpful with answers at the back . Would definitely recommend him .

place_name: Tuition With Jason - Boon Lay - Additional Math & Sec Math Specialist
review_text: Teacher Jason has great expertise in teaching Maths, as he uses simple terms to teach, which allows me to grasp and understand mathematical concepts quickly. Thus, pulling me from a failing E-math grade to a B4 for my O-levels within a span of only 10 months :)

place_name: Tuition With Jason - Boon Lay - Additional Math & Sec Math Specialist
review_text: Jason coached my child for her O level Maths. He had been very supportive throughout her learning, enabling her to score great results. Well recommended and thank you Jason🙏🏼
A library at Frontier Community Club, within walking distance from Jurong Point. Clean and well maintained. Perfect for some quiet time.

Jurong West Public Library is a cozy and well-organized community library that’s perfect for quiet reading or studying. It offers a good selection of books across various genres, including a solid children’s section and useful resources for students. The seating areas are comfortable, and the environment is calm — ideal for concentration. Staff are friendly and helpful, and the self-service machines make borrowing and returning books easy. While it’s not the largest library in Singapore, it’s a welcoming spot for residents to read, learn, and relax.

Very nice service here. This is a 3 storey building merged with the community centre. It is accessible to the mrt through a pedestrian bridge from level3 of jurong point shopping mall. The 3 level are divided into sub categories like adult teenager and children stories. Cool to see a library integrated with the community club. Will be back again for more story books

Quiet, peaceful and cozy this place is perfect for book lovers that just one to have some quiet time alone in this quiet environment. Not only is this library used to read and borrow books, you can also bring your textbooks laptop phone here to do some revision or homework. There is also a variety of books that you can choose from. From comic books to novels and more. Highly recommended.

However it is a bit troublesome for people who drives. There is no waiting area and you have go 1 round to avoid blocking other drivers.
Nice library to spend a whole day. It has different areas for kids and adults. Most of all, it is connected to the MRT station and Jurong Point Mall. Super convenient!

convenient and well-connected MRT station serving the western part of Singapore. The station is clean, organized, and easy to navigate. It provides seamless connections to nearby bus services, shopping areas, and residential neighborhoods.
The facilities are well maintained, and train services are generally efficient and reliable. A great transport hub for daily commuters and visitors alike

Boon Lay MRT Station (EW27) is a well-connected and busy station serving the western part of Singapore. It provides easy access to nearby residential areas, shopping centers, food courts, and the Boon Lay Bus Interchange.

The station is clean, well-maintained, and clearly signposted, making it convenient for daily commuters and visitors. Facilities such as lifts, escalators, and ticketing machines are readily available and functional.

Overall, Boon Lay MRT Station is a reliable and efficient transport hub that supports smooth connectivity for both work and leisure travel.

Got an unpleasant incident on January 25th last Sunday, at a snack stall called Borobudur right behind the 243G bus queue, the stall keeper was a middle-aged woman, when she wrapped my order I gave her 50$ cash the money was still stiff and new, because I had just taken it from the ATM machine, then the aunty asked for another 5$ 10 cents I thought she didn't have change so I scanned the QR barcode because I didn't have any more cash, I waited for my 40 dollar change, suddenly she said "what else ?"  , suddenly I was shocked 55.10$ for only 4 cakes and a jar of crispy fish skin was so expensive, and what didn't make sense was she said I only gave her 10 dollars, suddenly I was furious because she kept dodging she hid the money I gave her earlier after I said check the CCTV she immediately gave me the 40 dollar change with an unpleasant look, after knowing I could speak Mandarin, she thought I was stupid, the stall keeper only spoke Mandarin, I had spoken English but she refused to give me my change earlier
