The scrolling screen schedule are badly placed and turns so slowly that one has to stop to check timings than matching walking pace. The customer service are just patronising. Terminal does not match up to standards deserving in SG and the huge population in Jurong.

Boon Lay Interchange is a vital transportation hub located in the far west of Singapore, seamlessly connecting commuters to various parts of the city. Serving as a gateway for residents and students alike, it offers convenient access to Nanyang Technological University (NTU), making it an essential stop for many. The interchange is well-designed, featuring a spacious layout that minimizes congestion during peak hours, ensuring a smooth transit experience.

One of the standout features of Boon Lay Interchange is its extensive bus services, including my personal favorite, Bus 198. This bus route provides a reliable and efficient way to travel to work, weaving through the scenic routes of the western region while connecting to other key transport nodes. The frequency of buses is commendable, allowing for minimal waiting times.

Moreover, the interchange is equipped with various amenities, including food stalls and retail shops, making it a convenient spot for quick meals or last-minute purchases. Overall, Boon Lay Interchange exemplifies Singapore's commitment to efficient public transport, fostering connectivity and accessibility for all. Whether you’re a student heading to NTU or a commuter like me, this interchange serves as a crucial link to the bustling heart of the city.

Update: 14 Feb 26

Just had my first visit to the reopened Boon Lay Place Wet Market! It’s easily one of the largest and best-organized markets in Western Singapore now. The stalls are well-spaced, and the variety of items is incredibly extensive. The entire space is bright, clean, and the improved ventilation makes a huge difference. Recommended for fresh groceries.

__________________
Boon Lay Place Food Village has reopened! 😃

So glad Boon Lay Place Food Village has reopened! Just had a quick economic bee hoon breakfast there. The place looks fantastic after the 3-month renovation — brighter, more spacious, and much more comfortable than before! 👍🏻👍🏻

Recently reopened Boon Lay Food Place which has been around for the longest time.

The newly upgraded place is much more cleaner, cooler and have more space with the center segment flattened and added with more tables and seats.

Overall, the place is even brighter and houses many good food options here. When there’s a queue, typically it is good food here.

Only giving it 4-stars because the prices have all gone up, which is pretty sad as gradually hawker food do not feel as affordable as they used to be, and sadly it is a common action after any new renovations or upgrades. Just hope it is sustainable for the F&B people, as well as the patrons as most people who live around this area are much older people.

Will still frequent this place!

Although it's newly renovated hawker centre, but many areas are still required improvement, eg. cleanliness, seating, tray return etc. Majority food selling here is good and still within reasonable price, but few coffee stalls are selling drinks at higher prices compared with kopitiam or food court and quality is poor. Most stall owners are selfish and inconsiderate, they chope and reserve the tables in front of their stalls, just for the convenience of placing their stuffs, which cause diners are difficult in getting seats.

Lotsa great stalls here. They underwent renovation recently with better ventilation, cleaner environment which makes it more comfortable for patrons. My personal favourite power nasilemak, 擂茶饭，鱼汤, prawn noodle
lots of nice hawker foods with plenty of seats and tables. Many parking lots and wheel chairs accessible with toilet and fans facilities. Great for lunch & dinner.

Very crowded even on weekday morning. Soaking in the surrounding booths selling CNY goodies. Had the Beo Crescent Signature Lor Mee. The volume is big with lots of ingredients of pork, egg, fried fish etc. lots of good food in this Food centre. Highly recommended.

A popular local food centre among residents in the area, this spot offers a variety of options for vegans, vegetarians, and anyone looking for meatless meals.
There are two Chinese vegetarian stalls - Wan Yuan Vegetarian and Tofu Vegetarian Food — both of which are vegan-friendly and allium-free. You'll also find a Delisnacks stall serving traditional vegan and vegetarian snacks. In addition, some stalls, such as Thunder Bowl, offer dishes with vegetarian options.

There are a number of stalls i like. Lots of choices within this hawker centre.

The traditional Yam Cake and 肠粉 stall,
the Chicken porridge and chinese salad stall, Porridge stall that sells pork trotters, 擂茶，云吞面，酿豆腐，fishball noodles and Tang gui duck & roast pork rice. Recently another new stall Hello Fish. Pretty good. I tried the Assam style.

My favourite beef noodles stall had gone. So sadz.

I wish the tables can be cleaned promptly and concerned party to monitor the issue of excessive crows, magpies and pigeons picking food around the hawker centre. Some crows can be aggressive too.

​Update: 12 Mar 26

Had the $7 set at 潮汕卤味 Kway Chap for brunch. The taste was just so-so, but the main issue was the chilli, way too much vinegar. All in all, this Kway Chap is not to my personal taste, so I won't be visiting again. ⭐️⭐️

_________________________
First time trying the Nasi Lemak at 隆兴 today, as my usual stalls at Boon Lay Hawker Centre are temporarily closed for renovation. The rice was surprisingly good and fragrant, and the chili was spot-on. I paid $3.80 for fish, luncheon meat, and otah.
Definitely a good temporary fix.

Exploring to eat at a different food centre around my neighbourhood and this place came up.

Searched for some stalls to eat and shortlisted a few. After trying a few, yong tau foo, wonton noodles, chicken with porridge, lei cha rice and kueh tutu.. it was alright and didn't give me the wow or yum!
YTF: ingredients are decent and would say pretty fresh and you get to add your own condiments
WTN: char siew was on the dry side, noodles cooked to al-dente but sauce and chilli were lacking depth and this stall I went had no green chilli
Chicken Porr: porridge was smooth but chicken was little disappointing as I was expecting . Smooth but little tough. Succulent and smooth chicken that goes well with piping hot porridge...chilli didnt have that kick, was mild.
LCR: opted for brown rice without the green soup and add bittergourd+egg plant(both were yummy). Ingredients of tofu, different variety of vegetables were on the bland side which makes it healthier. Asked for chilli and it was some dark brownish looking dip that was meh. A chilli that enhances the flavour of rice+ingredients, that would be great!
KTT: mini..4 for $3 where customers can mix coconut+ peanuts. + point: bite size.  - points: not made upon ordering. Had done and kept in some kind of warmer and they just packed for customers. Fillings were decent to the amount of skin.
After eating, there are shops for one to explore around the neighbourhood.

90 Boon Lay Place Food Court is one of the standout food centers in the area. It’s clean, well-maintained, and offers a fantastic variety of food. My personal go-tos are Er Jie Rojak, Old Street Scissors Cut Curry Rice, and the fried rice from the Zhi Char stall.
Separately, can really sense the high level of competition here, as quite a few stalls have closed recently and are currently waiting for new tenants to take over.

In all fairness, my fave stalls like the ErJie Rojak, cai peng, curry rice are good & I would give at least four stars.
Drinks are drinks, except for 10cts more or less for the hot drinks varies coffee shop by coffee shops.

What I do not agree, is charging 20cts for half a cup of hot water, to top up a cup of Chinese tea ordered earlier, that had cooled down.  If I had requested for a fresh cup of hot water, am happy to pay.
However, for the same cup & only requesting half a cup (as it’s half full), charging for this is daylight robbery.
I shd hv asked the neighbouring stall where we bought our food, and am sure she’ll be happy to top-up foc.

Some stalls still open either 24hrs or until last night. Arrived at around 12am. Dinner crowd is crazy packed.

Carpark charges in photo attached.

Great spot for gathering
Open air seating are wonderful
Very nice lighting and toilets are clean
Lots of parking spaces
Nearby bus stop
Great food choices
Speed cooking
Clean environment
Value for money
Ordered 2 noodles 1 rice. The noodles so salty as if salt is free and totally not like the photos shown. Taste so bad! Waited for very long as there seems like no system. Waste money only.

It will be great if they can deep fried the chicken to keep the crispness and juiciness

The rice is very fragrant. Drumstick is very skinny. Likely a diet chicken.  Chilli is nice.

I really don't understand why so many people are giving it good reviews. I've been to this store many times and haven't found anything special about it. Last time I bought two packs, and my wife and I both had diarrhea for two days. I think it must be something wrong with the chicken; the taste is still weird, just like before.
Never going to support this shop again! Very disappointing!. We are paying for the food and the quantity packed is so little!. Don't treat your customers like this!. 👎👎👎
$5 to or the Ayam Berempah set! Very economical

One of the worst kueh I've eaten in my years of living... Again n again being disappointed with their kueh quality.. but I chose to give chance.. today is just the worst. ondeh2 was 90¢ each . It has no aroma , the filling is not gula Malacca at all.. not forgetting the glutinous ball is so bland... Just tasteless...Feels so cheated !

Had read the CNA story when it came out and mentally noted about the home-cafe. Finally Visited on 08-02-2026
Luckily we got seats and everyone else took it to go. We tried hot and cold matcha latte, strawberry X yogurt matcha, white coffee, Earl Gray shaken and slice of banana cake. Unfortunately the banana pudding and focaccia were sold out!
The drinks were made fresh to order and it showed in the flavour profile. I like that each drink was about enhancing the flavour with no additional funk. The hosts were descriptive with information and I thank them for the hospitality. Banana cake and matcha latte was a perfect pairing.

Asked for recommendations on coffee and matcha so we got pandan latte and strawberry x yogurt matcha, and both were really gooodd! :D

Very nice home cafe with good music chill vibes.

Love the poster outside the gate too.

P.s. Got a banana cake and it was really moist and soft!! Will be up to try their other food options next time.

(invite; but only raw and honest opinions oki)
visiting the noob coffee marks my first HBB visit in the west! 😆😆

here’s what we got!
🍵earl grey matcha (w oat milk)
the matcha’s intensity is impressive, very gao! interestingly, I found out that they actually make their own earl grey syrup. while it leans on the sweeter side so it might be a bit much for some, I found it to be the perfect counter-balance to the matcha’s earthiness. it rounds out the flavors so beautifully that you get all the richness without any harsh bitterness.

☕️dirty earl (w oat milk)
since it was our first visit, the owner recommended their Earl Grey Coffee, which is one of the Noob’s favourites! think of it as a sophisticated, 'Ang Moh' Yuan Yang!! the blend of earl grey and coffee is spot on. my sister, who is a coffee addict, is absolutely obsessed with it

Highly highly recommend their earl grey matcha latte and tnc focaccia!!! Earl grey matcha latte is super fragrant and the focaccia is really fluffy and not too dense. The owner is super nice to talk to as well and my friend and I had such a good time there ☺️ will definitely come back again!

this was not my first time eating their banana pudding, and i always find myself coming back for more! this time, i ordered it for a 10 pax christmas party and my friends finished everything 😋 it has the right amount of sweetness and i love the texture! i have also tried their coffee and matcha (original/strawberry/earl grey), in which all were great! the owners are also really friendly and welcoming :) if you live in the west, i would really recommend everyone to pop by and enjoy matcha & banana pudding at their lovely place 😗 thank you to the noob coffee once again for accomodating to my requests for my christmas party 🎄

place_name: Bok's Kitchen by Hidden Chefs - Singapore
review_text:
It was our first time dining at Bok’s Kitchen, and overall we had a nice experience. We ordered freshly brewed iced lemon tea, truffle carbonara, chilli crab pasta, and crispy pork cheek.

The crispy pork cheek was fantastic, especially when eaten hot, as it tends to lose some of its texture once it cools. The chilli crab pasta had quite a strong level of spiciness, so those who are less tolerant of spicy food may want to take note (not sure if a less spicy option is available). The truffle carbonara was enjoyable, though my husband would have preferred a slightly thicker sauce.

Overall, it was a pleasant dining experience, and we enjoyed trying the dishes

place_name: Bok's Kitchen by Hidden Chefs - Singapore
review_text:
The crispy pork cheek was love at first bite—super crunchy outside, tender inside, and the sweet Thai chilli sauce tied it all together. Portion? Generous.

I went for the grilled chicken burger with sweet potato fries (love that upgrade option ✨). The patty was juicy, though flavour-wise pretty average. Luckily, the sauces made it a messy, sauce-lover’s kind of burger.

Since they accept CDC vouchers, it does get pretty packed and noisy, but hey—good vibes if you’re into the bustle. Overall: not gourmet, but hearty, affordable, and satisfying.

Would Deinfitely come back to abuse the cdc voucher again

place_name: Bok's Kitchen by Hidden Chefs - Singapore
review_text:
This place is a new gem! Definitely deserves our 5-star review.

The char siew rice is a must try! The mushroom soup comes with two pieces of toasted garlic bread. Probably one of the most garlicky breads I have ever had. If you are not a fan of garlic, you might want to skip it, but I personally loved it!

Will definitely be back to try more items on the menu. 😊

place_name: Bok's Kitchen by Hidden Chefs - Singapore
review_text: I completely agree — Bok’s Kitchen is a hidden gem that’s well worth the return trip! The atmosphere is incredibly cozy and welcoming, making you feel right at home. On top of that, the food is delicious and shiok. I’ll definitely be coming back for more.

place_name: Bok's Kitchen by Hidden Chefs - Singapore
review_text:
Visited Bok's Kitchen upon a recommendation by a colleague. If you are driving, it might be a bit hard to spot the place. It's located on the extreme left hand side of the building (when you are facing the building).

For the main dishes, we ordered:
Spinach Mushroom Aglio
Truffle Carbonara
Char Siew Rice Bowl
Salmon Teriyaki Rice Bowl

We prefer the pasta dishes over the rice bowl ones. Portion is considered small but the quality of food is definitely good. Question of quality over quantity 😊

For the side dishes, we ordered:
Bok's Garlic Winglet
Crispy Pork Cheek

Both side dishes are worth trying. Crispy and tasty.

We wanted to try the chili crab pasta that many customers are raving about but it wasn't available during lunch.

We will be back to try the other dishes in the menu. Recommended place to visit if you are in the vicinity.

Jurong Point has really evolved into one of the largest and most comprehensive suburban malls in Singapore over the years. The mall keeps expanding and now offers an impressive variety of restaurants, cafés, retail shops, supermarkets, and leisure options all under one roof. There are also at least two sizeable food courts with plenty of local and international food choices, making it easy to find something for everyone

One of the biggest advantages is the excellent convenience - it has always been directly connected to both the MRT station and the bus interchange, making it extremely accessible rain or shine. Despite being a heartland mall, it feels vibrant and lively with many different sections to explore, from Japanese-themed food streets to family-friendly spaces and lifestyle shops

Jurong Point is one of the more convenient suburban malls in Singapore with a huge variety of shops, supermarkets, restaurants and daily necessities all under one roof. The mall is directly connected to Boon Lay MRT, so accessibility is definitely a big plus.

What I like most is the wide food selection ranging from local hawker-style eateries to proper restaurants and cafes. It’s also spacious enough for long walks without feeling too cramped on weekdays.

However, the mall can get extremely crowded during weekends and peak dining hours, especially around the food areas. Some sections also feel slightly dated compared to newer malls in Singapore, and navigating between the different wings can be a bit confusing initially.

Overall, still a very practical and convenient mall for shopping, dining and everyday errands in the west side of Singapore.

As one of Singapore’s largest suburban malls, Jurong Point serves as a massive retail and lifestyle hub for the West. It’s famous for its immersive themed precincts and endless food options, making it a hotspot for both locals and visitors.
For my family and me living in the West, Jurong Point is our go-to spot for shopping, leisure, and meals because it is just so convenient. Plus, being directly connected to Boon Lay MRT and the Bus Interchange makes it exceptionally accessible.

Didn’t expect tiramisu to hit this good, but here we are at Tirato Tiramisu location at B1 near boast.

Caved and tried two flavours — pistachio and yam — and honestly? No regrets. Just me wondering why I didn’t get more

Pistachio was rich, nutty, and somehow still light? Super creamy, but not the kind that makes you feel jelak. The subtle crunch inside? Chef’s kiss. Definitely feels a little luxe.

Yam caught me off guard — it’s soft, naturally sweet, and has that nostalgic vibe, like something you grew up eating but upgraded. Super comforting and lowkey addictive.

What made it even better:
✨ No pork, no lard, no alcohol — totally kid-friendly (my little cousin loved it)
✨ Freshly made daily — no preservatives, no colouring, nothing artificial
✨ So many flavours to choose from — I was stuck at the counter for 5 mins trying to pick. Spoilt for choice fr.

They’re only $6.80 each, which feels like a steal for the quality.
Very fun shopping at Jurong point. Lots of things to see for Chinese new year from ornaments to cookies in jars and gift boxes. Got god of fortune everywhere too. Pineapple tarts and more ~

I bought tenderloin meat from the counter that sells pork. The staff would weigh on the spot. It says 0.314g. When i reach home to portion the meat into 100g each, i only got +-230g. Where's my balance 80g??

Today (5 Sept 2025) around 1.05pm, an uncle on his PMA crashed into my trolley while we were queuing at the cashier (he was just right behind me). Super dangerous!! If he can hit my trolley, he can easily hit someone else too. I already told two NTUC staff about it. Imagine if it happened to a small kid — no parent would accept that!!

This is one of the oldest Fairprice supermarket & it is very spacious. I have been shopping here for a long time & knows exactly where to find my stuff. But with more supermarkets around, they are not as attractive than before. Other supermarkets are offering higher discounts for senior & cashback, so their prices are not as competitive now.
The most annoying thing is their self check out kiosk, which has a weight balance system on the scanned & unscan items. If you hold on to your scanned item a second longer, the system will hang & require the staff to come and reset. I suppose this is to prevent theft. But how can you treat every customer as a potential thief? This is discrimination.

Shop at boonlay shopping centre today at 11.17am, casher ask me if I have member link card which I say no, then casher use her fairprice app phone hidden at counter, without asking me permission,  very disappointed.  Why didn nobody check or advise her

Today went to FairPrice to buy Ghee on 25/03/26 at 2pm . But can’t find it . Asked the customer service centre. Old lady asked me to go lane 11 . Actually that is not there then went to lane 8  (another staff helped me). After confront the customer service lady , she doesn’t say and never sorry or anything too. Then laughing say she doesn’t know . She thought it is drinking item . Basically not suitable to customer case and useless lady .

I entered this 7-11 and I wanted to get a basket from the cashier here, he said impatiently, "Is there no basket outside?", then he gave it to me from his counter, and then I bought some things, and the bread was marked as 1 yuan discount, but when I checked out, I was charged 1.50 yuan. When I wanted to explain to him, he was very impatient and didn't want to explain to me first, and let the customer behind check out before it was my turn? Helo? I can understand that you made a mistake in the checkout, but I really don't understand why your attitude is so bad? If you are in a bad mood and bring it to work, I suggest you quit. If you have such an employee like yours, you can only wish for yourself😀

Very convenient, friendly staff members.🌟🍦 Get ready for a sweet treat! 🍦🌟

For 4 days only, from July 25th to 28th, enjoy our amazing Buy 1 Get 1 Free offer on Cornetto Royale Unicornetto and White Rose Ex 134ml! 🎉✨

Don't miss out on this magical deal! 🦄🌹
Clean 7-eleven place like you enter town area 7-eleven ..Friendly staff, thanks to Salpiah she very cute and keep on smiling,we need staff like her who so friendly with customer
A compliment for a staff handling work prefessionally and cheerful.. Great job to the malay lady and very patience in doing transaction. 11/06/2025

So horrible, had to wait a whole week for the slurpee machine to be ready as they said it would but when I arrived the cashier told me it would take another 2 days. I am so disappointed, the cashiers are so rude too. When I asked if they had any giftcards they didn't even wait for me to finish my sentence and cut me off saying no. Please do something about this horrible staff

10 June 2026 - Extremely rude Muslim lady cashier. None of the item prices was listed on the shelf, and when I realised something was way more expensive than I thought, I asked to have it removed. When I was about to go put it back, she scolded me rudely about not checking the prices with her. Who would have thought that a bag of potato chips would cost 11 dollars? Am I supposed to ask the price of each and every single item at the cashier before buying it?

Whoever is in charge of this store, please teach your staff to be respectful and have actual manners instead of being uncouth. I understand I should have double-checked the price, but I don't see the reason to be scolded for it, even after apologising. She could only keep quiet and look smug after I had had enough and told her to learn some manners first.

If u see something fall in the store even it was happened near to you, think twice before telling the staff cuz u may end up being blamed for it 🙂 Want checking the CCTV to clarify what actually happened was impossible. The apology was only verbal, but payment was still required.

It was only one soya milk so no one will try to avoid responsibility okay? I only wanted to know whether the item actually fell because of me. Yet from the store staff to the follow-up customer service, no one was willing to provide the CCTV footage or seriously help clarify the facts.

Honestly, who cares about a 1 or 2 dollar refund? That was never the issue. I only wanted to understand what actually happened but apparently even that was too ambitious. The attitude was, of course, very sincere 🙂 verbally. The actions were arrogant, the customer service was nowhere to be found, and the whole experience was a wonderful reminder that some big stores are very good at making customers feel very small haha excellent

3 February 2026 at approximately 11:24 PM.
While I was preparing my chicken breast, I had not yet opened the packaging, which accidentally caused a loud sound. The Indian female staff on duty reacted by scolding me loudly in an extremely rude and disrespectful manner.

27/2 at around 0630hrs, I wanted to pay for my food using cash however the Indian lady staff refuses cash payment and only allow paywave. When questioned about why, rudely responded by saying she is closing counter so not accepting cash. The other counter also not opened.

Amazing staff, I wish all 7elevens had such a great team. Always welcoming, very friendly, very professional! Do NOT listen to bad reviews, I literally go there everyday and all the ladies are really amazing. Definitely one of the friendliest 7eleven team I have met so far!

place_name: ActiveSG Hockey Village @ Boon Lay Gym
review_text: Very spacious and not as crowded as other active SG gyms

place_name: ActiveSG Hockey Village @ Boon Lay Gym
review_text:
Definitely one of the really good ActiveSG gyms, especially on the Western side. While you gotta follow the usual SportsSG rules, the staff are usually happy to show you around and clarify workouts. Was surprised to see them this attentive.
Can be a bit crowded during peak timings but well equipped with facilities and spacious. Would definitely recommend over pricier options.

place_name: ActiveSG Hockey Village @ Boon Lay Gym
review_text:
Just in Singapore for a few weeks and was looking for a gym where I could pay per entry. This was perfect. $3.5SGD per entry.
Better than me committing to buying a month pass at other franchises, as I wasn’t sure how much time I would have available for the gym.

Ultimately only got to the gym 3 times in 3 weeks.
Facilities have all the basics, so don’t be expecting a bougee gym, but definitely still has plenty of free weights, equipment, pylo boxes, treadmills, squat bars etc (worth noting no deadlifts allowed in this gym).
Staff were friendly too 👏🏻

Note: you MUST have a sweat towel for entry. They don’t sell any, but the market next door sells plenty for $1 if you don’t have one

place_name: ActiveSG Hockey Village @ Boon Lay Gym
review_text: Nice gym, used to come often when visiting the shopping center. but now it has very strong chemical smell after renovation. It has been a few months, but inside the smell is still strong and i cannot bear for more than 20 mins. Not sure whether the smell is harmful to health.

place_name: ActiveSG Hockey Village @ Boon Lay Gym
review_text:
Although ActiveSG shows the gym capacity as only 35% full, there were actually many people in the gym. So I wonder how did the system compute the capacity. It’s rather misleading.

Anyway, I would avoid peak hours from 530pm to 8pm. Especially every Singaporean has $100 ActiveSG vouchers to spend. So it makes the gym more crowded now.

My wife recently went to this outlet.

Great space and equipment.

However the music is blasting and toilets are not cleaned well.

This has to improve. Dear AF team, do take note please
Been to a couple of their outlets and this is by far the biggest with ample equipments available. Everyone here is friendly and courteous. Highly recommend!

Spacious gym. Could do with more 5, 10 and 12.5kg dumbbells and sanitising sprays & cloths to wipe equipments and benches after using. I noticed hardly any members wipe down after using. Also noticed most lockers were locked while the gym was not full and members at that time kept their bags at the alternative wooden shelves during my visit (Sep 24, around 2pm).

Checked out Boon Lay Anytime Fitness for the first time and I’ve got to say — I’m really impressed! The gym has pretty much everything you need. Loads of machines, a wide range of equipment, and the free weights go all the way up to 50kg. The best part? They actually have two pairs of each dumbbell, so no need to fight over weights with anyone. Plenty of benches too, so you’re not stuck waiting around.

Big shoutout to Wandi at the front desk — super friendly and helpful! He made me feel really welcome on my first visit. Give that guy a pay raise!

The gym is clean and well-maintained, with four toilets/showers so it’s easy to freshen up after your workout. There’s even a vending machine with protein shakes, which is a lifesaver if you forget to bring yours.

All in all, awesome vibe, solid equipment, and great service. Definitely worth a visit if you’re in the Boon Lay area!

Spacious Neighbourhood 24hr gym. Gym manager very friendly and always smile. During weekdays after office hours can be quite busy, however crowd get lesser and lesser after 1030pm. Facilities well maintained. Enough equipments to execute most workouts.
Great place to play hockey games and futsal. Usually only guys here. Hope more girls can be attracted to join the sports as not much support from ActiveSG.
The futsal pitch is well maintained and the shower facilities are great

The futsal pitch is smaller and narrower than the others in SG.

The netting is really a big issue, you can't really run near them as you can easily trip over it, ie, getting your foot stuck in it, hence making the playing area much smaller than it is.

And you have to stop about 10mins before your actual time due to the closing timing. So you won't really get to play fully also.

The only good thing about this pitch  is that it is much cheaper.

Staff was not friendly and even aggressive. Specifically that one malay uncle who was pissed off after we left the futsal pitch a few minutes late, will not book the pitch again as the pitch itself is not worth the trouble and the clock is broken as well

Active SG. Two hours to kill whilst son attends a JSA close by. Senior rate @$1.50 plus 65c for motorcycle parking. Loads of gym eqpt to work everything and send you to lah lah land once you have reached back home and showered.

The kids playground is close until further notice. No info online. If u intend to go cos of playground better find ways to check with pool first.
No wonder playground look so quiet. I peep in to confirm there is playground before buying tickets. But in the end can't use playground.

Note! It's Feature pool price. I buy under normal cannot scan. Counter say it's feature pool.

I'm a regular at the Jurong East swimming complex but the facilities are either not opened or old so I decided to take two more stops to Pioneer MRT Station where Jurong West swimming complex is.

Just 5 to 10 minutes walk from the train station, Jurong West Swimming Complex share the same building with alot of childcare, enrichment Centre, basically kids friendly place and also a stadium.

It has 2 swimming pool, one of the them is about 0.9 m, suitable for kids who don't know how to swim. The play area is not too bad, 2 slides but not for adults and anyone above 1.2 m. Too bad the bigger slide and lazy pool is not opened. Oh yes, there is a jacuzzi by the side for adults.

Overall, the facilities are very well maintained.

The staff are very nice and extremely helpful in our situation. We had wanted to go swimming in Singapore and remembered that a few years ago, all you had to do was tap your bus card to enter.

However, things have changed- now, in order to enter at all, you have to register for an ActiveSG account. Unfortunately for non- Singapore residents, this process is nearly impossible without a Singaporean phone number, and then also a Singaporean credit card.

Thankfully, we had helpful desk staff figure out a way to sneak through the problem, and one staff offered to use his credit card in exchange for payment in cash. We still needed to provide the postal code for our flat, but eventually, after almost half an hour of trying, we managed to get through without registering for an account.

Then, when we got inside, we realised that the entire waterslide and lazy river had been closed because of no one doing regular maintenance during Covid!

Not only that, but even though we paid for adult passes inside, the kids were not allowed into the jacuzzi because they said only adults. And the kids were not allowed on the kid's playground either because they had to be very short in order to enter.

All that said, we enjoyed swimming in the pool even though we could have went to the pool next to our flat, but we thought this would be more fun with a waterslide.

We are very dissatisfied with the system, but the only reason for 2 stars is the staff were helpful, otherwise we would have had to go home completely.
Cheap entry, normal ticket, not feature pool ticket. The big slide is quite long and the kids love it. There is also a lazy river.

Inexpensive place to spend time with family, friends or even by oneself. Big enough place with more than 4 pools move around including a huge slide. Children can play in the water playground with slides for children under 1.2m, so not much quarrel there. Olympic size indoor pool for more serious swimmers. A lazy pool just to laze around, tubes for rental available at counter.

Free entry for members. Free for my 2yr old but need to pay $1.75 for my 7 yr old. There 4 swim areas. 3 for kids and 1 lap pool. Very spacious, change and bathing areas are conducive. Not very very fun but good enough for 1-2hrs of play time for kids.

There’s many eateries here by the way! Jacks place, indo food & Prata!

The pool is extremely suitable for bringing family with children. Although I do not like the curve end of one of the pool. It makes rest and standing there hard. Although the jacuzzi area is quite good where you can get massage at multiple places.
Last visited this pool about >5years ago. Today my first swim since Nov 2019 return back to SG. Pool appears no change, except less crowd due to pandemic.

Spacious swimming pools, for adults and for kids. The rain forest pool and frog pool are great for kids under 5. With water guns, fountains, controllable pouring rain and a nice depth of 0.8m, it's good fun for the kids. The nice touch is that the frog pool is heated so no fears of morning cold swims! Also, the showers are heated too! Would come again!

One of the worst place to swim in Singapore, The guard can turn into weather forecast , and can easily order you to get out of the pool , the worst thing is that is not even raining at all , will never ever come to visit this disgusting place ever again!!!

Golden Village is a reliable cinema option in the west, located conveniently within Jurong Point near Boon Lay MRT. The cineplex has multiple screening halls with comfortable seating and good screen and sound quality, making it suitable for regular movie outings.

Ticketing is efficient with self-service kiosks and automated entry gates, which helps reduce waiting time during peak hours. The cinema typically screens a wide range of movies, from mainstream releases to popular blockbusters.

The vibe is casual and family-friendly, though it can get bustling during weekends and evening shows. Being inside Jurong Point also means easy access to food outlets and shops before or after your movie.

A practical choice if you’re looking for a straightforward and comfortable cinema experience in the Jurong area. 🎬🍿

Watched hoppers last night, last showtime of the day. Ordered a mix of sweet and salted popcorn, but some of the popcorn tasted weird, like antiseptic. It did have a numbing sensation. And it was mostly from the left side of my popcorn box (not sure if this even matters). Thankfully we already read the other review before we visited, so we stopped ourselves from consuming the rest of the popcorn 💔💔 Never had such issues with other GV outlets… please QC!!

24 Nov 2025, Went for Jurong GV, 11am Wicked for Good. Delay for 39 mins, picture of not align, seeing blur movie, later got picture no music, later got movie no picture. Could not finish movie because of delay. What GV is trying to do? Look more like a scam... Need to report to CASE for such issue. It so irresponsible. Spoil the whole program for the family days. Some audience left.

At 12pm, the staff ask the rest of the audience whether they would like to move to another theater to see the movie properly.

Haven’t been to the cinema in forever and finally went to catch Zootopia 2 but the seats smells here (Theatre 4). Wonder if it’s because not much people watch movies these days so the hygiene level dropped?

3 stars cause seat is pretty comfy and there’s Ijooz now which you can bring into the theatre! It’s priced $1 higher than other machine tho. GV toilets v big and clean.

It was very disappointing this time. I have come to golden village a lot of times before but this has not happened before. There was this staff who shouted at me when I was buying the popcorn. We said sweet for the popcorn but ended up scooping salty. Hence we had to stop her and tell her it was sweet. However she had already done it so we had to get mixed or salty. And she shouted at us whether sweet or salty in a very disrespectful manner. In the end we had to get mixed. It was a very a disappointing experience.
There is only an eatery and toilets on the ground floor. Nothing much otherwise.
Poor attitude and inexperience drink sellers. I was confused with my order and asked the seller. But she was impatient and snobbish when replying.
This place at night would be nice for a coffee or tea, but in the morning whereby u will see a lady in the selling drinks stall which the CB face so black like u owe her money.. lol knn
The price is cheap for 6 persons. Each of us only spent 10 sgd. Each plate is big, and it is only cost 2 to 3 sgd.
Good place for outdoor beer
It fun
Playground

A pleasant neighbourhood park that’s generally well maintained and easy to enjoy. The greenery is neatly kept, paths are clean, and there’s a good mix of features like open lawns, walking paths, small water features, and basic fitness and play areas. It’s a comfortable space for a casual walk, light exercise, or a short break outdoors

The park is relatively small compared to some of Singapore’s larger parks, so it doesn’t take very long to explore. That said, its compact size also makes it convenient and accessible, especially if you live or work nearby and just want a quick escape into nature without travelling far

Jurong Central Park · Singapore ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

Visited in the morning and found it fairly quiet, which made for a pleasant short stroll. The park isn’t particularly spectacular, but it’s a nice spot for light walks and a quick escape into greenery. With the extension currently closed off, the full loop is relatively short at just under 1 km.

What makes the park distinctive are its playful, game-inspired features like the life-sized Snakes and Ladders playground and Ludo garden, alongside ponds and shaded paths. While not a must-visit, it’s a creative and family-friendly community park that adds variety to the West’s green spaces.

Weekend Wanders: Jurong Central Park
Took a stroll through Jurong Central Park over the weekend — a peaceful green escape nestled just minutes away from Jurong Point and Boon Lay MRT.

Though it’s not as expansive as Jurong Lake Gardens, the park offers a refreshing and lush environment that’s perfect for unwinding after a hearty lunch or shopping spree at the mall. With its open lawns, shaded walking paths, and the gentle rustling of trees, it’s a wonderful spot to just pause and breathe.

If you’re around Jurong and need a short nature break, this park is definitely worth a visit. Sometimes, it’s the smaller green spaces that offer the biggest reset.

The park is steeped in history and local knowledge about plants if you know who to look for. Overall also easily 3 degree Celsius cooler.

I spotted a local water hen having a blast and thought it's cute

Jurong Central Park is a well-maintained, family-friendly park in Singapore, featuring creative play areas for children and ample space for walking, running, and jogging.

Some visitors have noted its location in the western part of the island, which may be less convenient for travelers staying in other areas.

Overall, the park is appreciated for its clean environment and suitability for various outdoor activities.
Nice big playground for kids to play and adults and senior to exercise. All in one, cycling track, rooftop yoga space, basketball and football space. Park at open carpark near block 558

High speed Bicycle riding activities by yong children around the play ground are dangerous to the elderly people . There are two sign boards barring bicycle riding . But youngsters don't notice it. Don't care it.
Be careful if you are walking around the playground night time. We feel it is unsafe play ground for elderly people. It should be reviewed .

Visited this park for my group evening gathering as it has a big  viewing deck about 3 storeys height while the surrounding HDB flats are not very high...
But on the top of the viewing deck, there are many spots with broken and damaged wooden planks...which can be unsafe during night time...
Hope the relevant authorities can fix up all these damaged and broken wooden planks..
Lots of fun activities for kids, fitness stations for adults, basket ball court and roof level football area for kids.
Good place for kids for climbing and navigating their way up to the slide. Yay for strong arms and legs

🫛🍅Boon Lay Nature Garden is the result of the Edible Garden initiative started by the Foodscape Collective, with support from NParks.
🌽🥬The Collective aims to transform under-utilized green spaces in parks to community gardens.
🌿It was first named as Biodiverse Edible Garden at Jurong Central Park before the resident regular community adopted its present name.
🍂🪹The Boon Lay Nature Garden makes its own compost, with help from the Collective.
A wonderful place for plants lover to learn and work on gardenings👩🏻‍🌾🥰🥳✨
Lovely regenerative garden

It is well maintained. It is designed to be a place where everyone can relax for a while. There is a playground for the little ones, a gym for the adults, a walking ground. A park with a lily pond for those who love nature. An open field for entertainment. McDonald's for food. Drinking water facility, toilet facility, car parking. A separate place for parking bicycles. Nearby is the Poonle railway station. The place is beautifully located as a mall.

As a nearby resident, I visit Jurong Lake Gardens quite frequently. The walking tracks are always well-maintained, and I enjoy the scenic lake views. There are also plenty of shaded paths, which make for a very relaxing walk.

A pleasant but fairly small section of Jurong Lake Gardens. The landscaping is neat and well maintained, and there are some nice plants and boardwalk paths to stroll through, but it doesn’t take very long to cover the entire area

It’s fine to visit if you’re already nearby or doing a full loop of the gardens, but probably not worth going out of your way just for this section alone

The newly renovated Jurong Park @Chinese Garden @Japanese Garden is amazing. Many interesting plants, ponds, and fixtures are unique to only this park. Many themes are nicely decorated.

Food stores are scattered everywhere while facilities like toilets and water dispensers are generously available.

It's a good park for exploration, evening walks, and slow biking.

A huge garden! Took about 2 hours to slowly stroll, enjoy the view and reach the other end of the garden. It’s home to many animals and there’s so much to see. My favorite starts from the Japanese Garden onwards. Really enjoy the peaceful environment there and nice place to totally unwind. It’s a short walking distance from the MRT station.
nice and peaceful place. great for walks, jogs and runs. some do cycle around.

It's convenient where it is right by the bus and train stop. It ensures security with the keycard systems, and has a very cool garden themed pool area. The fact it has it's own pick up and drop off area is good, with a nice tennis court. The place has decent views.

Some improvements needed for the turnstile gate as the face ID can sometimes detect faces from afar, causing a bit of a hassle to get through the gate sometimes. Staying here isn't cheap either.

Very near MRT, very good place to go to, it is just that there are way too many mosquitoes (at the side facing the canal) and even monkeys went to the Balcony! Overall experience was Good, and even some inca doves went to nest in the balcony!

I wish to lodge a complaint against one of your Lakefront Residences security guards, Ainah.

I am a frequent visitor to this condominium and on 1 September at 1725, I did not register my visit with the guard house as I was in a hurry and a resident was just about to exit the premises. I waited at the side gate to enter when your security guard Ainah, started shouting at me from across the road in an extremely rude and condescending manner.

"Sir, you've been here many times before right? You know you need to register right? You not resident, you think free free can enter ah?"

After I had provided my details, she looked towards the side gate scornfully as if to say: "Get lost and now you may enter."

For the record, this was the first time that I did not register my visit and while I do not dispute the need to register, the manner and tone in which your staff reacted was utterly appalling, unnecessary and unwarranted.

As I was in a hurry, I did not want to waste any more time with her so I decided to let the matter rest then. After my visit, I did go back to the guardhouse and fortunately for her, she was no longer there otherwise I certainly would have given her a piece of my mind as I think her abhorrent behaviour has no place in our society.

I would like to believe that she is but one of the few black sheep in your organisation, and as a service organisation, I think you would agree with me that good service is of utmost importance. I therefore hope your organisation will take the appropriate action against this errant security staff so that the residents and visitors to Lakefront Residences will no longer have to put up with staff like her.
We love it here unit is a bit small but then again most units in Singapore are
mic check started from 9 am, playing super noisy music from 9:20. Strongly suggest people who need no sleep live here hahaha!
Quiet and simple straight path

My daughter attended JCK Kindergarten, and it has been a wonderful journey for both her and our family. The teachers are responsible, passionate, and truly dedicated to the children’s growth. After moving house, my second daughter had to attend a different childcare center, and that’s when I realized how special JCK is — not only in terms of education but also in providing a healthy environment. At JCK, my daughter fell sick much less often. I would highly recommend JCK Kindergarten to any families living around the area!

We are incredibly grateful for the experience we’ve had at JCK. The teachers are truly passionate and committed to the well-being and development of the children. We have seen our child grow remarkably, both academically and personally.

The curriculum is thoughtfully designed, creating a wonderful learning environment that supports each child’s growth.

Thank you to the entire team of teachers and staff for your dedication and hard work. We couldn’t be happier with the progress our child has made!

JCK was one of the best pre-primary school I got in singapore for my son. The efforts taken by all teachers for kids growth n development are really priceless. Concept of plus program really helpes kids to develope their imagination and thinking level as well. Teacher Mavis which are class teacher of my son are really a good,  kind hearted,  hardworking teacher. The efforts taken by Teacher Mavis to make kids more focused,  obedient n organised are really appreciating.   Thank you so much teacher.
I will definitely recommend JCK school.

Thank you JCK for nurturing my son to where he is today. We are so glad of our decision to let him study here.

We have only enrolled our son to JCK K1 and were thankful for the accommodation and understanding that the teachers gave to allow him a place in K1 Blue. We were keen to let him study in JCK after hearing reviews from his cousins’ mom and once we did, the rest were history.

Indeed, we have seen the transformation of my son and are happy to see him going to school excitedly and happily every morning. We believe his teachers (Ms Anna, 刘老师 and Mrs Mari) must have done wonders and are able to engage the kids such that my son enjoys his school time so much. Apart from academic, the school have other activities like music, gym, art craft lessons. We are impressed by the group art work (be it using reycled items or other themes) done by the class and this definitely allows my son to learn about teamwork and respecting other classmates’ opinions.

This year, we have decided to enrol him to K2 S-T-E-A-M programme and once again the school has accommodated to our request and this allows him to gain more friendships which he truly treasures and enjoys. We are grateful and thankful that Teacher Marvis has helped him transit smoothly and also triggered his curiosity for Science which he will share with us eagerly. We are appreciative of the learning videos that were sent too.

Thank you JCK for giving my son a memorable fun Kindergarten experience and nurturing him. Also, a gratitude to all JCK teachers for the effort and preparation to make the students happy during each celebratory occasions!

From parents of a K2 graduate

Teachers are all very caring and nurturing. Both academic and play focus. Especially the plus program, it added more fun to the whole learning process. Also, JCK has adequately prepare the kids for their P1 too 👍🏻

Sharing my experience as a first time parent, enrolled my child at BrilliantTots Jurong West infantcare a month ago.

Introduction and registration was thorough and principal Ms Dil is clear and help to clear any doubts. As a first time parent, Ms Dil also shared many pointers and advice which I appreciate.

The center is neat and clean, infantcare area is kept tidy and neat and also have colourful pictures decoration which is attractive for the infants.

Teacher Shikin, Chen Laoshi are both very warm and passionate. My child’s face lights up whenever the teachers greet him upon arrival. Teachers also provide short update during pickup (eg what did my child enjoy or how his slept/feeding went). The teachers take great care of the babies. There are also activity even for infant (eg hand/feet painting, reading picture book) and a short summary of it for each child. The summaries report is thoughtfully written by teachers and shows the attention provided to each child.

My child enjoys infantcare as he is full of smiles and eager to look inside the school.

Definitely go for a school tour and highly recommend this for your child if you are staying in the Jurong West area.

Thank you BrilliantTots Jurong West!

We enrolled our son at this wonderful centre in May 2024, and it has been an incredibly positive experience. The teachers are dedicated and nurturing, and the support staff—including the cleaner and cook—are equally warm, passionate, and caring. We are truly grateful 🫶🏻for the love and attention they show every day. 🤗 thank you

Brilliant TOTS Childcare truly lives up to its name. The staff are incredibly dedicated, nurturing, and passionate about creating a safe and stimulating environment for young children. Every day is filled with meaningful learning, joyful play, and thoughtful guidance that helps each child grow in confidence and curiosity.

The center is well-organized, clean, and welcoming—an ideal space for little ones to explore and thrive. Teachers consistently show genuine care and attention, making every child feel seen and valued. The curriculum strikes a beautiful balance between academic readiness and social-emotional development, laying a strong foundation for lifelong learning.

Parents can feel at ease knowing their children are in loving hands. Brilliant TOTS is more than just a childcare center—it’s a community that supports families and celebrates every milestone. Highly recommended for anyone seeking quality early education in a warm, professional setting!

The teachers at Brilliant Tots Jurong West Centre are very dedicated and loving to our kids. I have placed my son at the centre since infant care and now he is in N2. He always share stories about his teachers and friends on a daily basis and i am sure that he enjoys his time at the centre. Thank you for taking care of my son. He has definitely improved his speech and motor skills over the period.

Throughout the time that we are here, we have build good rapport with both teachers and other parents and i believe it is due to the events conducted within the centre e.g. Parent’s Day Celebration, Sports Day. Good effort and keep it!

Brilliant Tots at Jurong is an outstanding preschool. The teachers are incredibly kind and dedicated, creating a nurturing and engaging environment where the kids feel happy and safe. My child looks forward to school every day, and my family has been so impressed with the positive growth we've seen. We highly recommend this preschool for its wonderful staff and excellent care!!

Both my kids are literally "brought up" by the dedicated teachers here in Joy Talents. While my son has graduated into P3 now, he still misses Joy Talents alot and always reminiscing about his time with his teachers here.
My daughter is still enrolled in this centre eventhough we have shifted to Woodlands as the teachers and principle are really lovely, with a very humble boss who will always takes her time to chat w us.

As new parents, we found ourselves overwhelmed with worries and anxieties about childcare. Discovering Joy Talent was a blessing we didn't expect. The moment we visited and saw the genuine smiles on the children's faces, we knew we had found the right place for our family.

We are deeply grateful to Joy Talent for creating such a safe and nurturing environment for our kids. Miss Agnes deserves special recognition for her exceptional patience and dedication. She took the time to walk us through everything—from daycare expectations to what we should bring and what to expect in those crucial first weeks. Her guidance gave us the confidence and peace of mind we needed to entrust our child to their care.

The entire teaching staff has shown incredible kindness and warmth, making both our children and our family feel welcomed. We feel truly blessed to have Joy Talent right here in our neighborhood, providing not just childcare, but a genuine community of care for our little ones. Keep it up!

Awesome childcare centre! My kid just joined PG and I didn’t expect the transition to be so smooth, Teacher Angelica and the other teachers are so patient, dedicated, and attentive! The centre owner is always there as well to make sure the new kids are adapting well. My kid loves the teachers and really enjoys school while he’s there. Thank you so much!

my son was placed in infant care there , its very clean , very tidy , and the teachers were very passionate and kind . I definitely wish i could place him there longer but hence i moved to another place i manage to find a nearer infant care for my son , but i definately love joy talent so much , principal all recommended , thnku joy talent for taking care of my son from (2 months-10 months )

In just six months, we have witnessed our son's healthy and robust development in terms of physical and mental well-being, as well as emotional intelligence, under the meticulous guidance and care of Ms. Yang and the other teachers. As a primary school Chinese teacher with nearly thirteen years of experience, I deeply appreciate this. I express my sincerest gratitude to the teachers for their selfless dedication. Thank you, teachers!
Thanks for going above and beyond this year. I know it's been challenging, but I really appreciate all of you hard work to engage Wan Yen. She is really going to miss you all!
My child’s Chinese and English spelling improved and their staff is friendly and helpful. Highly recommended.
Attended ACJC's traditional biennial funfair. Such an exciting event! Kudos to all the students and teachers who made the event so grand.

Not only does this school love sucking out money from its students to allegedly maintain facilities that 1. students NEVER get to use and 2. NEVER become cleaner or better in condition, they also enjoy developing bladder diseases in their poor students.

Teachers, especially the discipline master, ridicule and punish, sometimes even forbid, students from simply using the toilet. A 5 minute trip to the toilet to empty one’s bladder from holding it in for hours is seen as an act of sin, a crime being committed by this school that praises itself for being so loving with the grace of God.

During chapel, assembly, or school events where students are gathered in the hall, using to the toilet is seen as a threat, a crime. Students will either be held captive in another room where they will not even be able to see what is going on in the main hall to appreciate the event, will be told to take the route that is much longer to come back to the hall (which makes absolutely no sense as one would have to walk past much more people if their reason is disturbing the course of event), or simply, told no and scolded for “not being able to hold it in”.

Teachers say to students in a humiliating, condescending tone that if they cannot hold their bladder for more than a few hours, they should “get it checked”. This is either implying that students should not stay hydrated or using the toilet is undesirable, when it is part and parcel of how the normal human body works. Over time, with students not being able to use the restroom when they urgently need it, kidney and bladder diseases may be manifested. Urinary Tract Infections are amongst one of the many diseases students would have a much higher chance of having after holding in their urine for extended periods of time just to adhere to some school expectations. Not only that, it is simply extremely uncomfortable sitting still with a full bladder.

I sincerely hope the school will be more considerate of students’ health and needs and be more understanding.

I went to ACJC this morning at 11:30am as I wanted to purchase a swimming paddle for my child. At the entrance, the security guard Mr. Liao asked me to register and requested for my IC. I simply asked him whether it was necessary to provide my IC, but instead of explaining politely, he responded in a rude manner and told me not to “talk too much,” threatening not to let me in and even challenging me to complain to the authorities.

I believe such an attitude is not appropriate, especially within a school environment that should set an example for students in communication and respect. If students were to overhear such an exchange, what impression would they be left with?

I sincerely hope the school can look into this matter and ensure better communication and professionalism from the staff. Your improvement will be very much appreciated.

Wonderful Junior College providing a holistic education. Tremendously benefitted from my experience here at ACJC in 2003-2004, with lifelong friendships and bonds still going strong 20+ years later. Blessed to be part of the ACSian family, with teachers and even our former Principal being ever supportive of our individual endeavours. The sports facilities were fantastic too from the track to the swimming pool and gym, I credit a lot of my sporting journey from this too. Thank you, ACJC! 🙏🏻

The location is strategic, as it sits between 2 mrt stations, and surrounded by many eateries and shopping malls. The sport complex is big, there's football field, running track, swimming pools, as well as badminton court.

place_name: Five Star Tuition Centre Pte Ltd - Jurong
review_text:
My child was admitted to RI after PSLE 2025, and I would like to thank FIVE STAR TUITION CENTRE for the support along the way.

My child attended Primary 6 Science and Math tuition here. We are especially thankful to Mdm Mani, who is very dedicated and responsible. She puts in a lot of effort to make sure the students understand the concepts and get enough practice.

This centre is especially suitable for children who need more practice. My child doesn’t really like doing homework at home, so most of the PSLE practice was done at the centre, which helped a lot.

Of course, every child is different. What worked for us may not work for everyone. It’s most important to find a tuition centre that suits your own child.

place_name: Five Star Tuition Centre Pte Ltd - Jurong
review_text: Ms Mani is literally the best!!! She is kind, passionate, friendly, knowledgeable, funny, and approachable among many other things, not forgetting the fact that she teaches very well. She pays close attention to each of her students progress and really goes the extra mile to mould the students and bring out the best in them. When teaching, she explains everything needed in an elaborate and detailed manner. Before proceeding to new concepts, she ensures that each and every student is clear about what’s been taught so far. Ms Mani’s tuition pushed my grades up tremendously and it helped me do well in PsLE!! I highly recommend this tuition !!!! Don’t hesitate, sign up before it’s too late !!!!!!

place_name: Five Star Tuition Centre Pte Ltd - Jurong
review_text:
We are deeply grateful to Mdm Mani, Mr Joseph and Mr Yeo for their incredible dedication, passion and patience in guiding Jie Yu through P5 and P6. 🌟 Over these two years, the FIVE STAR TUITION CENTRE team has truly gone above and beyond — organising numerous free PSLE Science, Math and English workshops even on public holidays like Good Friday and Labour Day, as well as during March, June and September holidays. It really shows how much heart and commitment they put into every student’s learning journey.

We’ve seen a huge transformation in Jie Yu — he has grown more confident, disciplined and self-motivated, and developed a genuine interest in his subjects. The teachers’ consistent encouragement, detailed feedback and tireless effort made a big difference in his PSLE preparation.

Thank you once again to Mdm Mani and the entire team for your warmth, energy and care towards the students. FIVE STAR TUITION CENTRE truly lives up to its name — not just in results, but in nurturing character and confidence. Highly recommended to all parents looking for teachers who genuinely care! 🙏✨

place_name: Five Star Tuition Centre Pte Ltd - Jurong
review_text:
My child joined this tuition centre in Primary 5 and we are very grateful for the guidance and support provided throughout her learning journey. The teachers are dedicated, patient, and genuinely care about each student’s progress.

What really stood out was the extra effort given during exam periods—additional workshops, extra materials, and revision support were provided generously, often free of charge. The lessons were structured, clear, and effective, helping my child gain confidence and a better understanding of the subjects taught.

The teachers are approachable and responsive, and they consistently go the extra mile to ensure students are well prepared, not just academically but also emotionally. We have seen steady improvement and a more positive attitude towards learning.

Highly recommended to parents who are looking for committed, caring, and results-driven teachers. Thank you for all the hard work and dedication!

place_name: Five Star Tuition Centre Pte Ltd - Jurong
review_text:
Five Star Tuition Centre truly lives up to its name! Mdm. Mani's teaching methods are outstanding, and she has helped my kids to excel academically.

Mdm. Mani is an extraordinarily dedicated teacher who goes above and beyond for her students. Her lessons are comprehensive, covering a wide range of skills from oral communication to critical thinking in English comprehension. What truly sets her apart is her passion for guiding students - she conducts extra special workshops at no additional charge, demonstrating her genuine commitment to each child's success.

The teaching materials at Five Star Tuition Centre are simply outstanding! Mdm. Mani provides well-organised notes and practice exercises that perfectly complement what my kids learn in school. The materials are updated regularly and always relevant to current syllabus requirements. Thank you Mdm.Mani for your effort and care on students :)

place_name: EduFirst Learning Centre (Jurong West Branch) - English Math Chinese Science Tuition Centre Student Care
review_text: We are very happy with Edufirst Learning Centre at Jurong West! The teachers are patient, caring and knowledgeable. They make lessons engaging and always ensure students understand the concepts clearly. My child’s confidence has grown so much, and we’ve seen a big improvement in results. The centre also provides regular feedback, which helps us stay updated on progress. Highly recommended for parents who want a supportive and effective learning environment!

place_name: EduFirst Learning Centre (Jurong West Branch) - English Math Chinese Science Tuition Centre Student Care
review_text: We are glad that we signed up with EduFirst Learning Centre Jurong West for my daughter's 'O' Level. The teachers are patience, ensuring she understood the concepts before moving to next topics. This resulted in exceptional results in her Pure Science, AMaths and EMaths. Highly recommended.

place_name: EduFirst Learning Centre (Jurong West Branch) - English Math Chinese Science Tuition Centre Student Care
review_text: This tuition centre provides a very supportive and effective learning environment. The teachers are not only knowledgeable but also patient in explaining concepts until students are confident. The lessons are well-structured, with a balance of practice and guidance, which helps students strengthen their foundation and prepare well for exams. The centre also encourages good study habits and motivates students to stay consistent. A great choice for anyone looking for quality tuition.

place_name: EduFirst Learning Centre (Jurong West Branch) - English Math Chinese Science Tuition Centre Student Care
review_text: The tutors here are wonderfully patient and supportive tutors who provide highly personalized lessons tailored to each student's specific needs. This custom approach is incredibly effective, ensuring concepts are not only understood but mastered !

place_name: EduFirst Learning Centre (Jurong West Branch) - English Math Chinese Science Tuition Centre Student Care
review_text: It's one of the best tuition centres u have gone to the learning was so detailed and easily understandable. The teachers were kind and caring as they know when we are tired and they would give us a break. I really recommend this tuition centre as I have improved in my English and Chinese.
It is going to be very convenient for the people living nearby.
Very crowded but plenty of on the go Food carts available
In Google maps its showing Js5,basically its JS6 Jurong West JRL Stn
It’s an good mrt station
This station is js6. Not js5
Construction is very fast, I hope to see how it will play out once it opens

Hope a mall can be planned and built around this station. There’re many residence blocks in this area but limited shops in the immediate vicinity. Closest malls are Jurong Point and Gek Poh Shopping Center but both are 2 bus stops away and crowed during weekends. It’s good to have another mall in the middle for the convenience of residents nearby, and there are enough residents and demand here. With the plan of a new MRT interchange, it’s also going to be a perfect location for business. Hope it will be considered by the developers for this area.
Under construction, to be operational by 2027. Provides essential connectivity for nearby primary and high schools, the fire station and the temple.
Ongoing construction MRT station and Jurong Innovation District will definitely bring economic impacts to this area.
4/5. looks good but train waiting times are a bit long
Snoopy waits with u at this bus stop. Comforting
You can use Chinese Garden MRT to reach here. This place is so relaxing and very comfartable to go walking, jogging and even just sitting at the bench.
During the week the gardens are very quiet.  The weekends it becomes much busier.  Weekends are great for extroverted folks week days are great for introverts.
Chinese Garden was beautiful.  A lovely location for a picnic & evening stroll
It’s good place to take snap photo.
Just across the japanese garden. Everything in this place is amazing! You can also enter the Live Turtle Tortoise museum. Oh, and the roaming lizard. :-)

Did not expect that this hawker center is so clean! We are really  impressed by their cleanliness !
We ordered Malay food, Nasi Padang. Very delicious! If we were to order these much ( see pictures) of ingredients in the food court or kopitiam, it would cost us at least $15 to $18. But here , it cost us only $12! We guess this is because it is located in the industrial area, rental may not be so high. And also to serve the blue collar workers. Kopi and Teh are nice! They were prepared by a very old uncle. Taste and flavours of the baverages remind us of the coffee and tea of the olden days Singapore! Very nostalgic!
Definitely will come back here again!

13/November/2025
Had mixed rice for brunch ($4.60), was normal. Lots of stalls to choose from, I'll be back. Limited parking lots available.
The Hong Kong roast duck is very nice.

Leong Kee Chicken Rice is the best chicken rice I have ever eaten, white chicken is the must eat but sell out very fast. The rice is very flavorful & the chilli one of the best, protion size of chicken is also generous.

Rare store that quality & quantity maintains very high

Update 30/07/25: their chilli quality has gone down, it is watery and lack the spicy kick

Halal Food option available at this hawker, try INDAH SAKURA shop.  They have good food. I came during lunch time on Thursday.  There were queue (~5 people in front of me) but very fast.  I had their sambal sotong (squid), sambal goreng (tofu, long bean), sambal kentang (potato) and chili sauce.  All taste good.  The man & lady attending the stall also nice people.  Online payment available, but pls prepare cash just in case.  Overall, recommended 😊

We were nearby after our visit to Lake Garden, so we came here for breakfast. The place is easy to find, with a large parking area surrounding the food court. It’s located on the second floor, while the wet market is on the ground floor.

The food court is neat and clean, and although it was quite crowded, it didn’t feel too packed and the ventilation was decent. There is a wide variety of food stalls to choose from, and the prices are very reasonable. Surprisingly, my meal cost only $3!

Overall, it’s a nice neighbourhood food court with good food, affordable prices, and a pleasant dining experience. Would definitely come back again.

Been eating there since 2025.

My favourites are:

beef noodle soup (02–72),
western stall (03-171)
Cai fan with porridge (02-80)
Lei Cha (03-120)

More than half the stalls will close after 2pm.

Ample wall fans around. Careful where u sit cuz some tables are smokers’ area.

The cleaning staff are so hardworking and busy. Really appreciate them!

For Taman Jurong Market and Food Centre, its ground floor is a busy wet market, while the second and third levels are dedicated to food stalls. Taman Jurong Shopping Centre is right next to the market and food centre. In fact, they are separated only by a small road (Yung Sheng Road) and are less than a minute's walk apart.
Btw, my favorite eating stalls are 58 minced meat noodle, Ying Ji Wanton Mee, Xin Kee Pig’s Organ Soup and, coffee in most stalls there are nice too.

"A multi-story hawker paradise in the West! If you’re coming here, you HAVE to queue for Feng Zhen Lor Mee (#03-146)—the gravy is thick, flavorful, and those handmade meatballs are life-changing.

If you’re not in the mood for noodles, Tien Lai Rice Stall (#02-66) has some of the best Char Siew and Roast Duck in Jurong (go early, they sell out fast!). For something unique, try ButterNut (#02-108) for their fusion pizzas.

Pro-tip: The center is circular, so it can be easy to lose your bearings. Use the central courtyard as your landmark. Plenty of parking and very breezy on the upper floors!"

This hawker centre has two storeys. There are quite a number of good food choices. Place was quite clean too. Interestingly, there is a smoking area. This site apparently is the location of Singapore’s first hawker centre, and is part of the Jurong Heritage Trail.

Since 58 Minced Meat Noodle at Taman Jurong Food Centre was closed today (Wed), I headed across the street to 399 Food Court for my Bak Chor Mee (BCM). The food court is air-conditioned and offers a great variety of options.
​I tried the 58 BCM ($4.50), an egg prata ($1.70), and coffee ($1.30), while also dabao-ed the Pig’s Organ Soup with rice ($5.80) to go. Overall, the mentioned food was quite good, with the exception of the egg prata, which was rather so-so.

Foods serve here are local dishes. Chinese, Muslim and Indian food are available in this eatery house. The 58 Buk Chor Mee is my favorite stall, the mushroom broil is power. Clean and A/C eating place.

Aircon environment, could get crowded and noisy at times. Tried the minced meat noodle and find it too oily for my liking. $4 portion has good value and good service from the stall. They accept cash and paynow.

You can practically get everything you need here. Here I am giving a mentioned on the foodcourt. Most popular is Seri Muslim Food stall. Be prepared to que for your order. Great food is being offered here. The typical malay breakfast food. More reviews next time.

The roast duck & pork are excellent... crispy and tasty.  These have been constant for these 2 years.   However the team members change so service level may not be consistent.
Recently they have a $2.80 for a bowl of noodles.

Building 101.This food court jist next to Taman Jurong wet market  ..This foodcourt open 24 hours. Many types of oriental food you can choose here... Reasonable price and some stall the food really nice...

Visited 24/2/2023, 10pm

Food 4/5
Cleanliness 2/5
Service 3/5
Price 3/5
Atmosphere 4/5
Overall 3/5

Not very crowded when I visited on a Friday night. Tend to be crowded at night. There is a variety of food options available. Stalls open till late. Price is slightly on the high side, where a cup of teh costs $1.40.

However, the cleanliness is fairly poor. The toilet reeks urine smell and is stained.

Otherwise the atmosphere is quite enjoyable and laid back.

Will likely return again to chill only if I'm around this area.

+ 24hr

- Prices are higher than at Taman Jurong Hawker Centre next door. Cheapest options are:

- Yu You Charcoal Roast Delight's Wanton Mee ($4)

- Tenderbest's Chicken Wing rice ($4.50)

- Jap/Korean stall's rice dons with egg soup (chicken $5, salmon $5.50).

- Other stalls don't offer cheap dishes (Thai stall starts from $7 Basil Pork Rice)

+ Plenty of tables and seats

- Avoid the small carpark right next to the coffeeshop.

- No grace period !!

- No per minute parking.

- $1 per half hour in the mornings and afternoons. Premium rate of $1.64 per half hour during lunchtime 11am to 1pm and dinner times 5pm to 7pm !!

- $3.06 per entry after 9pm Mon to Sat, and whole day on SUNDAY and Public Holidays, which is not uncommon after coming from say a night festival event at Chinese Gardens.

- The much cheaper and just as close alternative is d basement HDB carpark at Taman Jurong ” Shopping Mall”.
Lots of dishes to choose from. The Mala is a popular choice.

Quite a newly renovated coffee shop situated at beside the bustling multi-storey hawker centre, with loads of selection from your typical tzechar fare to Mala to Western (and whatever you name it!)

🌶 The Mala store is apparently a branch of a Chinatown food store, and it serves up really delicious and affordable food! There’s also the option to have it with less salt, oil and MSG 👍🏻

🍲 curry fish head was okay, not very spicy but hits the right spot in terms of the taste

🥞 the pancake was really flavourful, and it comes with a variety of flavours for those who are looking for a change from time to time - perfect after meal treat

Suzuki Coffee is one of those hidden gems that proves great coffee lovers are willing to travel. Tucked away in an industrial area and not particularly easy to reach by public transport, I was surprised to find the café quite crowded on a weekend morning. That alone says a lot about its reputation

The exterior is striking, with large coffee-themed murals covering the roastery buildings and a spacious industrial setting that gives the place plenty of character. Inside, the atmosphere is calm, minimalist, and distinctly Japanese-inspired. Warm wood furnishings, clean lines, and soft lighting create a peaceful environment that feels far removed from the bustle of the city

Despite the crowd, the space never felt overly noisy. Many visitors were quietly enjoying their coffee, working on laptops, reading, or chatting with friends. The large window-side seating provides plenty of natural light and makes for a relaxing place to spend an hour or two

The coffee menu offers a good selection of beans with detailed tasting notes, ranging from chocolatey and nutty profiles to more floral and fruity options. I tried an iced flat white, which was smooth, well-balanced, and refreshing without being overly bitter. It is clear that coffee is taken seriously here

You’d love this cafe spot tucked in an industrial area ☺️
A Japan-style inspired interior, cozy, quiet, delicious coffee. Travel down here for a little getaway with a decent cup of coffee and enjoy it with a book in hand.. perfect spot for you to escape the SG crowds.

They have a few coffee bean selections to choose from, save your mind and let the friendly baristas to recommend for you ☺️

Visited one of Singapore's oldest coffee roasteries which is located in an industrial area. A quiet traditional Japanese tea house with nice zen-feel atmosphere

Matcha latte ~ smooth, creamy and earthy
¿ Iced Cascara Lemonade ~ perfect balance" of fruity and refreshing

Chocoalte brownie ~ satisfying delicious
Sanjuen Dorayaki Matcha ~ light, moist texture with matcha bitterness sweetness

Very pleasant place! Double story with plenty of choices for beans. Except that there is limited choice of food and not cheap for a coffee - about 8.5 nett. But definitely worth a try in the west given very limited cafes around here.

Very cozy nice little cafe at West! This will be my favorite spot for coffee at west area. And westerner deserve more good coffee cafe like this.
