Had a wonderful meal here! The food was absolutely delicious—the fish was incredibly fresh and the broth was rich and flavorful without being greasy. The portions are very generous and great for sharing.
​On top of the great food, the staff were friendly, attentive, and patient in explaining the menu. The dining environment is clean and comfortable, too. Will definitely visit again!
It’s our first time trying and it’s nice. The fish is fresh and the soup tasty. Good for gathering or family dinner.

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

Newly opened little pond restaurant at Jurong Point, and definitely worth a visit! The food is delicious, the staff are very friendly and welcoming, and the environment is clean and comfortable. Will definitely come back again!

The restaurant can be partitioned off to accommodate a private group of eighty (small) or open up to a few hundreds (D&D’s, corporate party etc..)

Lighting could be brighter - as the low/dim lights cast a chilly effect & hides a multitude of ‘sins’ the restaurant may not want you to notice or look too closely.

Service was fast & efficient - believe the crew for Friday lunch event were the more experienced staff.

I requested the vegetarian option since it’s no-meat on Friday due to religion for me.  It was going well till the mock meats appeared, & I am not a fan of mock meats.

The various veg that I plucked out of a roast ‘chicken’ & ‘fish’ dish were tasty enough for me not to starve.  The crab-meat soup I smelled from my fellow diners was so strong, I was glad to have my veg winter-melon soup.

The spinach mushroom dish was well-balanced, the sauce was light & tasty, coating the vegetable & mushroom without being over-powering or too thick like eating starch (I had this at other restaurants).

The orh-nee dessert was enjoyed by all, although some commented that it was a bit too sweet.

Overall, the non-veg dishes were as expected by the group, the vegetarian options needs to think of using less mock meats (?).

I attended a dinner function here recently and had their 8 course dinner menu. Their service was good and food was all nice. The fish was fresh too.

The only thing disappointing was that the food portion was a bit too small. There were actually 9 adults at my table instead of the usual 10 person and we finished everything. Some guests still felt hungry afterwards. Hope they can consider increasing the portion size for the dishes.

A delightful dinner at Pearl Garden at Jurong SAFRA. We were extremely pleased with the quality of the food and overall experience although there was a short circuit which lasted 15 minutes. I’m heartened to know that this restaurant is willing to employ senior citizens mostly age between 50 to 55. The venue is suitable for a big event to house up to 1,000 guests. There are multiple screens so you will not miss the performance wherever you are seated.
Celebrated my wife's birthday and mother's day on 10/05/2026. Food is great. Service is good. The or ni (Yam Paste) is has been and is still Fantastic!!!

Suitable for more than 50 tables for dinning.
Spacious restaurant.
Good delicious food.
Many video screens in the room.

A nice and modern mall conveniently located at the Jurong East transportation hub, with sheltered walkways linking it to two other popular malls in the area. The mall is spacious, thoughtfully designed with plenty of greenery, and offers a pleasant shopping experience.

Some sections are non-air-conditioned, which can feel warm during the afternoon, but the open concept adds to its vibrant, lifestyle-focused atmosphere. With a wide variety of food and beverage outlets, it’s a great destination for dining, shopping, and leisure.

Mall is around 10 years old located in Jurong east central and beside the MRT station. Good shopper traffic, more home goods, Fnb and lifestyle/services shops such as Nittori, Dreame and Paradise restaurants etc. Toilet cleanliness and  amenities can be improved. Carpark is easy to navigate.
Great place for casual shopping. Good spread of food. Easy connection to the jurong east train station.

As a frequent shopper in the west, I’ve spent countless hours at Westgate, and it has firmly established itself as one of my go-to destinations. Located at 3 Gateway Drive and directly connected to Jurong East MRT Station, its accessibility is virtually unbeatable . The mall is also linked to JEM and IMM via covered walkways, creating a massive interconnected shopping belt .

Westgate truly shines as a family and lifestyle hub. The layout is a mix of sleek, air-conditioned corridors and its signature open-air "Courtyard," which houses popular alfresco dining spots . However, the crown jewel for families is definitely the Westgate Wonderland on Level 4. This fantasy-themed outdoor playground is a massive hit with children, featuring giant interactive structures where they can burn off energy .

Foodies will be in heaven here. The mall boasts an impressive spectrum of dining options. You can grab a quick, affordable bite at the Food Republic food court in the basement or indulge in world-class ramen at Ippudo and next-level Japanese barbecue at Yakiniku Like . For those with a sweet tooth, the newly opened Kind Kone offers fantastic all-vegan ice cream with unique flavors like "Blue Planet," while Flipper’s serves up their legendary Japanese souffle pancakes . Don’t miss the Isetan Supermarket in the basement, which is a treasure trove of premium Japanese groceries and ready-to-eat meals .

On the retail front, Westgate covers all the bases. You’ll find major anchors like a sprawling two-story Uniqlo and a Japanese department store, alongside high-street favorites like Zara and COS . Tech enthusiasts can explore the Samsung Experience Store or Best Denki, while beauty lovers will appreciate the large Sephora outlet .

If I have to levy one major criticism, it’s the notorious parking situation. The carpark has a single, tightly designed entry and exit point that leads to the pick-up/drop-off area, creating absolute chaos during peak hours . Many reviewers report it can take 30 minutes just to exit . My advice? Take the MRT. It drops you right at the mall’s doorstep, saving you the headache.

Despite the driving frustrations, Westgate remains a vibrant and well-maintained mall. With its excellent store mix, incredible variety of food, and dedicated kids' facilities, it offers a complete package that caters to everyone from office workers to young families. It’s a solid 4 out of 5 stars for me.

Right next to JEM but feels a bit more "atas." The outdoor playground on the top floor is great for families with kids. Lots of popular restaurants like Tim Ho Wan and Din Tai Fung here. The link bridge to Jurong East MRT is very convenient and fully sheltered, which is a life-saver during those heavy afternoon downpours.

On April 1, 2026, I had a remarkable shopping experience at NTUC Fairprice Extra at Jurong Point, where I purchased hot beverages. After I paid, the cashier carefully packed my items, but unfortunately, the bag tore due to the sharp packaging. However, near the customer service counter, Officer Ms. Loh Koy Lin's attentiveness transformed the issue into a positive experience, as she swiftly guided me to a table outside the counter and offered assistance. With genuine kindness, she asked me to wait while she fetched a new bag to repack my items, showcasing her dedication to exceptional service. Her professionalism and courtesy not only salvaged but elevated my shopping experience, leaving a lasting impression and highlighting the impact one team member can have.

FairPrice Xtra at Jurong Point is a 24-hour retail giant.
It’s my top choice for hot-pot preparation because the ingredient selection is so extensive and complete. From household needs to specialized food sections, this hypermarket is a fantastic all-in-one destination for families.

Clean, big and spacious. This FairPrice Xtra has a lot of plant-based stuff for vegans and vegetarians (like plant-based meat, milk, cheese, dishwashing liquid etc). Organic stuff is available too.

Happy Shopping.

Whenever I buy either boneless chicken breasts or boneless chicken chop, I have to throw away at least half of the chicken because it is spoiled. It is shocking to me that the quality control is so bad in this regard.

Also, a minor pet peeve. There used to be an ice cooler for free ice to keep your items chilled on the way home near the service desk. They have moved the cooler back to the seafood/vegetable area. While I understand how this is useful for seafood purchases, it has made it difficult for anyone else who needs ice for their purchases.

Side note that facilities are also kept clean which is a plus! But would be good to include more dumbbells at the lighter range (between 4 to 6kg) as there may not be enough when classes are full.

Special thanks to coaches Raine, Danial, Chris, Kesavan, Daniel, Jeethin, Grace, Phoebe, and Eijah. They are always encouraging during workouts and very attentive in correcting my form. I have found a safe space where I can work towards my fitness goals! 💪🏻

He takes time to understand my fitness goals and always tailors the workouts to push me just the right amount. Min motivates without being overbearing, explains techniques clearly, and ensures I train safely while still making progress. Every session with him is effective, challenging, and enjoyable.

Highly recommend Snap Gym 24 Fitness, especially if you can train with Min—one of the best trainers I’ve met. 💪

Nice new gym. Very modern equipment, including those for Hyrox training. Plenty of treadmill for me.
Similar concept as Anytime F, but the equipment is so much better!! Check them out!

Joined Snapfitness Jurong West about 6 months ago and it’s spacious and well-maintained. Their wide range of equipment supports both strength and functional training really well.

I initially signed up the gym membership due to their HYROX class (they are HYROX affiliated!). The HYROX classes are well-structured, challenging, and trainers are encouraging. I’ve made many friends through these classes. The atmosphere during these sessions is energetic and supportive, making it easy to stay consistent and push my limits.

If HYROX is not your thing, they have Muay Thai and Yoga classes as well (which are very popular as well). There are also classes where they conduct gym equipment introductions for new gym goers.

Overall, a solid gym with good facilities (did I mention, they have showers too?) and excellent HYROX training — highly recommended for anyone serious about training. 5 stars!

The gym is overall quite nice—clean and well-maintained. It’s a bit of a tight squeeze space-wise, but for now, everything still feels fresh since it’s a new outlet. I hope the cleanliness is something they will continue to uphold as time goes on.

One thing that feels awkward is the design of the shower area. There’s no door, which I assume is because it’s a unisex space, but it’s a little strange to be drying your hair while being able to see someone working out just outside the corner of your eye.

Also, one of the toilet fan vents directly faces outward toward nearby windows. This feels like a potential privacy concern, (I’ve attached a photo for reference).

Overall, it’s a good gym with potential, but I hope they can address the shower privacy setup and keep maintaining the current standards of cleanliness.

The Jurong West ActiveSG was very Beautiful specially when it is Night time... It is very helpful for people of all ages specially the Older & Kids to do various kinds of Workout & Physical Activities...

As a visitor I got the Chance to visit it both Night & in the Morning... the Experience was very good
Usually go there for table tennis. Only Table 2, 3 and 4 are good for match play. Other tables have smaller spacing and suitable only for training.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Jurong West Swimming Complex — Hidden Gem Alert

Best Sunday experience, hands down. Not crazy crowded, which is a rare win in Singapore. The lap pool is bliss—can actually swim freely without dodging elbows. Bonus: it’s shaded from the sun, so no need to fry while doing laps.

Even under CAT1 lightning alerts, you can still swim (huge plus). The kids’ pool is a comfortable 0.9m depth, perfect for little ones. And yes—warm showers in the children’s facilities. Game changer.

There’s a decent water park setup for kids and a surprisingly long water slide that’s way more fun than expected. Honestly, this place is the most underrated swimming complex in Singapore. Total hidden gem. Don’t sleep on it.

A multi-story complex consisting of a stadium, swimming pool with water feature like slides and lazy river. The. Complex also houses Gymnasium, child care, tuition center, dance hall, cafe.....etc.
A complex cater to all age group. You can literally spent a whole day here. Car park is at complex ground level so no worries even if it rain.
As of 23 April 2022, the children play pool is still closed due to some maintenance works

Came here for the swimming lessons. The water playground is closed until further notice. ActiveSG staff is very friendly and helpful. Common areas are very clean. Floor of Toilet nearest to pool is quite dirty. Likely due to the extra human traffic with wet swimming attire, and not the fault of the cleaners.

Update (01Jun2022) - Water playground is partially opened. The mega slide is still closed. Lots of kids are playing in the water playground.

Some people are coming to play badminton without booking a badminton court. They simply use any empty court they see. Most of them are elderly people who come to join others’ games for free without booking a court or even bringing their own shuttlecocks. Hope the management can do more patrols and monitor the situation.

Jurong West ActiveSG Sports Complex is a gem in the heart of Singapore's western region. Perfectly equipped to cater to a wide range of fitness and recreational needs, it offers a welcoming environment for people of all ages and fitness levels.

One of the highlights of this sports complex is its state-of-the-art swimming pool. With clean and well-maintained facilities, including a kids’ pool, lap pool, and a wading pool, it’s a haven for swimmers and families looking for a refreshing day out. The lifeguards are attentive, ensuring safety for all.

Fitness enthusiasts will appreciate the well-equipped gym, featuring modern machines and spacious workout areas. The atmosphere is conducive to achieving fitness goals, with friendly staff always ready to assist. Additionally, the complex offers a variety of group classes such as yoga, Zumba, and aqua aerobics, catering to a wide range of interests.

The sports halls are another standout feature, accommodating sports like badminton, basketball, and table tennis. These facilities are well-maintained and ideal for friendly games or competitive events. The running tracks and outdoor spaces further enhance the appeal of the complex.

Accessibility is another advantage, with ample parking and proximity to public transport. The affordable entry fees and ActiveSG credits make it a cost-effective option for families and individuals alike.

Overall, Jurong West ActiveSG Sports Complex combines excellent facilities with a vibrant community atmosphere, making it a top choice for sports and recreation in Singapore. A visit here is always a rewarding experience!
Well built with combination of multi sports facilities. Court Labeled with specific sports. At down side, the air vent not as good. Warm weather with crowds in hall doubled the heat.

Played badminton there a few times. It has more than 10 courts to book. The courts are not super stuffy. Although courts near the doors might be an issue as you might need to keep closing them properly when people come in and leave the door open

It's really great to have a stadium close to where you live and use it for exercise. For seniors, this is perfect. Get a morning jog/walk in with many other seniors doing various exercises. And after cooling down, walk to the nearest hawker center for your favorite breakfast.
For national day celebrations, this stadium is also used for fireworks.
Great initiative to host a National Day celebration to bring together the whole estate - with food vouchers that helps to bring about business in the hawker centre! 🇸🇬

I attend the event for EZlink card presentation.  We were told to proceed to the buffet after taking our photo. I waited like fool when all the people at my rows and people behind me was given theirs.
I approach one of the staff, he assist to check for me. He came back to inform me that that photos was passed downstairs.
It's odd that 2 staff can distribute the photos and evepass to the lady beside me, accept me????

Great facilities and service from the staff here. I also like that they have a book exchange corner for residents to take and give away books.

I took the 1st dose of Pfizer Covid 19 vaccine here on 23 Jun 2021 through Parkway Shenton. it was smooth and orderly and well organized. The air conditioned badminton court was used as the vaccination centre.
Screened ,Vaccinated and discharged free gift from MOH 2nd on 05 June 2021
Clean, tidy and spacious. Many interesting course for adults and children.

Went to the help desk to speak to a male staff(middle age). Made sure that I was standing in front of him pointing to printer, asking him for help as I need it.

Rejected me 3 times. Nowhere around entire premises/neighbourhood could print. I DIDNT REALISE COMMUNITY CLUB is not for community simple help. Could have easily print but chose not to. He didn’t even thought about it and just reject. The feeling was horrible. Joke on how PA has such compassion when compassion is part of your motto.

Please check your CCTV. Rough time given to you. Email me if required, I can point out the person.

Great condition, fkoor not slippery and good ventilation yet not windy. So much better compared to Frontier CC. Water cooler is nearby and cold too!

Minus 1 star because ceiling too low haha

Small cosy community center serving the residents nearby. Center even has its own Mac! Courses offered by center are wide ranging and inexpensive. There are festive programs offered time to time that attracts lots of participation.
A community public place for all. You have event hall and can convert to basketball court. Facilities like badminton games can be play here as well.

The area is great for doing exercises..I usually do my morning walk along Jurong West st42 canal facing PIE, and then some exercises that are being put up by the relevant authorities.. Lately I found some dead branches that have fallen from the giant banana trees and coconut trees, and for more than a week, they are not been removed.. I hope the relevant authorities will be looking at these both unsafe and poor environment cleanliness...and quickly remove them away.

A newly opened playground in the park with clean facilities and a nice play area for kids
There is a toilet nearby, which is very convenient for everyone especially families with young children.

Do note it can get quite hot around noon as there’s limited shade. There isn’t a vending machine so it’s good to bring own drinks and snacks.

Overall, good addition to the park and great place for kids to spend their energy!
Nice new park. But partially open only. Cant wait to see what else is there. There’s another area with no playground where not much people!
Nice park with a sheltered playground for kids. Good to go in the morning. There are water coolers around and a toilet just beside the playground.

This playground is surprisingly impressive, offering an extensive variety of play items that keep children of all ages engaged with its diverse climbing structures and interactive stations. It’s a fantastic spot for a family afternoon, though it is important to note that there is currently no dedicated on-site parking, so you will need to utilize a nearby HDB carpark instead.
Looks nice, hope it can last longer than the equipment at Jurong Park. And please don't park at Street 22, pity the bus drivers can't make the turn.

Forest Ramble is a fantastic children’s play wonderland nestled in nature. The themed play zones — Monkey Play, Squirrel Play, and Snake Play — are creatively designed and encourage lots of climbing, balancing, and imaginative play. It feels adventurous yet thoughtfully integrated into the surrounding greenery

The playground is well laid out with different zones catering to kids of various ages, making it easy for children to explore while adults supervise comfortably. Do note that some sections may be under maintenance at times, but there’s still plenty to keep kids entertained
A beautiful relaxing park and with nice playground. Except that some parts are closed for maintenance since 7th July 2025. That’s quite a long time for maintenance….

One of the most exciting children outdoor playground.
Plenty of different play stations.
Swings, spring jumps, slides, flying fox, mini trampoline, adventure bridges, rope runs, see saws and many many more.
Exciting moments for kids age 5 to 12 years old and 2 to 5 yrs old with adult supervision.
Shaded area, water cooler, drinks vending machine and toilets are available.
Opening hrs:
8:00am - 10:00pm,
Closed on Mon (except for PH) and Tue if preceding Mon is a PH

This was a great place to bring my nephews and cousins. The playground is spacious, fun, and has a lot of different areas to explore, so it kept everyone entertained. It’s well designed, safe, and a really nice outdoor spot for families to spend time together.

Very nice playground with lot of play setup for kids from swings to slides. Jumping climbing everything was there in the playground. On Rainy days play equipments are not allowed to use until dry. Totally recommended

A great place to workout in jogging and exercising. Convenienly provided with a toilet (male & female) and there is a bus stop( bus 178) just below the staircase ,which will bring you to JE .Also a great place to have gatherings with friends, family ,and there are playgrounds for kids and two BBQ pits with washing facilities, under shelters.Alot of sheltered places in case of rain & there are alot tall trees with shelters around.

Taman Jurong Greens Park, nestled in the heart of Taman Jurong, is a true hidden gem that has stood the test of time. Known for its classic, old-school charm, this park offers a refreshing change from the more modern, bustling parks found around Singapore. Its mature trees, well-worn pathways, and serene landscapes make it an ideal spot for those looking to escape the fast-paced urban life and reconnect with nature.

One of the most convenient aspects of Taman Jurong Greens Park is its accessibility. Public transportation is a breeze, with bus number 178 stopping right by the park. This easy access makes it an attractive option for both locals and visitors who want to explore a more traditional side of Singapore. Additionally, there are various other amenities around the area, which adds to the park’s appeal as a convenient hangout spot. There are local eateries, convenience stores, and even a few retail shops within walking distance, so you can grab a snack or drink before heading to the park, or even enjoy a post-park meal nearby.

The park itself, while not overly large, has a charmingly rustic appeal. Its design reflects an older style of landscaping, with mature trees providing ample shade, well-maintained grassy areas perfect for picnics, and a few quaint park benches where you can sit back and relax. The pathways, though simple, are well-kept, allowing for a leisurely stroll or a morning jog without too many interruptions. While it may not have the modern playgrounds or extensive facilities of newer parks, Taman Jurong Greens Park holds its own through a sense of nostalgia and quiet beauty.

One of the standout features of this park is the sense of peace it offers. On most days, especially during weekday mornings, the park remains fairly quiet and uncrowded, providing an ideal environment for reading, meditating, or simply enjoying a moment of solitude. It’s a great place for families with young children to spend a relaxed afternoon, elderly folks looking for a calm walk, or individuals who want to reflect and unwind.

Taman Jurong Greens Park may not have the glitz of newer recreational spaces, but its charm lies in its authenticity and simplicity. It’s a park that holds memories, a park that serves as a reminder of simpler times, and a park that offers a space for genuine relaxation. Whether you’re a local resident or a curious visitor, Taman Jurong Greens Park is definitely worth a visit for anyone who appreciates a touch of nostalgia combined with nature.

Quiet and nice park, provides hanging poles for birds. A jurong heritage trail site, site for 1st national service camp. Now a fitness park, playground for kids with a corner for folks to hang their pet birds. There is a free public toilet within the park.
A small, clean and quiet park. Good for a walk with family and friends.

Small little neighbourhood park to visit. Neighbourhood residents are also commonly spotted here, particularly young children playing physical games or the elderly exercising. Not a bad place to visit, but rather small and not much to do there.

Very nice place to either have a slow walk, trail walk, or trail run. There are challenging slopes & steps, but there are also friendly sections to catch your breath. Distance wise, one can choose to walk from a short 3 KM route or a longer 6KM+ route. With the terrain, it is a good place to burn some calories and at the same time, enjoy the greenery with some  sightings of animals in the wild. So far, I have encountered calugo, blue diamond coral snake, pangolin, wild boar, monitor lizards, drongo (bird) and of course, monkeys (plenty of them).

Went hiking at Bukit Timah and Rifle Range and it turned out to be a really refreshing experience. The trail was surrounded by tall trees and thick greenery, making the whole walk feel calm and peaceful. As I went down the wooden steps, I could hear the sounds of nature all around me—birds, insects, and the rustling of leaves.
Along the way, I noticed small details that made the hike even more special, like tiny plants growing out of old wood and patches of moss spreading across fallen logs. It reminded me how alive the forest is, even in the smallest places.
The hike wasn’t too easy, especially with the long paths and steps, but it felt satisfying. I managed to cover about 7.8 kilometers, and even though I was tired, it was a good kind of tired. Overall, it was a simple but meaningful experience—just being surrounded by nature, slowing down, and enjoying the moment.

Bukit Timah Nature Reserve is a great spot to switch into hiking mode on the weekend.

There are lots of stairs, so be ready for a workout — definitely a sweat-it-out session 💦
Perfect if you’re looking to move your body and get a good sweat in nature.
Brisk walking during the early morning... able to feel the fresh air... a good place to have a short exercises as well.

If you love nature, I recommend you doing a hike here. It’s good for beginners and all the trails are colour coded for the difficulty level. Along the trails, there will be sheltered post for you to rest. You can easily spot some common dwellers such as snakes, monkeys and wild boar. If you are lucky enough, you may find some cool birds. I come here almost every week and spend 1-2hrs doing the hike.

A lovely small rainforest park tucked away in the west that feels surprisingly immersive despite its compact size. The walking trails are well-paved and shaded by tall trees, making it comfortable for both casual strolls and light workouts. There’s also a playground and a simple fitness corner, so it works well for families as well as those looking for a quick exercise session

One of the highlights is the lookout point overlooking the quarry — a peaceful spot where you can pause, enjoy the greenery, and listen to the sounds of nature. Wildlife sightings are quite common too; it’s not unusual to spot colourful birds, squirrels, and even monkeys if you’re lucky. History buffs will appreciate the remnants of WWII structures such as old pillboxes scattered around the park, which add a subtle layer of heritage to the natural scenery

The only downside at the moment is that the path leading to the Syonan Chureito (the WWII Japanese remembrance site) is under maintenance, so access is temporarily closed

It was my first and hope it's not the last visit to this place. I really want to go there again.

Really liked the place and enjoyed walking around this place. This would definitely be a cute for all of your sadness.

Didn't realise there was a quarry in the park, took a long walk in nature and found many wonderful things! Was caught by a sudden downpour, appreciated the thick trees around the area which sheltered me from the rain.

Good mix of trails, some paths are cobblestones so be in a proper footwear.

Caught a squirrel in action too!

🌳A lush greenery that is just a stone throw away from the Bukit Batok Housing Estate, providing residents living here a green lung to relax, exercise and be with nature.
🚶‍➡️🏃‍➡️Many came here in the morning for exercises and brisk walk.
🛝🏋️At the Bt Batok Nature Park, besides having numerous shelters and walking paths, there is a Children Playground for little ones to enjoy, a Foot Reflexology Path and an Adult Fitness Corner that are next to the Playground.
🏞️But the centrepiece is the Lookout Point, where one can rest, take in the sight of the tranquil water and it's scenic surrounding.
⛩️Another interesting place around the Park with World War II history is Syonan Chureito Memorial
🚧Unfortunately, the access path from the park proper is closed for safety reason and is undergoing stabilisation work which is expected to be completed end Dec 2027.
Nevertheless, the site can be reached from Lorong Sesuai.
🪨Fun fact:
Bukit Batok Nature Park was formerly a granite quarry site.
Like many other granite quarry around, it ceased operation for safety and environmental reasons. And was then transformed into a nature park for all to enjoy.
Bukit batok natural park nice and peaceful park see sunrise depend on the cloud good place exercise 2 jan 2026

It's been such a long time since I last visited the Chinese Garden. So much has changed. Evening walk till night and managed to get two different scenery of the same place. Very quiet with few joggers and walkers. Interesting new features are added here. Saw a magpie calling for friends and a stork having a stroll. White crane feeding at the open field from the mrt station. Surprised to find a little bamboo lane leading to a waterfall.

Nice scenic garden in the west, conveniently accessible via public transport and a network of cycling pathways linked to its entrances. Completely free of charge as well. Best to come here on weekday evenings for better weather and less crowd.

A scenic and serene park built in the style of northern Chinese imperial architecture and landscaping. It is just a short walk away from either Chinese Garden or Lakeside MRT Stations. The key highlights of the Garden are the 13 arch White Rainbow Bridge, the Grand Arch building, the Stone Boat and Tea Pavilion, the pagodas and the gorgeous lotus pond. You may get to see little animals like birds, snakes and others thriving in the park

Very spacious garden/park area! Highly recommend coming during sunset as it sort of cools down and there’s more shade.

I’d come on a weekday to avoid crazy crowds of people sitting and talking at specific spots.

There’s an ice cream shop and a cafe in the park areas as you walk around!

It’s very clean and suitable for all ages! There are bikers and runners so keep your ears open as people are going pass you!

A Tranquil Masterpiece in the Heart of the West

The newly reopened Chinese Garden is simply stunning. Every detail — from the traditional architecture to the lush landscaping — feels thoughtfully designed and beautifully maintained. It’s like stepping into a serene, storybook version of ancient China, right here in Singapore.

The paths wind gently through water lily ponds, stone bridges, pavilions, and tranquil courtyards. The garden feels spacious but intimate at the same time, with quiet spots to sit and reflect, and picture-perfect views everywhere you look. You can tell that immense care has gone into preserving the cultural elements while making it inviting for all visitors.

It’s not just a place to walk — it’s a place to slow down. Whether you’re into photography, nature, or just want a peaceful escape from city life, the Chinese Garden delivers a truly magical experience.

I can’t recommend it enough — one of Singapore’s most beautifully restored gems.

1. It is the 1st largest man-made floating wetlands in SG.
2. Opened in 2021, it is a key feature of the lake enhancement, offering a large boardwalk and serving as a natural habitat that improves water quality.
3. It is managed under National Water Agency PUB’s Active, Beautiful, Clean Waters (ABC Waters) Programme, the wetlands not only play a part in maintaining the water quality in the reservoir but also creates a natural habitat for wildlife.
4. It consists of a 22 cm thick buoyant layer made from five layers of foam mats and coconut mats, which supports diverse aquatic plants.
5. Area of constructed floating wetlands 3,850m²; Area of Jurong Lake 70 hectares (700,000m²)

A fantastic spot for wide, open views of Jurong Lake. From here, you get a clear and scenic perspective of both Jurong West and Jurong East buildings, nicely framed by greenery and water. The elevated boardwalks make it feel immersive yet peaceful, especially on a breezy or cloudy day

It’s a great place to pause, take photos, or simply enjoy the contrast between nature and the surrounding urban skyline

I was Just passing by Jurong Lakeside and just decided to walk through and that was a nice decision as it was really peaceful and relaxing! The view there is Especially nice and Moreso from the Chinese Garden MRT Side, definitely will be coming here again to check out the Chinese Garden Islet!

A good place to take a small walk alone or with your friends and family. But you gotta know that this place is really small. If you got enough time then why not take a look at the nearby Chinese and Japanese gardens.

Beautifully created floating platform on top of the lake with a closer next-to-water experience on the lake. Has many seats along this platform for resting as well as to catch sunrise and potentially some sunset views.

Good placement in front of Starbucks that you can take a break there if you’re tired or thirsty as well!

place_name: My First Skool at Blk 459 Jurong West St 41
review_text:

They are also willing to listen to parents feedback and regularly update parents on child progress in school.

The principals (past and present) are very friendly, reliable and approachable. Location wise is also convenient for parents to pick up and drop off their children from school and have quick dinner at coffeeshop nearby or engage in a bonding session at the nearest playground. Would definitely recommend any parents to enroll their child at JW8 centre.

place_name: My First Skool at Blk 459 Jurong West St 41
review_text:
Our family has been with My First Skool at JW8 for several years now. Both of our daughters started here in infant care, with the older one now being 4 years old. The environment has been consistently warm, supportive, and engaging throughout their time here.

We really appreciate how dedicated and caring the Principal and teachers are. They take time to understand each child and keep us well informed about their learning and progress. It’s been very encouraging to see our kids pick up new skills and grow in confidence.

What stands out most to us is how happy our children are to go to school every day. The school has a very welcoming atmosphere — even the auntie cook and cleaning aunties are friendly and approachable, helping to create a safe and comfortable environment for the children.

We highly recommend My First Skool JW8 to any parent looking for a nurturing and well-run preschool.

place_name: My First Skool at Blk 459 Jurong West St 41
review_text: We are very fortunate to be able to send our kids here :) JW8 has some of the most caring and hardworking teachers I've ever met. Plus they're almost always aligned with our parenting style and attitudes. Thank you JW8!

place_name: My First Skool at Blk 459 Jurong West St 41

I didn't have pain and I realised after reaching home that there's a slight chip on my front tooth that was not there.
She told my teeth was fine and told me that one year later will be fine and why suggest for an x-ray. Are these dentists newbies sent to poly dentals and attend to seniors like myself. Just curious. Dammit, there's a chip on my tooth!

Jurong Town Hall Bus Interchange is a small but convenient interchange that connects residents and office workers to nearby areas. The layout is straightforward, with clear signboards and sheltered boarding areas. Buses arrive and depart regularly, and the place is generally clean and well-maintained. There are benches for waiting passengers and a sheltered walkway linking to nearby buildings. The interchange is also wheelchair accessible with ramps and wide pathways, making it easy for everyone to use.

New interchange is big and airy. Causeway terminal is right at the back.

decent bustop, outside parameters and connection bridge like never completing project.  Here you can get Malaysia cross border bus, quite funny on Malaysian bus. you will surprise on their strictness on safety awareness, I suppose as long as the bus arrives at the destination --- it is a good and working bus.

Jurong Town Hall Bus Interchange is similar to Jurong East's secondary "airport" where there are now four local bus services and the Causeway Link of Malaysia taking up tenancy at this terminus. This is to further enhance the easy management of bus movements within the Jurong East area as there is construction for J'Den (future residential) and Jurong East JRL (JE5) MRT.

There are five bus berths and three arrival berths (in a parallel parking orientation). All four local buses are trunk route services with 78 (Clementi loop via Pandan Gardens and Penjuru), 79 (Boon Lay loop via Teban Gardens and Jalan Buroh), 160 (Johor Bahru Sentral via Woodlands Checkpoint loop), and 870 (Tengah). The Causeway Link CW3 (Bukit Indah and Perling via Tuas Checkpoint) and CW4 (Terminal Pontian via Tuas Checkpoint) ply between Malaysia's Iskandar Puteri and Kukup areas with Singapore's Jurong East terminus

A Near-Perfect Hokkien Mee Experience at Feng Hokkien Mee

I'm thrilled to share that my second visit to Feng Hokkien Mee was a resounding success! After providing feedback on my previous visit that the Hokkien Mee was a tad too soggy, the boss took swift action to adjust the recipe. And wow, the result is near perfection!

The Hokkien Mee now strikes the perfect balance - the noodles are cooked just right, allowing the wok hei to shine through. The chilli is expertly mixed, complementing the pork lard beautifully. Each bite transports me to the streets of Singapore, where Hokkien Mee is a staple.

Living in the west side of Singapore, it's a challenge to find authentic, top-notch Hokkien Mee. Feng Hokkien Mee is a game-changer. With its commitment to quality and responsiveness to customer feedback, this humble stall has the potential to claim the throne.

Starting a new business is never easy, especially with soaring costs. I applaud the owners for persevering. Now, it's about spreading the word and building a loyal customer base. Nine months of consistent excellence will surely cement Feng Hokkien Mee as a neighborhood favorite.

If you're a Hokkien Mee aficionado, do give Feng Hokkien Mee a try. Trust me, you won't be disappointed.

Rating: 5/5 stars

Recommended for: Hokkien Mee lovers, foodies, and anyone seeking authentic Singaporean flavors.

Tips:
- Get there early to avoid disappointment - this stall is a hidden gem that's gaining popularity.
- Try the Hokkien Mee with extra pork lard for added richness.
- Share your feedback with the boss - he's keen to improve and delight customers.

In conclusion, Feng Hokkien Mee is a must-visit for anyone craving authentic, top-notch Hokkien Mee. With its commitment to quality and customer feedback, this stall is on the rise. Don't miss out!

Would you like to know more about Feng Hokkien Mee's menu or location? Have you tried their Hokkien Mee before, and what's your take on the dish?

Thank you for taking over Cathay! I really enjoyed the Premiure Class experience. Food ordering was easy and steamless. The seat are spacious and comfortable. Thank you for taking over Cathay — the Premiere Class experience at Shaw Theatres was truly impressive 👏

From the moment we stepped in, everything felt elevated. The ordering process was seamless and fuss-free, making the whole experience feel smooth and well thought out.

The seats were incredibly spacious and plush, perfect for fully relaxing throughout the movie. What really stood out was the thick, cozy blanket — a small touch, but it made a huge difference in comfort and warmth.

Overall, it’s a premium cinema experience that delivers on both comfort and convenience. Definitely worth it for a more luxurious movie night — will be back again! 🎬✨

First of all, thank you Shaw for taking over Cathay and giving us West-siders a proper cinema again! Super grateful to have a nice place nearby to catch movies. I want to commend the team for transforming the space into a much more comfortable and movie-vibe environment compared to the previous operator (specifically the hallway and toilets). Took many photos during my trip.

I recently had the opportunity to experience the Premiere halls (specifically Premiere 1 & Premiere 2). I understand that this is a new team and they are still familiarising themselves with the SOPs. I can see they are trying their best. Still, I would like to share both the positive aspects and areas for improvement based on my visits.

What I Loved:
1) I tried the seafood pizza & it was delicious. Although the price is on the higher side, the quality justifies it. The food definitely stands out as one of the highlights of the Premiere experience.

2) The adjustable sofa chairs are very comfortable & the overall privacy in both Premiere halls is excellent. It really elevates the experience and makes the upgrade feel worthwhile.

3) When I caught The Predator at Premiere 2 hall, the sound was loud, immersive & clear. Exactly what I expect from a premium hall. Sadly wasn’t the case for Premiere 1 hall.

Areas of Improvement:
1) The lighting for the seat numbers is very bright in Premiere 1 hall (refer to photo) and was distracting during the movie. Dimming them slightly would improve the overall ambience.

2) Because the team is new, I understand they’re still adapting but there were noticeable inconsistencies:
- Premiere 2 hall, I was given cutlery and tissue with my pizza. Premiere 1 hall, I wasn’t.
- I encountered issues with both Premiere halls: once I couldn’t use the discount code and another time my order didn’t arrive until staff repeatedly came back and forth to resolve it.
The staff are young and clearly trying their best but the service processes may need refinement so the experience feels smooth and premium.

3) When I watched Wicked in Premiere 1, the audio sounded muffled and soft, especially from the front speakers. This was quite noticeable and very different from my excellent experience in Premiere 2. I’m not sure if this was due to the sound mix provided for the film or the hall setup but the inconsistency between the two halls was obvious.

Despite the hiccups, the improvements to the cinema are significant & I appreciate having such a comfortable well-designed place close to home. Just need adjustments to service consistency and technical setup (JEM please don’t disappoint Shaw good sound system reputation).

Thank you.

Hindhede Nature Park is one of Singapore’s hidden gems for anyone looking to enjoy nature without committing to a long or strenuous hike. Despite its relatively small size, the park packs in an impressive variety of rainforest scenery, peaceful walking trails, and one of the most rewarding viewpoints in the country

The trails are well-maintained and easy to follow, making the park suitable for families, seniors, and casual walkers. Along the way, you’ll find lush tropical vegetation, towering trees, interesting plant life, and plenty of shaded paths that make for a relaxing stroll even on warmer days. The shelters and lookout points are thoughtfully placed, offering great spots to rest and soak in the surroundings

The highlight is undoubtedly the former quarry lookout. The emerald-green water framed by dramatic rock cliffs creates a surprisingly scenic landscape that feels far removed from the city

It seems this is my favourite spot to hike and train myself for my next climb. The trail is like super easy so I do many repetitions. The place is nice. Plenty of wild snakes and monkeys to look for. If you are lucky, juvenile king cobras too.
Nice and peaceful park with an easy walking trail. Great for a short stroll, and the quarry view is really nice.
This is an easy trail and accessible via either from car park or Bukit Timah visitor center. That’s a playground for children age 7-12 also. No admission ticket required.

Such a pretty park ! Super near Bukit Timah Summit so it can be a little outing. Went around noon and I was almost fried by the sun. Lowkey got lost but it was relatively easy to find my way to the Quarry ! Recommend to visit in the late afternoon to avoid the sun hehe. Not sure why they say you need to buy tickets but it’s free !!
Good.

We had such a wonderful time at the Northwest Cluster’s Sports Day Carnival, organized by Kidz Meadow @ Woodlands! 🎉 The event was very well planned, fun-filled, and engaging for both the children and parents. It was heartwarming to see the kids so excited and confident while participating in the activities.

The teachers and staff did a fantastic job ensuring everything ran smoothly, creating a safe and joyful environment. We truly appreciate the effort, dedication, and energy that went into making this event such a success.

Thank you, Kidz Meadow, for creating meaningful memories for our children and families. We’re already looking forward to the next event!

Teacher Yanna and Cikgu Seri have made a wonderful impact on my child. Under their caring guidance, she has grown more confident and independent, especially in simple daily routines like wearing and taking off her shoes on her own. With their gentle encouragement, she has also managed to stop using her pacifier and smelly pillow, which is a big milestone for her.
I’m truly grateful for the patience, warmth, and dedication they give every day. Their support has helped my child develop not only academically, but also emotionally and socially. Thank you for making such a positive difference in her life.

We honestly have nothing but good things to say about this childcare. The teachers and staff are all so warm, patient, and genuinely caring,you can really see how much they love and look out for the kids.

We would like to compliment all the teachers who have taught and cared for Uwais in N1B, with a special mention to Teacher Mubee. Her dedication and love for her class really shows. She takes the time to understand each child, and it’s clear that she gives her best every day.

When Uwais first started, he was a quiet, nervous little boy who didn’t want to go to school. Now, he has grown into a much more confident child and is always excited to tell me about his day and all the fun activities he does in school. That change didn’t happen overnight; it’s the result of consistent support, encouragement, and care from the teachers and staff.

We are truly grateful to everyone who has been part of Uwais’s journey so far. Thank you for creating such a safe, nurturing, and joyful environment for him to grow, learn, and gain confidence. We feel very blessed to have him in this childcare.

So many positive things to say abt kmjw i dont even know where to start. No regrets for enrolling our child here, where we met soooo many great teachers and where the environment is always so lively and loving. Special mention to Teacher Tse Er and Cikgu Seri for being the best teachers to my daughter and genuinely caring for her since day 1. Thank you for setting the bar so high that no other schs or teachers may not be able to meet our expectations. Thank you for being such core memory for us as first time parents and for our girl as well. Continue soaring high and remain as lovely and warm 💕

My son is currently in Toddler B. He absolutely loves going to school to meet his teachers and friends! He has grown so much since he started school.

Recently we had a school trip to River Wonders and since my son has been learning about sea creatures, he was very excited to see the animals.

His teacher, Tr Nurul is very patient, bubbly and knowledgeable. We are very sad to hear that she’s transferring to another centre in the East. Nonetheless, I wish her good luck in the new center.

Thank you Tr Nurul, Tr Tse Er, Cikgu Seri & Tr Dhilah!

place_name: Star Learners @ Jurong West | Preschool & Childcare

review_text: I am happy with the teachers and principal. They are professional and caring towards my child. They always update me on my child`s progress.

place_name: Star Learners @ Jurong West | Preschool & Childcare
review_text:
Both of my daughters have attended Star Learners @ Jurong West, and overall, we’ve had a very positive experience. The teachers are warm, caring, and genuinely dedicated to the children’s growth and happiness. The environment is safe, welcoming, and encourages creativity, play, and learning. We especially appreciate how well they communicate with parents through daily updates and photos, it gives us peace of mind knowing how our children are doing throughout the day.

The curriculum is well thought out, with a good mix of structured learning and free play. Our girls have developed both socially and academically during their time here, and they always come home with big smiles and new stories to tell. The staff turnover seems relatively low, which is a great sign of stability and good leadership.

Overall, we’re grateful for the care and support the team has given our daughters, and we would recommend this center to other families looking for a nurturing and enriching environment for their children.

Our family has had a wonderful experience at MindChamps Preschool @ Jurong West. All three of our children have attended the school, with one already having graduated to Primary 1. The teachers are patient, caring, and communicative, which gives us great peace of mind as parents.
We transferred from another preschool two years ago after reading the positive reviews, and it has truly been the right decision. Although we travel quite a distance, it has been well worth the effort.
We would like to give special thanks to Ms Jamie and her dedicated team of teachers for nurturing our children and helping them grow into such confident little Champs!

My son has been attending MindChamps Jurong West since PG. We explored other preschools before deciding on MindChamps, and we’re glad we did. Their extensive curriculum, together with how quickly my son adjusted and how much he genuinely enjoys school, made it the right choice for us.
The teachers are professional, nurturing, and patient. They consistently provide constructive and supportive feedback to help us better support our child’s development.
A special thank you to Ms Fer & Ms Liu, their dedication and care have made a remarkable difference. I’m truly proud of my son’s growth under their guidance. ☺️

It’s been 1.5 years since my girl joined MindChamps Preschool @ Jurong West, and we’ve seen steady growth—not just academically, but also in her confidence and communication skills (both English and Mandarin!). Kudos to the principals, teachers, admin staff, and aunties for their dedication!

Both my kids have graduated from mindchamps a few years back. Mindchamps has helped to build a strong foundation for their Primary 1, allowing the transition to P1 to be very smooth. They are both confident learners in their current primary school.

MindChamps Preschool impressed me with their exceptionally well-organized sports day event. From start to finish, the seamless coordination and meticulous planning were evident. The engaging activities were thoughtfully designed to cater to the diverse needs of young children, fostering both physical and social development. The dedicated staff ensured a safe and supportive environment, allowing each child to participate with enthusiasm. The inclusion of various sports not only showcased a holistic approach to education but also emphasized the importance of teamwork and sportsmanship. MindChamps Preschool's commitment to excellence was truly evident, making the sports day a memorable and enriching experience for both children and parents alike.

Thank you Mindchamps Jurong West for an unforgettable experience.

Although I was a mediocre student at O levels, I had scrapped through to enter JJC then located close to Pandan reservoir. My WORST educational experience ever. Looking back it is Best to ERASE them totally from my mind as the years go by
My form tutor was a very bullying woman and looked down on me thinking I will eventually be a Loser in life. Fast forward and I had proven her WRONG!!!
Except for one classmate, all the rest of classmates both male and female were Bullies.
Especially these tall guys named Elwin Ng, Patrick Tay and Perry Thye.

This jc has closed. The merged school is jurong pioneer jc.
Powerful gym with good pokemon. Easy to take down

I am writing to express my sincere appreciation for the outstanding service provided by Madam Maria, Care Coordinator at Pioneer Polyclinic. Her exceptional professionalism and empathetic approach were evident throughout our consultation, and her guidance on managing pressure was both valuable and reassuring. Her commitment to her work is genuinely commendable, and I firmly believe she warrants formal recognition for her exemplary care. Additionally, I would like to commend Dr. Tricia Lim Wan Ning for her professionalism, patience. Well done and care, Pioneer Polyclinic has improved significantly over the years.

I had an excellent experience at Pioneer Polyclinic. The registration process was smooth, and the waiting time was reasonable. The doctors were professional, patient, and took the time to explain my condition and treatment clearly. The nurses and counter staff were also friendly, courteous, and efficient throughout my visit. The clinic was clean, well-organized, and provided a comfortable environment. Overall, I’m very satisfied with the care and service I received. Thank you to the entire team for your dedication and professionalism.

I made an appointment and arrived an hour early. However, you still have to wait till your appointment time and the number of patients before me just kept increasing as time passes by, even when it's my appointment time It's no use being early, you will be penalised to wait. It's better to be late then early in this system as they will slot you in if you are late. Being early means you will be exposed to virus for a longer period also.

Here’s a clean, firm Facebook post (serious but still controlled and credible):

Today I want to share a difficult experience at Pioneer Polyclinic.

I arrived at 9:46 AM for my 9:50 AM appointment, already rushing for my medical consultation. At the entrance, I was stopped by security and subjected to a screening process in full view of the public.

I was required to show my IC, open my bag, remove my belongings, turn around, and raise my hands. This was done openly where other patients were present.

I am deeply dissatisfied with how the screening was conducted. While I understand security checks are necessary, the way it was carried out felt intrusive, unnecessarily public, and not respectful of patient dignity—especially in a healthcare setting.

What made it worse was the lack of courtesy and the dismissive attitude during the process, even after I explained I was late for my appointment.

As a result of the stress, my blood pressure rose significantly, which is concerning given my health condition.

I hope this is reviewed so that security procedures in healthcare settings can be carried out with more respect, discretion, and consideration for patients who are there for medical care.

This is shared as feedback, not to attack individuals, but to highlight a serious concern about patient experience and dignity.

The system that assign appointment really need to be changed or updated. 10 times of doctor appointment, at least 5 of it is late by at least an hour and 3 of it is late by up to half hour. The remaining 2 are either slightly early or on time. There is seriously no point of giving out appointment time but in the end it is not being followed? This is my point of view based on at least 5 years of bringing my mother for appointments and once every 3 months.

I was seen by hand therapist Jasmine Ang for my central slip injury with fracture. With the right exercises and guidance, she helped me recover much faster than I expected. Very professional, supportive, and explains everything clearly. Highly recommend for anyone needing hand or finger physiotherapy.

I was seen by hand therapist Jasmine Ang for my left dequervain.

She is very professional and detailed in explaining the cause of my pain and every process of the therapy. A patience therapist that I trust and my hand improve quite well after third appointment
Thank you Jasmine.

I had left wrist pain De' Quervain since June 2025, seen by hand therapist Jasmine Ang. She was very knowledgeable and guided me through exercises to improve my condition. She clearly explained the reasons behind my injury and made the sessions engaging. I would highly recommend her as an occupational therapist!

The dermatologist deserve a 0 star to be honest.

First visit I was prescribed steroid cream, and I was told that the "blisters" on my hand (I have dyshidrotic eczema) would go away.

Having tried steroid cream all my life and them not really working, I had my doubts and asked "well, what if it doesn't go away?" and I was told that "The blisters will go away" and I will get seen again in 4 months.

Well, to be fair it did go away, but the blisters came back again!! On the 2nd visit, told the doctor there that it did go away for a week or so, but then it came back!!

Told the doctor that it got worse, it is spreading everywhere (on my feet now as well), guess what I got? The same steroid cream (which I have been applying for the past 3 months mind you and it didn't work), and I will get seen again in 6 MONTHS.

Just because my condition is MILD, it is not being taken as seriously as it should be. This chronic condition has caused me a lot of distress, but what would the doctor know? Just keep using steroid cream and hope for the best?

I am trying to keep my hands free of blister forever, but so far every doctor I've seen just wants to treat the symptom and never the cause.

Trigger finger release. OT Jasmine Ang helped me to regain full function of my triggered finger. She is really good in explaining the science behind what she did and how the exercises helped in the recovery process. Very competent and her friendly disposition helps a lot during the therapy sessions.

I am utterly appalled at the incompetence and negligence shown by the student care services at KRTC Jurong West . My experiences seeking help and resources have revealed deep issues with the staff's ethics, responsibility and basic concern for student safety.

At every turn, staff have evaded taking any real action or ownership of students' needs. They consistently push issues off onto other departments and pass the buck endlessly. I have found the management to be the worst offenders when it comes to apathy and deflection. They seem to have no grasp of what their roles entail or how to provide competent oversight.

Most disturbing is the lack of safety protocols and preventative measures put in place. On multiple occasions I have found myself and fellow students in dangerous situations that could easily have been avoided if staff demonstrated basic care and precaution. It is clear this department is putting students at unacceptable risk.

I urge all students to steer completely clear of these deficient and hazardous student care services. They will not support you in any way and worse still, actively allow harm through negligence. The administration must intervene immediately to address the incompetence of management and overhaul proper training as well as accountability measures for all staff. Our well-being and safety should be their top priority. I hope sharing my experiences prevents more students from such mistreatment.

Yao Lao Shi is a remarkable teacher who consistently goes above and beyond to inspire, educate, and support their students. Her dedication, passion, and innovative teaching methods have made a positive impact on the lives of my daughter. Thank you, Yao Lao Shi, for your commitment to excellence and for being a beacon of knowledge and guidance for my daughter.
Miss Liza is very patient teacher. She used good methods to help my cousin understand the math concepts that are needed. My cousin enjoys her lesson very much. Recommended.
The teachers and people are good hearted and genuinely care for the students. In a convenient location also
Very effective teachers, very accommodating and helpful staff and management,  Highly recommended

It is one of the larger size interchanges, however the layout is not the most convenient. Some of the buses which are going to the same directions or at the very two ends of the station, making it very inconvenient for people to choose between two buses. Despite the fact they do have an update on digital schedule board, the timing isn't totally accurate . The location also becomes extremely hectic for both pedestrian and vehicle traffic.

Pros:
Update digital schedule board for bus arrival
Large

Cons:
Digital schedule board isn't totally punctual
The layout of the line is confusing
New bus interchange, spacious and clean. Though it’s a slightly longer walk from MRT. But nicely sheltered.

This happened on the 19th of March at around 4.25pm, Jurong East Bus Interchange. I arrived at the interchange and saw on My Transport SG app indicating bus service 335 is arriving in 1 minute time. By the time, I rushed to its bus stand, the information panel verified that it’s arriving. Two minute past and the next thing I know, the information panel suddenly indicated that they wait has turned into a whole 8 minute.

Needless to say, those there were in the same queue as I were visibly frustrated. As I stayed around Jurong West Avenue 1, I actually have two more bus service options to choose (namely 98 and 335), but since not bus stands are placed a whole 5 min walk away from the 334 bus stand, it doesn’t allow passengers to quickly switch queue based on first arriving bus service.

I’m highlighting this as a concerned commuter as our bus service has always been pride for its reliability and transparency. As I understand, Tower Transit is a contracted, I would still hold the very same expectations I would with other bus companies like SBS. This gross lapse were fueled when there was no explanation given by the subsequent driver of the bus service nor was there any attempt to quell on the interchange PA system.

This total disregard to the commuters’ time is concerning and honestly, infuriating. Please do better.

Service no 335 punctuality is suck.
Just a bus stop outside the terminal , displaying 1 minutes waiting time can end up waiting for 20 minutes. This happens quite frequently. Need to improve.

New interchange replacing old Jurong East Temp Int to facilitate rail building.

Picture taken from 2019.

There are lots of food options here with affordable price. I personally like the western food, Japanese food and mixed veg rice here.
If you are first time here, recommend the western food for taste and price., the western comes with two side dishes and the portion is very generous. Taste is yummy!
The Japanese food is yummy, except the kimchi.
Cai peng tastes nice, very affordable and big portion.
The drink stall upstairs has a variety of choices.
The fish soup at level 1 is quite generous in terms of its portion, taste wise is average.
The duck rice is cheaper than other places, and portion is slightly bigger but the duck meat that I had is not tender tho, quite hard.
The wanton noodle upstairs is average, portion is small but has quite a special texture.
The noodle stall at level 1 (last pic) tastes bad, however, cheap, only $3 for bak cho Mee, not recommend unless you are tight in wallet. The dry bak cho Mee pork is so watery and the taste is so diluted therefore, noodle texture not good as well.
To conclude, would recommend this hawker centre here.

Found this very nice pan-fried pork bun @Xiangyu Pan-Fried Bun stall in the new Jurong West hawker center. Very hot & juicy inside. Nicer than xiao long bao.

Place is very spacious and clean and with basement carpark. Not crowded as place is new.

One of my favourite hawker centers! The seating is spacious and the fans make it cool. The food is always delicious, authentic and there are some unique stalls that serve up something different (and tasty)! My favourite go-to stall is the xiangju pan fried bun. It’s a bit like being in Shanghai, but with the Singaporean friendly touch! The rojak is a good comfort food alternative too.

1st tried at the decent stall serving freshly made beef noodles with generous portions. The broth is umami-rich and flavourful, yet light, not oily or overly salty. Hawker environment is clean & airy.

I tried the pan fried pork bun ($4.50 for 3 pcs) and nasi lemak ($3.50). They were both amazing. Really hearty and filling, also moderately spicy. I also tried their iced kopi ($1.50), which was strong and a perfect balance between sweet and nutty.

Really good food with spacious dining and multiple options. Reasonably priced as well. I would visit again to try other foods.

A beautifully decorated food hall with so  many types of food to choose from. Ordered Set A - Boneless Grilled Fish, this is a unique dish & taste with green peppercorn but a little salty for me. The Basil Pork Fried Rice with mango salad is oily and salty not to my liking but the mango salad is refreshing. The Mala Fish is soggy, I think they used Dory Fish, also not to my liking.

I was at Jurong Point again and went to "Jiak Ba Buay" food court by Kopitiam for lunch. I ordered Kimchi Pancake, Kimchi Soup, and Pomfret Fish Soup. The Kimchi Pancake and soup were yummy, but the Pomfret Fish Soup was a little mushy — not to my liking.

Fairly spacious and very good price specially with the app. Nowadays it has a lot of “specialty gourmet fares” for people it’s affordable price. Certainly worth it for value.

Don’t expect exceptional services though as most are just making ends meet

[New update]
Tried the newly opened Yuen Kee Dumplings at the kopitiam food court. Be prepared for long queues and waiting time, we queued for 30 min just to order, followed by another 20 min for the food to be ready. This was around 7:30pm, and by the time we actually started eating, it was already 8:30pm.

The food was decent when served hot, but as it cooled, it became less enjoyable and started to feel quite jelat. Portions were huge and definitely filling. If you are a small eater, ordering just the dumplings without noodles would probably be more than enough.

I ordered a set ($10.80) which came with 6 dumplings, noodles, a drink, and a side.

Overall, it’s okay, but I personally wouldn’t queue and wait more than 20 mins for this.

[Old update]
Whole grilled fish from what the fish. Great for sharing between 2 pax! Fish was rather fresh and tender. Ordered the pickled savoury flavour — sour and tangy with slight spice.

It takes a while to find a seat here, so do be prepared to wait.

Damage: $21.40

Beverages stall staff very patience and friendly. Especially the cooking coffee Dajie, even my order is a bit trouble during the peak period. She still very patience and efficient😊. Appreciate for her patience!😊😊 22 Mar 26 (10:52).

Kopitiam@JP stands out for its variety of local food options, offering authentic flavors that cater to diverse tastes. From hearty noodle dishes to fragrant rice meals, western foods and it’s a heaven for lovers of Asian cuisine. Combined with the food hall’s elegant decorations, cleanliness, and kid-friendly amenities like high chairs, it’s a great spot for enjoying local favorites in a comfortable and hygienic setting.

2 🌟 - Below Average: Needs improvement in multiple areas, not meeting expectations.
3 🌟 - Standard, Average: Meets basic expectations, nothing exceptional but acceptable.
4 🌟 - Standard, Above Average: Exceeds expectations slightly, better than the norm.
