Windy coffeeshop. Was there to try the mookata buffet @1pm.  Nothing special and taste ordinary. Don't understand the hype.
Everyday buying Milo kosong thick and suddenly said cannot sale already. If want to increase the price just said . Don't said cannot sell . Bad staff.

Staff need to improve their processes especially if there are promotions going on.

Kopi peng tasted so-so. Saw the staff just pouring white liquid and black liquid. Didnt see any proper coffee being made at the coffee machine. Weird....

Staff unable to manage the crowd cause the collection area is so small, and many customers crowding around. Many cups on the collection table but so messy too. The oven machine is just beside the collection counter which looks very inconvenient for the staff who needs to heat up the pastries. Overall layout of the staff inside area seems very small and inconvenient.
Tried the new Kopi Peng today. Good coffee, nice and refreshing. Definitely worth a try!
Nice coffee with friendly baristas. Tried the Kopi peng and it tasted delicious!
Coffee with a real kick! If you love rich and flavorful coffee like myself, this is the perfect spot. So glad they finally have a branch open in the West now!

place_name: Mamma Mia Trattoria E Caffè (Jurong Point)
review_text:
Had the Milanese Chicken Chop at Mama Mia, Jurong Point. The chicken was fried to a nice golden-brown colour with a crisp coating that provided good texture. The meat inside was reasonably tender, and the lemon wedge added a welcome touch of acidity to cut through the richness of the fried crust.

The potato wedges were the strongest component of the dish—well-seasoned, crispy on the outside, and fluffy inside. The accompanying sauces worked well enough with the chicken, although they didn’t add much beyond the expected flavours.

Unfortunately, the fennel salad was underwhelming. It looked somewhat wilted and lacked the crisp freshness typically associated with a good fennel salad. The presentation felt rather rushed, and the salad didn’t provide the refreshing balance that the fried chicken needed.

Also the mushroom soup that came with the set meal was not even hot or like warm. But at room temperature. That ruins a good lunch to start off with.

Portion size was generous and filling, making it a satisfactory lunch. However, while the individual components were competent, the dish lacked the refinement and attention to detail that would make it memorable.

A decent, hearty meal that delivers on comfort food expectations, but there is room for improvement in the freshness and presentation of the accompanying salad. Overall, an average dining experience that earns a solid 3 out of 5 stars.

place_name: Mamma Mia Trattoria E Caffè (Jurong Point)
review_text: Had such a peaceful lunch date here with my husband today. The restaurant had a very calm and relaxing atmosphere, which made the experience even more enjoyable. The food was delicious, service was friendly, and we really appreciated how cozy and comfortable everything felt. Perfect place for a quiet meal and quality time together. Will definitely come back again!

place_name: Mamma Mia Trattoria E Caffè (Jurong Point)
review_text:
Honestly I was looking forward to try this place out due to recommendation from my partner. Seeing their pasta choices I decided to dine in and find out because the flavours looked promising!

Firstly not sure if it was an skill issue but not indicated properly on where to Q while we waited to be seated...Got 2 sides to enter from one near the cashier and the other further (saw people standing at both ends so I was unsure). Secondly unpleasant start because staff was not attentive as to my partner and I waiting...but hais issokay still can give chance abit

Thirdly I felt very unwelcome as soon as I sat down at the chosen table, staff just gave the menu and left without any greeting or good vibes

Fourth, I thought I would be okay already because the waiting time for our food to arrive like 10 minutes or less which wow I was impressed! But then I understood why food came out so fast, FOOD NOT EVEN HOT OR WARM!!! Noodles were like cold, sauce as well and the meat in the pastas... I don't mind waiting for like a reasonably time for our meals to be properly prepped but come on lah, serving cold food?? I could have made a comment on it to the waitress but hungry already T^T. The turkey strip bits ah why so hard?? Like hard rock sia (on-going issue based off other reviews from past patrons) , unfortunate that I didn't raise concerns to the waitress, that one my issue lol

Fifth thing was why was it so hard to get attention of waitress? The time we dined in was barely peak hour, I fully understand if like tired or lack of man power but come on lah at least still have staff give their best and stay attentive...Raise hand for awhile like what, we in pre-school?

Dessert: Was meh, $5 for that size and taste not worth, save that money and buy somewhere else

TOTAL SPENT: $51...

TLDR: I will never patronise this outlet ever again. With the lack of good customer service and below average food. Honestly only yummy thing was the mushroom soup at least hot and got taste lor.

place_name: Mamma Mia Trattoria E Caffè (Jurong Point)
review_text:
Dined in for dinner on Mother's Day(10/05/2026) and ordered the following dishes: Pan Roasted Ribeye, Braised Beef Oxtail Cheese Baked Rice and Crispy Milanese Chicken with Truffle Cream Pasta at 16:49:35hrs.

Verdict: The Braised Beef Oxtail Cheese Baked Rice and Crispy Milanese Chicken with Truffle Cream Pasta were served to us correctly. The braised beef oxtail was tender but also consisted of slightly fatty parts within the lean meat while the cheese baked rice was hot, creamy and cheese pulls were observed.

At the same time, the crispy milanese chicken was unfortunately not as crispy as it should've been due to the truffle cream pasta being placed onto the top of the crispy chicken cutlet. However, the mushrooms were juicy and tender while the pasta was cooked al dente.

We indicated in our order for the steak doneness level to be "Well Done" but we received the steak doneness level as "Medium" instead(please refer to the images attached in this review). Due to steak doneness level being incorrect, my family had to order another main(which was the Creamy Seafood Cheese Baked Rice) as my mother was unable to finish her meal.

After the Creamy Seafood Cheese Baked Rice was served, we realized that the "broccoli" component that was listed as one of the ingredients inside the menu item was missing. We inquired with the service staff who proceeded to check with the chef and we were informed that only seafood were supposed to be in the dish. My family felt misled by the inaccurate and incorrect information indicated in the QR ordering system. Additionally, there were only clams and 2 tiger prawns inside this "Creamy Seafood Cheese Baked Rice" dish.

Overall, this was the first time that my family patronized this outlet and it was a disappointment for the amount that we paid for the meal. Please kindly send your chef for retraining if he/she is unable to cook a "Well Done" steak doneness level accurately.

place_name: Mamma Mia Trattoria E Caffè (Jurong Point)
review_text:
Was nearby and decided to come over to Jurong point for dinner and decided to visit mamma mia as my child is craving for spaghetti.

The menu is not overly extensive and has the various mainstays as well as some of their unique items.

I had the clam pasta which was fresh and al dente, the taste was good and savory (umami) that you can only get from fresh seafood.

The tomato base meatball pasta was nice as well with 4 big beef meatballs, nicely cooked and fresh as well.

The honey wings was delicious and even more delicious when coupled with their dipping sauce.

I also had iced lemon tea and tiramisu as well, both were home made quality and freshness.
The mushroom soup and minestrone soup are delicious as well, although the soups could have been a bit warmer or hotter.

The staff are friendly and service was efficient, most main courses are ranged between $15 to $25, which for the quality that they are offering is considered worthwhile.

The store is wide open store front, so it can get quite noisy during peak hours, but then again the ventilation is good lol.

A very old-school mall with an open-air concept, literally filled with small shopfronts facing the front only. The building is three stories high, but the elevator on the third floor has not been working for months, with repairs seemingly still in progress. This suggests either a lack of budget or poor management care.

Only the Giant supermarket and McDonald’s have enclosed, air-conditioned spaces. There is also a Koufu eatery, but that’s about it. Nothing fancy and nothing really worth visiting unless you are here for something essential. Otherwise, it feels like a dead mall in an ulu location. 🏬😐

I’m writing this to warn everyone about a frustrating experience I just had at this shop located at pioneer mall. Please be careful before you buy anything here!

The promoter at the shop  push an item that didn't actually meet my needs. Even though I was hesitant, they explicitly promised me "don't worry, you can always get a full refund if it doesn’t work out."

When I tried to return the item, the story completely changed.

Other than a well stocked Giant Supermarket on L3 and 4, this mall offers nothing else. It has a McD on the Ground Floor and Anytime Fitness on L3.

Guess it fulfills the needs of Jurong West residents but definitely not a destination.
It's okay with a variety of food and drinks. It's non-aircon, very airy and with nice fan to cool patrons there. I like that it's nicely spaced and feels roomy when I'm there.

There is a Macdonald ,which is 24hrs, at ground floor with a macafé. A cake shop,kofu,seven eleven too and also a lot of shops in 1st and 2nd floor. There is a giant supermarket at 3rd floor which is opened 8am to 11 pm. There is a lift and escalator service with a public toilet and a Atm too..There is still more to discover 😁

Came to buy chips jn. After taking my chips, I had to squeeze through to get to the counter since the place was so narrow. There was a lady who was still taking her stuff from the shelf, so I had to squeeze in front to queue for payment, since I didn't want to hit the lady with my extremely heavy and bulky bag. There was this blonde hair casher (looks about 60-65 years old), she saw me and decided to yank me backwards. After the lady told her I wasn't cutting cus I already had my item while she didnt, the cashier just laughed.. No apology, nothing. I get that it looks like I'm cutting, but even so, she should've asked if I was cutting queue before assuming I was and trying to forcefully pull me back. The store was also crowded for such a tiny place, so if I fell backwards, it could also hurt other people..

I have been having PT sessions with Darren for around a year and I have seen how my body has changed and grown stronger over this period. He is always pushing me to do better, whilst ensuring that I do not hurt myself in the process. He emphasizes that my form should never be compromised, even when I’m tired and even when he needs to support the weight. I am more confident in the gym and more confident when I go about with my other physical activities like rock climbing now. Would definitely recommend him as a PT!

I started training with Darren in June last year, and it has been an excellent experience. From the beginning, Darren took the time to understand my fitness goals and tailored every session to suit my needs. His training plans were effective, practical and helped me make consistent progress.

What stands out most is his dedication and encouragement. Even on days when I felt like giving up, Darren always pushed and motivated me to keep going. He regularly checks in on my goals and adjusts the training accordingly, ensuring that every session is aligned with what I want to achieve.

Darren is knowledgeable, patient and genuinely cares about helping his clients succeed. I would highly recommend him to anyone looking for a personal trainer who is committed to helping you reach your fitness goals!

Absolutely appalling experience with this branch. I was charged a $14 late payment fee even after clearly explaining that my card had been compromised due to a scam, and I had only just received my replacement card. This wasn’t a case of negligence or irresponsibility on my part—it was an unfortunate situation completely out of my control.

What’s worse is the complete lack of empathy and flexibility from the staff. Despite my explanation and polite request for a waiver, they refused to budge. Instead of showing any understanding, they hid behind the terms and conditions I signed at the start, quoting them as if that somehow justifies a complete absence of basic customer care.

It’s incredibly disappointing that a business would prioritize a $14 fee over maintaining goodwill with a paying member. Situations like these are exactly where customer service should step up—but clearly, that’s not the case here.

Honestly, you’re better off going to the Nanyang branch instead, where the service is far more reasonable and customer-oriented. This experience has completely turned me off from this outlet.

Would not recommend if you expect even a shred of understanding or reasonable service.

review_text:
Our hearts are truly overflowing with gratitude. For the past six years, KidsCampus Jurong West has been more than just a preschool. It has been a second home to my children.
To every teacher who has walked alongside them, thank you for caring with such genuine empathy and unwavering passion. Your ability to understand their feelings, comfort them on tough days and celebrate their little victories means more to us than words can express.
Your passion for teaching shines through in every moment such as your creative lessons, your joyful energy and the way you inspire the children to explore, imagine and believe in themselves. Because of all of you, our kids run into school with bright smiles each day, knowing they are safe, loved and truly understood.
Under your nurturing and heartfelt guidance, they have grown, thrived and blossomed in ways we could only hope for. We will forever be grateful for your compassion, your dedication and the incredible impact you have had on their early years.
Thank you, KidsCampus Jurong West, for being such a beautiful part of our children’s journey. ❤️

review_text:

The university contains a huge campus and the campus is amazing.
One of the most iconic architecture building by Thomas Heatherwick in Singapore! The texture used and the orientation of the organic building were impressive

Smart Campus Experience: Safety & Tech Done Right!

"This isn't just a campus - it's a tech-powered campuss. I was blown away by the thousands of CCTV cameras (yes, you read that right!) with over 1,000  analytics cameras constantly safeguarding students. Forget old-school security - this is next-level protection!

Parking is frictionless genius: barrier-free entry, EV charging stations, and the game-changing Go Parkin app.

Register your vehicle once, and the automatic plate scanners handle everything - no tickets, no cash, just drive in/out while the app bills you seamlessly.

Between the Instagram-worthy landmarks (seriously, bring your camera!) and seeing how every detail prioritizes safety and convenience...

If you want peace of mind with 21st-century techno education, then this campus sets the gold standard.

Nanyang Technological University (NTU), sprawling across its lush, eco-friendly sanctuary at 50 Nanyang Avenue, is a breathtaking masterclass in how a modern research institution can harmonize high-octane innovation with environmental stewardship in 2026. From my deeply insightful exchanges with pioneering materials scientists and AI developers who call this campus home, it is impossible not to be captivated by NTU’s evolution into a high-fidelity, merytorical powerhouse of global renown. My colleagues frequently marvel at the pedagogical electricity within "The Hive" and "The Arc," describing a rare environment where scholars are empowered to master complex sustainable energy or aerospace systems through a uniquely collaborative, cross-disciplinary lens. Public sentiment remains exceptionally high, positioning the university not just as a technical school, but as a visionary architect of a talent pipeline that is currently dominating the fields of green chemistry, robotics, and digital humanities. Reflecting on these glowing, authentic testimonials from peers who have revolutionized global supply chains, I perceive this institution as a distinguished epicenter of human progress that executes its mission with a level of scientific audacity and garden-city grace that is truly refreshing. I am particularly fascinated by the university’s "Smart Campus" initiative, which my associates describe as a living laboratory where the future of urban mobility and carbon neutrality is being forged in real-time. The institutional culture is consistently praised for its inclusive meritocracy, treating every aspiring researcher as a vital partner in the quest for global solutions. It is truly heartening to hear from those "in the know" that the university’s commitment to "NTU 2025/2026" strategic goals has created a resilient, future-ready workforce that remains peerless in the international labor market. Many of my professional contacts highlight that the university’s robust industry partnerships provide a distinct competitive edge, ensuring that theoretical breakthroughs are rapidly translated into tangible societal benefits. I personally admire the way NTU maintains a respectful dialogue with its global counterparts while fearlessly pushing the boundaries of what is possible in the Fourth Industrial Revolution. Every conversation I engage in regarding this institution reinforces the belief that it is an extraordinary engine of both intellectual and economic transformation. The school’s focus on the "Triple Helix" of academia, industry, and government is, in my view, the definitive blueprint for 21st-century educational success. I find it remarkable how the campus provides such a serene, professional backdrop that simultaneously inspires deep focus and radical creativity. Those who have navigated its rigorous, world-class curricula describe it as a transformative journey where youthful curiosity is meticulously refined into elite professional expertise. Ultimately, aligning one’s academic trajectory with Nanyang Technological University is regarded as a strategic masterstroke for any ambitious individual determined to lead the global technological transition while remaining part of a supportive, high-caliber intellectual family. This institution stands as a brilliant testament to Singapore’s unwavering dedication to the highest calibers of scientific and humanistic excellence.

Pioneer MRT Station (EW28) is an elevated station on the East-West Line (EWL). It is a key transport node for residents of Jurong West and a vital gateway for students and staff of Nanyang Technological University (NTU).
📍 Location & Layout
* Address: 31 Jurong West Street 63, Singapore 648310.
* Structure: It is an elevated station with an island platform (trains stop on both sides).
* Unique Feature: It is one of the few above-ground stations equipped with privacy screens on the trackside to protect the privacy of residents in the nearby HDB blocks.
🚶 Exits & Connectivity
The station has two main exits, both leading to Jurong West Street 63:
| Exit | Destination | Notable Nearby Landmarks |
|---|---|---|
| A | Jurong West St 63 (North) | Jurong West Primary School, Blk 651-654 |
| B | Jurong West St 63 (South) | Jurong West Stadium & Sports Centre, Pioneer Mall |
🚌 Bus Services & NTU Shuttle
* NTU Shuttle: The station is the primary pick-up point for the NTU Campus Rider (CR), which transports students directly to the university.
* Public Buses: Key services include 179 (to NTU/Boon Lay), 241 (to Boon Lay/Jurong West St 91), and 192 (to Tuas).
🏢 Nearby Amenities
* Pioneer Mall: A community mall featuring a Giant Supermarket, Koufu food court, and various retail shops (approx. 8–10 mins walk).
* Jurong West Sports Centre: Includes a stadium, swimming complex, and gym.
* Jurong West Primary School: Located right next to Exit A.
* Jurong Point: Just one stop away (Boon Lay MRT), which is one of the largest suburban malls in Singapore.
🕒 First & Last Train Timings
* Towards Pasir Ris: ~5:32 AM (Mon–Sat) | ~6:02 AM (Sun/PH)
* Towards Tuas Link: ~5:23 AM (Mon–Sat) | ~5:53 AM (Sun/PH)
> Note: If you are heading to the Pioneer Centre (industrial building) we discussed earlier, it is about a 15-minute walk or a short bus ride from this station.

🚇 When you exit Pioneer MRT, you'll see a 7-Eleven right away. There's also a POSB ATM nearby.

🍜 There are various food shops around. Go downstairs and you'll find Kimly Coffeeshop (no aircon).

I am a very fat man who likes to eat a lot of food. Recently, I've decided to stop my unhealthy lifestyle and go on a diet. However, when I went out today, I couldn't resist my temptation to take a sip of my herbal tea, OUTSIDE THE MRT STATION, IN FRONT OF AN EATERY CALLED MAKAN QUEEN.

Fortunately, the SMRT staff came to the rescue. As a kind women with strong sense of justice and the heart to help people in need, she went all the way from her working post inside the station, walked outside of the station, to outside makan queen, where i was standing and drinking my herbal tea, and told me no drinking is allowed here or I would be fined. For the first time in my life in Singapore, I've heard that you cannot drink OUTSIDE MRT STATION, in fromt of an eatery. I know that the kind hearted SMRT staff said these words so that I won't break my diet. Thank You So Much SMRT, I didn't break my diet and on track to lose more weight! Compliment to the kind-hearted SMRT staff!

The atmosphere is also very positive and welcoming. Staff are friendly and helpful, and the overall environment makes workouts enjoyable and motivating.

I’ve personally had a very good experience here. The gym environment is energetic, motivating, and always gives a positive vibe. The staff are friendly, helpful, and professional, which makes every visit comfortable and enjoyable.
The facilities are clean, equipment is well maintained, and the overall atmosphere really makes me look forward to coming back. The community here is also great and very welcoming.

been a tenant at this dormitory for a few years now and I think if the management is as bad as what the reviews wrote, I don’t think my company will continue renewing the contract with them.

There are definitely area for improvement for the team and the management but some of the reviews are super one sided. I’ve not gotten any “bad treatment” or rather rude treatment from any of their staffs. When there’s an issue, it’s always solved in an amicable way.

We have had a very positive experience with Kian Teck Dormitory and would highly recommend it to other employers. The management team is extremely professional and always gives clear instructions during contracts and administrative processes, which makes everything smooth and transparent. Their communication is straightforward and supportive, which really helps both employers and workers feel assured.

One of the biggest strengths of this dormitory is their excellent hygiene standards. The place is always well-maintained, clean, and regularly checked, which gives our workers a safe and comfortable environment to rest after a long day at work. This attention to detail in cleanliness is something we truly appreciate.

What stands out the most is how much our workers enjoy staying here. Even when we suggested moving them to a dormitory closer to the jobsite, they preferred to stay at Kian Teck because of the comfortable living environment and the way the dormitory is managed. A major highlight is that the dorms come with en-suite kitchens and toilets, which our workers appreciate far more than the usual shared communal facilities. Having their own cooking and sanitation spaces makes them feel more at home and gives them greater comfort and privacy.

Special mention must go to the Dorm Manager, who has been outstanding. He is approachable, responsive, and always willing to go the extra mile to resolve issues quickly and effectively. His dedication to both the workers’ welfare and employers’ needs really sets Kian Teck apart from many other dormitories.

Overall, I would confidently say that Kian Teck Dormitory is one of the top dormitories in the West. From management to cleanliness, to facilities, to worker satisfaction—everything is handled with excellence. We are grateful for their consistent efforts and highly recommend them to any company looking for a reliable and well-run accommodation solution.

AVOID!!! If you're considering this dormitory for your workers, I strongly urge you to look elsewhere. The management and security staff are CONSISTENTLY rude, unhelpful, and inflexible. Any attempts to clarify procedures are met with hostility—as though you owe them something, despite the high rental fees you’re paying.

From the beginning, the contract process was riddled with mistakes made by staff. Despite being patient and respectful, I was never shown the same courtesy. Their instructions are unclear, responses are cold, and processes are slow, manual, and outdated. There are no exceptions even in emergencies—you have to wait until office hours to speak to someone, regardless of urgency.

Living conditions are unacceptable. You are expected to provide your own fridge, fans, and washing machine for your workers. What they offer is a bare metal bed frame (no mattress) and a rusty, dirty locker—nothing more. The overall condition of the facilities is old, poorly maintained, and far below reasonable standards.

When it's time to move out, you’ll be charged for any "damages," regardless of whether you caused them. Wear-and-tear items like toilet doors, lockers, or fittings are treated as if they're brand new and you're held liable for their full replacement cost. These charges are pushed onto workers or companies without any room for discussion.

There is a serious lack of respect, professionalism, and basic human decency shown to tenants. Every interaction feels confrontational. It’s draining and demoralising.

And now we understand why the tenancy is locked in for 3 years—because if people could leave, they would.

If I could give zero stars, I would.

Please do not house your workers here. Look for a better-managed and more HUMANE alternative.

I have had a very positive experience with Kian Teck Dormitory and would like to share my perspective after seeing some negative reviews.

From my dealings with the management and admin team, I have always received clear instructions, especially regarding check-in and check-out through their online portal. The process is straightforward, and whenever I had questions, the staff were helpful and willing to guide me.

One thing I really appreciate is the effort they put into maintaining cleanliness and discipline. The dormitory has a demerit point system to encourage residents to keep their rooms tidy. In fact, I was once fined for untidiness, but only after repeated warnings, something I found fair and consistent. This system ensures a clean and healthy living environment for everyone, which is not something I experienced in my previous dormitory.

The management also conducts hygiene inspections at least once or twice every month for every unit. This shows that they are serious about providing a safe and well-maintained place for workers to live in.

On a personal level, the dormitory manager has always been approachable, open to discussions, and kind in handling any concerns. I have never encountered the rudeness or inflexibility that some reviews mention.

Overall, I find Kian Teck Dormitory to be one of the more disciplined, well-organized, and well-maintained dormitories. My experience has been positive, and I truly believe the management makes an effort to ensure good living standards for all residents.

If I could give zero stars, I would. Please avoid this dormitory. The management is consistently rude, unhelpful, and inflexible, as many other reviewers have also highlighted.
Poor Communication: When seeking clarification, management provides unclear instructions, and any attempt to follow up is met with disdain. Basic courtesy and professionalism are sorely lacking.
Contract Issues: Our initial contract contained numerous errors, and even after amendments, mistakes persisted—without any acknowledgment or apology.
Unreasonable Expectations: Upon moving out, tenants are expected to restore the premises to “perfect” condition, including repainting, regardless of normal wear and tear.
Rigid Security Staff: Security personnel, like the management, are inflexible and unsympathetic.
The system that they use for registration of residents is also very archaic.
Overall, this dormitory demonstrates poor management practices, lack of respect for tenants, and unreasonable policies. Strongly recommend looking elsewhere for accommodation.
Many birds and nice places to explore  nature. About 2km+ with proper walking path. Must bring insects repellent, many many mosquitos
Kranji gate is better reached by cycling or driving as roads do not have sidewalks

Super quaint and ulu place where most of the visitors are bird watchers (run into a few during the visit). Quite hard to reach because closest bus stops (let alone train stations) are kilometers away. Walking to the location is also hard since the road has no pavement. The upside is that it's free of charge and the observation tower at the end of the trail gives a nice vantage point over the bird reserve and Kranji Reservoir nearby. Maintenance works could've been better since some of the sheds seem to be quite run down.
Very quiet place with few visitors. Smell of animal poo, probably some animal farm nearby.

Kranji Marshes offers a unique escape into nature, but it's a mixed bag in terms of maintenance and experience. Located in Singapore, the marshes are a good option for those looking to immerse themselves in a quieter, more natural environment away from the hustle and bustle of the city.

The walking trail is decent, making for a pleasant trek, and I found that a 30-minute walk provided an opportunity to appreciate the surrounding flora and fauna. However, the lack of upkeep was evident. Some paths were overgrown, and the area felt somewhat neglected, which detracted from the overall experience.

One significant downside was the mosquito situation. I ended up with 18 bites during my visit, so I recommend bringing insect repellent or wear long sleeves and pants if you decide to go. The marshes can feel quite deserted, which might appeal to some seeking solitude, but it could also be a bit unsettling for others.

Overall, Kranji Marshes has potential for a refreshing nature walk, but be prepared for a few inconveniences along the way. If you are willing to overlook the maintenance issues and the pesky mosquitoes, it can be a worthwhile retreat into Singapore's natural landscape.

Love this newly renovated Jurong Eco-Garden Park. The greenery is lush and well-maintained, with a beautiful man-made lake that adds to the peaceful atmosphere. The best time to visit is early morning or late evening. Do remember to bring sunscreen and mosquito repellent.

Be cautious of wild boars in the area, and watch your step on rocky or gravel paths, as they can be slippery or uneven. There are also some fallen branches along shaded areas that may be easy to miss, so take care while walking.

The lake is a great spot for wedding or photography shoots. You might even spot a giant turtle—likely a Southern River Terrapin—alongside common terrapins. It’s huge and almost looks like a monster! Tilapia are abundant here, and kids will enjoy feeding the fish (bring some bread).

Do note that fishing is not allowed. The toilets are clean, and there’s a water filter station available along the walking path. Overall, a lovely place to relax and enjoy nature.

Very nice garden/park! Good for casual running. Has fitness equipment and playgrounds. Has a water cooler. There are toilets and an additional toilet for individuals with limited accessibility. Lots of parking lots as well. Best timings to come are in the morning around 7-9am and in the evening 6.30-7.30pm.

A park just opposite my house, via a bridge over PIE. Quiet as little people, you can hear the birds chipping as you walk along. Playground for children, toilet available. Visit the pottery and saw lovey chickens. There are pottery for sale like huge vases, plates, cups, etc. You can walk to the campus for food, not sure if open on weekend.

It’s a very new park with lots of eco features. I’ll say that it’s actually an exhibition of water technologies used by the semi-public companies in the area and what you’re actually seeing is the experimental site where they’re showcasing the results and testing their ideas irl. Quite cool actually. BUT ALOT OF MOSQUITOES THAT WILL BITE AND STING YOU. I had itchy spots everywhere. (minus one star sorry - bugs can coexist with us but in harmony please! why the Wolbachia not working issit)

Finally the garden is open to public, nice garden for a run or cycling. There is a playground / fitness corner / toilet / drinking water and a big pond. The place is of average size. Nice little place to chill after my run.

Cool garden within Japanese Garden. You can find many species of plant here that is growing on the wall. A little like Balinese style garden. There is a small dessert kiosk over here for you to grab cold drink as well.

A beautifully designed and tranquil space that feels like stepping into a hidden rainforest. The sunken layout, moss-covered walls, and lush vertical greenery create a cool, calming microclimate that’s perfect for slowing down and soaking in nature. The circular opening above and the central water feature make it especially atmospheric, almost meditative

Well maintained, thoughtfully planted, and wonderfully quiet compared to the main paths — this is a great spot to pause, reflect, and appreciate the diversity of plants. Highly recommended if you enjoy serene garden spaces and unique landscape design

Quiet spot. Interesting sort of Latin American vibe for the cenote concept. Has some charm in it for sure.

Not sure if too much resources or too little resources were awarded to bring this concept to life. Or perhaps it'll take some time to complete and I'm prejudging.

Could we stop with the 'hidden 💎 gems' already 😂 😂 😂
Quiet little spot with greenery and space to relax. Not a major attraction, but good if you want some peaceful photos away from the usual crowds.
June 2026: great photo spot. Has a hidden cafe in the area.

​The park's main activities are conveniently clustered around McDonald's, which serves as a great central spot for dining, socializing, and recreation. Aside from active play, it is a fantastic place for gatherings, featuring camping grounds, barbecue pits, large open lawns for picnics, kite flying and jogging.

West Coast Park is a long but relatively quiet park that stretches along Singapore’s western coastline. It’s a wonderful place for a relaxed outdoor day, especially for families with kids

One of the highlights is the large playground, which is one of the better ones in Singapore. The climbing structures, slides, and play areas keep children entertained for a long time, and there’s plenty of open space nearby for running around

The park is also great for cycling and casual walks. You can easily rent bicycles or family pedal carts near the entrance and ride along the park connectors while enjoying the sea breeze

For those who enjoy spending more time outdoors, the park has BBQ pits and camping areas, making it perfect for weekend gatherings with friends and family. There are also scenic spots along the waterfront where people fish or simply relax and watch the ships in the distance

I do like to come to this nature park by the sea - to take in the salty air and the cool sea breeze, both of which relaxes and then invigorates me. I like to hear the waves crashing against the shore and to be among the greenery in this nature park - in particular, the bamboo fringed walkway as regards the latter. The amenities at this nature park is adequate - with enough car parking spaces and public toilets. And overall, this nature park is well-maintained.
Well maintained park and a good place to spend time alone or with family and friends. Suitable for all ages, whether to exercise in the mornings or catch a lovely sunset in the evenings.

West Coast Park is considerably smaller than East Coast Park (ECP), but it remains a highly viable zone for R&R. I have scouted the perimeter and identified several optimal coordinates with good sightlines to hold position and decompress.

Civilian population density in this grid is minimal. The decreased foot traffic results in a secure perimeter and a highly tranquil atmosphere—ideal for lowering your guard and recovering stamina.

Be advised on two minor variables:

First, visuals on the maritime border are compromised and do not match the aesthetics of ECP.

Second, heavy transport vessels are active in the adjacent waters. My MJOLNIR armor’s environmental sensors occasionally detected industrial exhaust drifting into the area. It is non-lethal, but noticeable without a helmet filtration system.

Overall assessment: A solid, quiet sector for downtime. Proceed to coordinates at your discretion.

🌱 If you’re looking to enroll your child in a faith-based preschool, you’re definitely at the right place with Little Seeds Preschool (St Andrew's). I first discovered this school while searching for preschools in the Jurong West area. After a quick call, I was able to arrange a site visit on the very same day—and I was so impressed that I enrolled my child in the playgroup class just a week later.

Fast forward two years, my son is now 4 years old and thriving here. The teachers and staff have shown him so much love, care, and patience that he looks forward to school every day. We recently moved to a new home, and even though a preschool opened right below our apartment, my child insisted on continuing at Little Seeds Preschool (St Andrew's). His reason was simple yet profound: “I can feel the love from all the teachers and staff.”

That, to me, says everything about the environment this preschool has created. It’s not just about academics—it’s about nurturing children with warmth, values, and genuine care. I’m grateful for the wonderful foundation Little Seeds Preschool (St Andrew's) has given my child, and I would wholeheartedly recommend it to any parent seeking a faith-based preschool where children truly feel at home.

My child enjoys going to school a lot. The teachers here are very responsible and caring. The curriculum is comprehensive and engaging, suited to the respective age group. They have water play exposure once a week.

When my child was 19mo & just started school, she had trouble falling asleep on her own in school as we always rocked her to sleep. Her teacher (Aunty Elvina) was very patient & skilful as she managed to train her to fall asleep without rocking her to sleep. We are very thankful for this as even at home it’s not easy for us to get her to nap. Kudos to her PG teacher!

My son started attending this childcare centre when he was 18 months old, and now it’s been almost three years of watching him grow in a truly nurturing environment. I’m genuinely grateful for the care, love, and patience shown by all the teachers (who are called as Aunties and Uncles). My child often tells us how much he enjoys being with them and how excited he is about the school activities.
The admin team keeps parents well-updated on what’s happening in school and responds actively to concerns and feedback. The communication is clear, timely, and always centred on what’s best for the children.
What I appreciate most is how closely the teachers work with parents. They’re attentive to each child’s needs and collaborate with us to support his development both in school and at home.
Thanks to Little Seeds St Andrew’s for such a supportive and caring school community for my child.

Little Seeds st andrews have very amazing teachers from PG all the way up to K2. I initially chose little seeds for their location, but later on, their routine and caring environment convinced me. From the daily outdoors making use of the futsal court, open space, farmers plot, playground, and the occasional walking distance visits to the nearby wet market, they also have weekly water and sand play for the kids to have free and imaginative play. Indoor curriculum are equally comprehensive with clear routines, structured classes and healthy discipline from the teachers who are genuine, caring and very capable.

We were looking for a preschool in the Jurong West/Boon Lay area, and Little Seeds really stood out. The classrooms are bright, open, and inviting, and the teachers, principal, and staff are all warm, friendly, and respectful. The teachers are open to feedback and work closely with parents to support each child’s growth and navigate any challenges together.

Although we’re free thinkers, we’ve felt comfortable and welcomed from the start. My child began in the Playgroup and has since progressed to Pre-Nursery. I’m really glad we chose to enrol here—it’s been a nurturing and supportive environment for my child.

place_name: Raffles Kidz @ Jurong West (Yuan Ching) - Best Childcare and Preschool Singapore
review_text: Our child transferred from China to K2 at Raffles Kidz Pres. Thanks to all teachers’ patient care, especially Chinese teacher Wen, he adapted fast and enjoys school life now. We are satisfied with the school’s warm environment and teaching.

place_name: Raffles Kidz @ Jurong West (Yuan Ching) - Best Childcare and Preschool Singapore
review_text:
Our son has been in school for 1.5yrs since he was 18mo. During transition from PN to Nursery was when we were given feedback on his development, and at which point we considered changing schools for something that was more suitable for him. Principal Gwen stepped in and offered a change in class and teachers and we agreed to see if this allowed our son to enjoy school and learn better. We are so glad we were encouraged to this step because fast forward 6m later, our son has shown so much growth in social, emotional, physical & cognitive development. We must especially commend Teacher Arinah and Xing Lao Shi, his form teachers, to whom both have been very dedicated in caring and guiding our son along the way. Particularly, there was more feedback provided to us either by phone calls or after school drop off chats — that helped us to also support the teachers work in helping our son’s progress. Our son has since been much happier in going and talking about school.

We would also like to thank Teacher Adillah, Zann and Anna who also made school so much more enjoyable with their warmth and investment to really engaging (chatting) with our boy!

place_name: Raffles Kidz @ Jurong West (Yuan Ching) - Best Childcare and Preschool Singapore
review_text: The teachers are always so welcoming during drop-off and pick-up which gives parents great peace of mind. We transferred our son here and it has definitely been the right choice. The teachers have been patient with him especially during meal times and we’ve seen great progress that he is now toilet trained. Lessons are also well rounded and engaging. Shoutout to Teacher Zann and Xing 老师 for their dedication and care.

place_name: Raffles Kidz @ Jurong West (Yuan Ching) - Best Childcare and Preschool Singapore
review_text:
Raffles Kidz has been an amazing preschool experience for our daughter. From the very first day, the teachers made her feel incredibly warm, welcomed, and safe. You can really feel their dedication — they take the time to understand each child’s personality, and they create an environment where the kids are excited to learn and explore.
Our daughter looks forward to school every morning, and we’ve seen her grow so much in confidence, language, and social skills. The curriculum is engaging, the activities are thoughtfully planned, and the entire team communicates well with parents.
We’re truly grateful to the teachers and staff at Raffles Kidz for giving our little one such a happy and nurturing start to her learning journey. Highly recommended for any parent looking for a caring and enriching preschool!

place_name: Raffles Kidz @ Jurong West (Yuan Ching) - Best Childcare and Preschool Singapore
review_text:
My kids have attended Raffles Kidz since Pre-Nursery and I can say with confidence, “No regrets at all!”

The school compound is spacious, neat, safe and definitely a fun learning environment for the kids to be in. The curriculum is enriching and holistic with plenty of engaging activities that supports both academic and social development. Stars of the centre have to be the teachers and principal! They are patient, kind and encouraging towards the students.

Special mention to Tr Triff, 牛老师, Tr Rosz and 张老师!

Tr Triff and Niu Laoshi have played a significant role in my boy’s early education journey, building a strong foundation for my son as he graduates from K2 this year. I’m truly grateful for their commitment in nurturing my boy, helping him reach his full potential and preparing him well for primary school.

I also deeply appreciate Zhang Laoshi and Tr Rosz for their unwavering dedication and effort towards nurturing my daughter who is currently in N2. With their warmth, patience and guidance, my girl is able to learn and grow in a positive environment, and she has showed tremendous improvement in all aspects.

I definitely highly recommend RKI! For parents looking for a good pre-school, Raffles Kidz is your top choice.

place_name: Blessed Seeds Student Care @ Jurong West
review_text:
I loved the environment of Blessed Seeds. They have a huge space yet with lots of cozy corners. Teachers are friendly and caring.
My daughter shared that a teacher accidentally pressed on her elbow while playing tag together. I was very impressed as usually, teachers do not join in the games with children in other schools. Rather they will stay at the sides to supervise. Thank you for giving my daughter such an engaging experience.

place_name: Blessed Seeds Student Care @ Jurong West
review_text: I'm happy to share that my child Sarah Kate had a very pleasant experience during her stay with Blessed Seeds Student Care (Jurong West). It did help my child to grow personally and improved her in socializing with fellow students. Thank you so much for the love, care and the teachings shared during my child's stay with the student care. Do keep up the good works and may God bless you all abundantly in His grace.

place_name: Blessed Seeds Student Care @ Jurong West
review_text: Blessed Seeds is truly a gem with their team of lovely teachers. What I really appreciate is that the children get 2 to 3 sessions of outdoor play each week within the building. This is something quite unique, as not many student care centres provide such frequent outdoor or sports time. While many other centres focus on enrichment classes at extra cost, I value that Blessed Seeds prioritises play and character building. After finishing homework, I prefer my children to enjoy playing ball games and running around — and Blessed Seeds gives them just that.

place_name: Blessed Seeds Student Care @ Jurong West
review_text:
My son had been with BS since the start of this year. He really enjoys going to student care especially he has many friends there. Of coz the teachers as well. My son is always looking forward to play football with the friends in BS.
I would like to say the teachers in BS are really kind, patient and encouaging. What i love about BS is that u can feel the warmth, the love the teachers have for the children.
Lastly, my sin also enjoys the home cook food by the auntie in the centre.

I highly recommend BS if you are looking for one.

place_name: Blessed Seeds Student Care @ Jurong West
review_text: Did a transferring for my son into Blessed Seeds SC. My son loves the way the SC having time for various activities. Even during school holidays, optional programs were rolled out to broaden up the students exposure. Alvin and his team will also provide updates on the student. 👍🏿

The institute looks grand and well maintained, being the training grounds for the senior leaderships of the SAF. Buildings have a rustic and spacious feel, but are showing signs of deterioration. Exciting place to be for first timer, not so much for regular military personnel and conscripted NSFs.

was invited by SAFTI staffs and attended the graduation ceremony last week. seeing how huge the camp inclusive its facilities building and etc, the cleanliness and organised, it reminded myself when i was in my uni's years. nonetheless, grateful for the opportunity given and insyaAllah looking forward for other event in near future.

thank you to Mr Lee for the invitation and Mr Lim for the warm welcome. appreciate it🌸

I was at SAFTMI pass office this morning to obtain a parking label to park in premises for meeting.  Encountered the most unpleasant and insolent MP 3SG Collin and his 3WO FD Lim. I mistook the glass window counter to be the place to change pass. When I finally found the right place which was to enter a dark glass door, I was shouted at by 3SG Collin. 3WO F D Lim was called in but was unhelpful by using jargons like "white listing", expecting a civilian to comprehend what it means! Overall, the behaviour of the military police were uncalled for and unprofessional and they acted like thugs, bringing down the image of SAFTIMI.

A grand place befitting the status of the school. Car park for those picking up or alighting the cadets is via an entrance to the side of the main and grand entrance. The wings are located somewhat far away from the drop off zone so best to have at least 1 hr buffer time.

Things were not well planned for family day. Shuttle bus took forever to start and crowd at waiting point had to wait with no directions as there were no signboards or labels for the meeting point. OCS organizes family day once every 3 months and still can't get it right?
Invitation form for the family clear states of attire restriction but no one enforces on the rules. Why put it down in then? Disappointment in the management. Can you do better?

Mdm Han is a teacher who cares about her students’ academic performance and wellbeing. In lessons, she provides us with worksheets that sharpens our comprehension skills and newspaper articles that help us with our paper 1. She is also a very enthusiastic person and keeps lessons fun! With her help, I managed to improve from a C6 to an A2 (in English). Thank you Mdm Han ❤️

This education centre is a hidden gem! My son was struggling with his Secondary School studies and he had to take his O-level examination last year. Given less than a year with Achievers Journey, he passed his English, securing his sweet spot in his desired course through EAE in Poly.
Ms Han was firmed with her students. She was clear in her teaching and all thanks to her, she helped to eyeball and handheld my son quite a bit, in order to keep him laser-focused on his goal.
With such dedicated teacher, my son knows he cannot pull a fast one with Ms Han. This minimises slipshod work and indirectly, I can see the improvement in both my son's learning attitude and result.
Good and sincere tutor is hard to come by and I strongly recommend this centre to any student who needs guidance in their studies.

I’ve been attending Achievers Journey for English tuition, and I have to say, it has been a wonderful experience! Mdm Han, our teacher, is absolutely fantastic. She has a deep understanding of the subject and explains things in a way that’s easy to grasp, even the more complex concepts. What I really appreciate is how patient she is, always taking the time to ensure everyone understands before moving on. She’s also very encouraging, making the lessons feel less like a chore and more like a rewarding experience. Overall, I’ve noticed a significant improvement in my English skills, and I feel much more confident now. I highly recommend this tuition center and Mdm Han to anyone looking to improve their English!

I have been in this tuition for almost a year now, and my English has definitely improved a lot! Mdm Han often shares her own stories to help us remember and understand her lessons better. She also incorporates newspaper articles into our lessons, which has encouraged me to read newspapers more. Her engaging teaching style has made learning English enjoyable and effective. Highly recommended!

Madam Han is an amazing and supportive teacher. Before I joined her classes, I found English really hard and confusing. My grammar was weak, my vocabulary was limited, and I struggled with comprehension. But after attending her lessons, I realized that English can actually be fun and easier to learn. Her lessons are clear, engaging, and enjoyable. She explains things in a way that makes English simple and easy to understand. Plus, she gets everyone involved, encouraging us to ask questions and participate. Because of her teaching, I’ve gained a lot more confidence in English. My grammar, vocabulary, and comprehension have all improved, and now I feel much better about the subject. Thanks to Madam Han, English doesn't seem so challenging anymore.

Spacious JE Centre Library with many seats availability. Cool Air-Cond Environment, Front Welcoming Display, Information,  Photo Wall Taking, Green Wall Digital Huge Screen & Clean well maintained Toilet. Great Place for Reading, Desktop PC Computer Internet access & comfy sofas. Wonderful!👏🇸🇬

Outstanding library. Convenient location (OPP Science Centre) and many floors or materials. Their program room is great for kid activities.

Surprisingly, toilets are badly maintained :(
Nice, quiet and with lots of books. Highly recommended library of you love reading!

Amazing! Just no words to say. It was my first time to the Jurong regional library and the very scale of it took me in awe. So many collections of different books and so many different levels to go and explore! They have a community space for events and workshops , then there is a cafe and bakery inside with good food options. There is so many different things to explore! I couldn’t even finish going through all the sections! I will definitely go there again soon. The place is also located very close to the MRT  station and the Jurong east bus terminus . It has a sheltered walkway so we could easily reach the place even though it was raining. Many people from all different age groups were there. There is study tables with charging points. For some of the study tables you need to book in advance but for the rest you can always find and empty space. There are DVDs and other stuff as well. If you have to borrow books you need to register , but I just went for reading so I didn’t have to register anything. The library closes at 9pm and is open all days of the week. They will announce over the mike before the closing time so you have time to pack your stuff. I especially liked the photography section and farming section . There’s a separate magazine section and newspaper section as well. Different languages from Tamil to Malay to Korean you will find in the library .

This library was visited few times in the span of 15 years. Still impressed by its cleanliness, calm and  peaceful atmosphere in addition to the silent air, the display of new arrivals also catch my eyes. Always full of study-loaded students and other book-lovers, you will still find a seat for enjoying some books or surfing internet on the provided PC's as the library is very spacious. Remarkably, there is a vast collection of books.
Found check point10, (SAFTI,) of Jurong heritage trail here

Surprised by the variety of food options, complete with both Halal and non-halal options.

Food is generally tasty and reaonable priced.

Ample carparks (at a fee) for both vehicles and heavy vehicles.

Washroom available on site as well.

Ample seats for customer to dine in.

Place can b warm on a hot day though.

​I decided to get the You Mian soup takeaway from Kah Toh Fook (家多福) for my daughter's lunch today. She gave it a huge thumbs-up, especially loved the generous amount of fried ikan bilis (炸江鱼仔) the owner added, which really enhanced the dish.
Only had a drink here but there was quite a good selection of food.

Food Master @ Jalan Bahar is a food court that open daily from 6 AM to 11 PM, it offers a variety of dishes from multiple stalls, including Korean, Thai, braised duck rice, and BBQ seafood. Some notable stalls and their offerings, including lobster Pao fan, Shun Heng BBQ, Shun Heng Prawn Noodle, even Bahn Mi. Overall, Food Master @ Jalan Bahar offers a diverse dining experience in a nostalgic and spacious setting, making it a great spot to enjoy a variety of delicious dishes.

Nov24. First time here which surprised my working partner. He said the prawn noodle is famous, thus try the pig tail bee Hoon with prawn @ 7. 2 pieces of prawn and a few pig tail. The soup was bit light, have to add some dry chilli powder. The prawn was fresh but the pig tail is only average. Overall average .. with so many food around the food master if you r not the soup type - just give a miss.

Very fresh seafood. The 4pax set is really value for money. The fish was big and very fresh. The prawns were not reared but wild ones from the sea, the sweetness (鲜甜) is way better than those tiger prawn in seafood restaurants. We were torn between the seafood soup and sea cucumber crab meat broth. In the end, we were appreciative that the boss made both for us, 2 pax portion each. The seafood soup is really food, thick slices of fish and very tasty minced pork. We changed the thai prawn to bak cheok, a cooking method which allowed us to appreciate the freshness of prawn. The boss has a ship and catches his own fish.

A nice spot for yummy fish soup! Affordable set options such as the Chicken cutlet rice & fish soup, or go for tzechar style dishes eg. Seafood soup (compared to the normal fish soup, this has a taste of Chinese wine), Sambal petai prawns (spicy & strong taste of hae bee hiam), or Seafood horfun (portion enough for 2 or 3 to share).

IG @pingguo301

Food: Ordered the lunch set instead of the premium fish soup. I thought it's good to be honest. There are pork within the soup and the fish tastes very fresh.

Service: Although we are given a buzzer, a lady still served my order to where I'm seated, great service here and I didn't expect that.

Atmosphere: Take a look at the photos I took. Very clearly, the owners put in a lot of efforts in the aesthetics of the restaurant.

If you are at this area, don't miss this place. Highly recommended

Good selection of fish to be cooked in various tasty ways. This restaurant boasts of fish and other seafood that’s not farmed but caught. A bit pricey but the quality and taste is very good. The owner Johnny is very experienced with seafood and will be able to keep you entertained with his experiences.

One of the best local restaurants in Jurong. Serving great fish soup and other good seafood dishes. I find most of the foods are quite authentic and original here, not the usual zichar taste. If you have budget, go for their fresh wild catch seafoods(availability depends on your luck). Boss is friendly, if he’s around he would check out on customer to ensure the food quality. My family moved to jurong 2 years ago, and this has been our family restaurant.

I knew nothing about boxing, but my husband pushed me to join this gym since it has both gym and boxing classes. I tried a free lesson, then signed up for 6 months. After two weeks, I really liked the vibe there. The coaches know their stuff and teach well. I love meeting new people, and the members are super nice and helpful. After two weeks, I extended my package to a year and don't regret it one bit! I love going to the classes. It's something different, and I always have a great time. I always look forward to going.

I started training at KOS in July 2025, and it has genuinely been a life-changing experience. Thanks to the intense and highly effective training sessions, I have lost 10kg and am finally back in my healthy weight range! The value here is incredible because for the price, you get access to both the amazing boxing classes and the gym. I absolutely love the high-energy boxing sessions, and the culture and positive vibe here are unmatched. Having 24/7 gym access is a massive plus, giving ultimate flexibility to train whenever it fits your schedule. The gym itself is always so clean, with a bright, fresh look, and it is packed with the latest equipment. Plus, I love that I never have to wait around for others to finish using it! A huge shoutout and thanks to Coach Kumar, Jude, Ebe, and Xander. They are incredibly motivating, patient, and always ensure we are executing the correct boxing techniques. Highly recommend KOS to anyone looking to smash their fitness goals!

Had a great experience during the free boxing trial. The coaches were friendly, patient, and made beginners feel comfortable. The training was fun and energetic, and the environment was very welcoming. Definitely recommend for anyone looking to start boxing or improve fitness!

I’ve trained at multiple gyms before, but nothing comes close to King of Strength Jurong. The gym is seriously awesome. They have all the right equipment to train every part of your body, and the membership price is relatively affordable compared to other gyms.

What makes KOS Jurong even better is the boxing side. They have a proper ring where you can step in and practise your skills, plus punching bags that are great for releasing stress without hurting your hands or wrists. The coaches are also friendly, passionate, and genuinely focused on helping you improve. They do not judge you. Instead, they motivate you to become a better boxer.

Overall, KOS Jurong has a great environment, solid equipment, affordable pricing, and amazing coaches. Definitely a 5-star gym.

King Of Strength Boxing Gym is more than just a gym to me — it’s a place that holds a very special place in my heart. From the moment I walked through the doors, I was welcomed into a supportive and motivating environment that feels like a second home.

The training here is top-notch, with sessions that challenge you physically and mentally while helping you grow stronger, more disciplined, and more confident. Whether you’re a complete beginner or an experienced boxer, everyone is treated with respect and encouraged to become the best version of themselves.

A special shoutout and mad respect to Coach Kumar and Coach Krishna. Their dedication, knowledge, patience, and passion for the sport are truly inspiring. They don’t just train fighters; they build character, instill discipline, and genuinely care about the progress and well-being of every member. The positive impact they’ve had on me and many others goes far beyond boxing.

What truly makes King Of Strength special is the strong sense of family and community. The friendships, support, and encouragement you’ll find here are something rare and valuable.

I’m incredibly grateful to be part of this gym and proud to call it my second home. If you’re looking for a boxing gym with excellent coaching, a welcoming environment, and a community that feels like family, King Of Strength Boxing Gym is the place to be.

Rifle Range Nature Park is a great extension to the Bukit Timah nature area, offering a mix of easy trails, lush rainforest scenery, and interesting glimpses into Singapore’s natural and historical heritage. The park is well connected, with clearly marked paths, boardwalks, and links to nearby nature reserves

One unexpected highlight was spotting a durian tree - and even seeing fallen durians on the ground, a reminder of how rich and diverse the forest ecosystem is. Along the trails, you’ll also find impressive giant trees, dense vegetation, quarry views, and remnants of the area’s past hidden among the greenery

I loved Rifle Range Nature Park — it’s very friendly for children and the elderly. The boardwalks are well-paved, anti-slip, and mostly barrier-free, so it’s easy for prams and seniors to walk comfortably.

The access is super convenient: just take Beauty World MRT Exit A, go to Level 4 of Beauty World Centre, and cross the overhead bridge that leads directly into the park. Highlights include the Quarry Wetland, Colugo Deck, and a fun Forest Exploration Trail for kids. Highly recommended for a relaxed family nature walk.

Nice new park with natural trails and paved paths. Has many unspoilt flora and fauna, saw several durian and starfruit trees along the paths. Not so easy to get to if depending on public transport. Toilets and carpark are available and some paths r equipped with CCTV! Not crowded on a weekday morning.

A nice trek away from the hustle and bustle of the city located in the Bukit Timah area of Singapore. Amidst a lush green rain forest with thriving ecosystems Easy to access and to reach on foot and public transport from the nearest MRT station called Beauty World - Downtown Line (Blue Coloured Line). An overhead bridge links one of the entrances of the park to the roof top Food Court (highest floor) of Beauty World Shopping Centre. Easy enough for amateur trekkers, family outings, a stroll in the park or a jog. There is a main building for meeting area, car drop off and pick up, giant ceiling fans to cool you down, chilled drinking water dispenser, vending machines, maps, loo and a park ranger on duty.  Good place to shelter if it rains There are some slopes on the way up to the cliff top of the old quarry with a stunning viewing Birds Eye area perched right at the top of the cliff. Not a long distance but short slightly steep slopes but totally worth the climb. Saw families with kids around the age of 5 and above at the top so it’s really not that challenging or difficult. Also Not as far as I expected. But do take note of your way back because if you take a wrong turn and end up on Rifle Range Road it’s more than a 1 hour walk to the nearest MRT station especially if you are already tired. However the paths are all well paved and safe. Do note the park closes at 7pm. You can also access this park from the Green Corridor.

About mid range level of difficulty. Lot of monkey though. Place is very peaceful and the lookout area is scenic. In awe. It’s connected to Rail Corridor as well, that one is pretty easy in difficulty level. It’s a literally straight road. Just need endurance for the distance.
The gate opens promptly for us to pick up our nephew.
Beautiful place, fresh air and noise free zone

This park connects the Pandas Garden and the West Coast area, with a canal stream running beside it. Although signs prohibit fishing, locals are sometimes seen trying their luck. The trees provide good shade, keeping it cool in the late afternoon. It offers a tranquil atmosphere with enough greenery between the HDB flats and nearby condominiums. Otherwise, nothing particularly special.

The only downside is that the pedestrian and vehicle paths share the same side, so you sometimes need to move aside for bicycles or vehicles. Another issue is that the signboard is blocked by electric booths, making it unclear to the public.

Pros
– Shady and cool in late afternoons
– Greenery and tranquility between residential areas
– Canal adds a natural touch

Cons
– Pedestrian and vehicle paths overlap
– Signboard blocked, unclear to the public

⸻

I would like to give feedback about Small Wonders Preschool (Taman Jurong).

First of all, I would like to sincerely thank Ms. Sally, the Principal, for welcoming us like family into the school. From the very beginning until the end, she made the entire process smooth and stress free for us. Her warmth, patience, and professionalism truly made a difference and gave us great confidence and comfort. She is truly the “Big Mummy” of the school. In fact, she is the main reason I decided to enrol my little boy at Small Wonders.

Coming to Ms. Sally’s team, Teacher Nika has been an amazing teacher, guiding my little boy with care and dedication until today. My son always talks about her at home and proudly says that Teacher Nika is his favourite teacher. He loves her so much, and as a parent, that means everything to me.

I would also like to thank Teacher Aida for her patience and care, especially during drop-off and pick-up times. Handling young children is never easy, but she always manages it with calmness and kindness. I always see my little boy cheekily “bullying” her to help him wear his shoes 😄

Choosing a preschool is all about trust, and Ms. Sally and her team have earned ours completely. What I value most is their commitment to keeping parents in the loop; their consistent updates on my children’s well-being took away all my worries. This was not our first time dining here, and we have always found the food quite enjoyable. However, this visit was a disappointing experience.
We ordered their signature pork belly stew, and when it arrived, there was an unpleasant smell that made us question whether the ingredients were fresh. After tasting it, we found that it had a sour taste. We were unsure whether it was due to the capsicum or if the pork belly itself was no longer fresh.
We ordered four portions of the stew. Two portions were already on our dining plates, while the other two were untouched on the serving dish. We informed the server that the dish tasted sour and raised our concern. However, the service recovery was disappointing. The two untouched portions were taken away, reheated, and returned to us, with the explanation that the kitchen had redone them. Unfortunately, after trying them again, the sour taste was still there.
The server then told us that this was how the dish was supposed to taste and that the capsicum was used to cover the pork belly smell.
We understand that mistakes can happen, but the way this situation was handled was not satisfactory. A better resolution would have been to offer an alternative dish or provide a refund, rather than returning the same dish after reheating it despite our concerns about the taste and freshness.
We were dining with young children, and our main concern was food safety. We were worried about the possibility of them falling sick after consuming food that did not seem fresh.
Unfortunately, we left feeling disappointed, not only with the food quality this time, but also with how the situation was handled by the service team.

Overall a nice place to chill and dine. Food wise, a good selection of western dishes to choose from. Personally, find the truffle fries and iberico pork good, and will recommend them. The other dishes we ordered was good as well. It was a good dining experience for my group of 6.

Chanced upon this eatery. It has a good spread of appetizers, mains and beverages. We tried the battered fried shiitake mushrooms, mushroom soup, pan seared halibut and salmon served with 2 sides (mashed potatoes & barley salad), and carbonara, they did NOT disappoint! Though the bill came up to ~$90 but the dishes were tasty and to my liking, particularly the fish (I’m a foodie and fussy with fish). I’d go back again from the north.

Wow wow wow! Their garlic chicken wings ARE AMAZING! Affordable and cozy place for Western food. Almost every dish was great! Ribeye steak was done well! Pizza was crisp thin but so delicious. Chicken cutlet was crispy and not dry at all. Wonderfully surprised! Will definitely be back again

Decided to try this place for dinner. Was not dissapointed.
Had the Fried Fish Fillet, Tomato Cheese Baked Portobello and the Grilled Ribeye Steak.
The fish was so so, pretty flat but tasty.
Portobello was fantastic the cheese and mushroom medly was exceptional and i highly recommend.
Steak came with a barley side and mash and was cooked perfectly to medium rare.
Recommend overall to try.
Decor was great but lights were a tad bit bright in some seat locations.

Went consecutively two days to try different drinks. Hubby loves the banana pudding while my favourite is hojicha latte! The tiramisu was great!

However the ice is a bit too much and the drinks on the pricey side. Overall still very recommended by the good quality dessert and drink!

Highly recommend visiting this home-based café if you’re from the west side!🌿

I’m in love with their banana pudding!! Super creamy and not too sweet!😋 Their brownies are delicious too especially with the sea salt touch.❤️

My favourite drink is definitely the Strawberry Cloud Matcha and I also recommend trying their Milo Hojicha. It is such an interesting and tasty combo!

Their dog is also super cute hahahah

Taman Jurong Shopping Centre is one of those classic heartland malls that may not look fancy, but is incredibly practical and convenient for everyday life. It has a very old-school neighborhood feel and almost everything you need can be found here

The biggest draw is definitely the large 24-hour NTUC FairPrice, which is great for grocery shopping at any time of the day or night. There are also plenty of food options nearby, from McDonald’s to the food court and various small eateries around the mall

What I like about this place is the mix of useful services packed into one location - clinics, bakeries, TCM shops, barber shops, beauty salons, and other small local businesses that have probably served the community for years. It feels authentic and functional rather than commercialized

Do not expect a modern luxury mall experience, as the building and some shops do feel a little dated

I am writing to express my profound appreciation and heartfelt commendation for one of your exceptional team members, Ah Long, whose outstanding service on Friday, 30 January 2026, left an indelible impression on our entire organization.

On that afternoon, our team visited your Jurong Superbowl outlet to purchase supplies for our upcoming community outreach event—8 cartons of mandarin oranges and 2 cartons of mineral water. What began as a routine shopping trip transformed into an extraordinary demonstration of customer service excellence that deserves the highest recognition.

Ah Long's dedication went far beyond the call of duty. With meticulous care and genuine concern for quality, he personally opened and inspected every single carton of mandarin oranges, ensuring that each piece met the highest standards. When he discovered items that fell short, he promptly arranged replacements without hesitation. This level of attention to detail speaks volumes about his commitment to customer satisfaction.

But his exceptional service did not stop there. Understanding the weight and volume of our purchases, Ah Long took the initiative to load everything onto a push cart. Then, in an act of kindness that truly moved us, he personally pushed the cart across the road to our office building and helped us transport everything up to the 3rd floor—all with a cheerful and willing spirit.

Perhaps what impressed us most was Ah Long's unwavering integrity. When my colleague attempted to offer him a monetary tip in appreciation for his extraordinary efforts, he politely but firmly declined. He even refused a simple drink of water, respectfully explaining that Sheng Siong's service policy guides his actions. This display of professionalism and principle is genuinely rare and deeply admirable.

Ah Long embodies the very best of what customer service should be: attentive, proactive, generous, and principled. His exceptional magnanimity and genuine desire to serve reflect not only his personal character but also the outstanding training and values that Sheng Siong instills in its team members.

Thank you for cultivating such remarkable talent. Your commitment to service excellence through team members like Ah Long sets a gold standard for the retail industry.

I would like to commend Yong Sheng from Anytime Fitness Taman Jurong for his outstanding service, responsibility, and professionalism.

When I encountered a serious issue where my gym pass could not be accessed at all through Apple Pay after changing my phone, Yong Sheng stepped in to assist me even though Taman Jurong is not my home club. My home club is Jurong East Central Blk 134, yet the staff member Ray did not make any effort to help me and simply brushed me off without providing assistance, follow-up, or solutions.

For three consecutive days, I had to personally text Yong Sheng to check whether he was on duty at Taman Jurong so that someone could help open the door for me. Despite having no obligation to assist a member from another club, Yong Sheng never ignored me, never showed frustration, and never treated my issue as a burden. Instead, he remained responsive, patient, and genuinely willing to help throughout the entire process.

This clearly shows what good service looks like. Yong Sheng could have chosen to ignore me, just as my home club did, but he chose to take responsibility and help. The contrast in service standards between Yong Sheng and Ray is very apparent. Yong Sheng demonstrated true customer care, while my home club failed to support me when I needed them most.

Yong Sheng is an excellent example of what Anytime Fitness staff should represent, and his dedication deserves to be recognised. Thank you Yong Sheng, sincerely from my heart. Kudos!

Watie's exceptional maintenance efforts ensure the equipment is always in top condition, and her organized activities foster a sense of community. Herzy's expertise as a trainer is unmatched, and his willingness to correct techniques and provide guidance to all members, not just his clients, is truly commendable. His jovial nature makes every interaction a pleasure.

Watie and Herzy form an incredible team, and their dedication has created a supportive and motivating environment. Kudos to the entire AFTJ team! We appreciate you, Watie and Herzy - keep up the fantastic work!
