A beautiful, relaxing and vibrant place for walks, for taking a step back from the hectic pace.
Spending 3 hours walking and appreciating the fresh air, the manicured gardens, the lake and wonderful architecture, helped to destress and reset.
If you've not been, well worth some time out.

Definitely one of the nicest and must go to parks in Singapore if you are alone, couples, families group of friends wanting to escape from the concrete jungle of the city. Jurong used to be perceived as a very faraway venue to commute to but with so many new MRT train lines it’s so much easier and faster to reach. Do take note of opening hours. Free entry and easily accessible with many entrances by Chinese Garden MRT or Lakeside MRT and buses. Great for all activities, walking , strolling, jogging, cycling, picnics and there is even a skate park and dog run. What makes this park stand out from the rest is the sheer size, the large lake with canoeing; stunning replicas of historical Chinese and Japanese architecture and pagodas, bonsai garden and wildlife. And especially the stunning Lily Flower Pond at the Japanese Garden and the Lotus Flower Pond at the Chinese Garden. Definitely worth to make a trip down to enjoy this green oasis escapade rich in flora and fauna and water. It’s very tranquil and peaceful from Mondays to Saturdays. Ample toilets and drinking water dispenser and also a beverage shop selling nice cold beverages.

NParks really did a good job in revamping this park.
There's plenty of interesting plants and wildlife to spot.
Took me and my friends 3 hours to casually explore the entire area.
It's definitely a nice place to run in the mornings/evenings.

I highly recommend to visit this gardens, as there are a lot of attractions to see and they are all free! It’s a beautiful area to dive into culture and nature, just 40 min from Singapore with the bus. Very relaxing and a moment to take a pause from the overtourism and consumistic view of marina bay and the Singapore center.
Not as big as the botanic garden, but I’d say it’s better to view this than that.
Really nice gardens. Will be even more pleasant to walk in when the trees in the still new Japanese and Chinese gardens grow older and provide more shade. Looking forward to that!

place_name: ADJ Tuition Centre & Student Care (Jurong West)
review_text:
My daughter has been with ADJ since P3. We are deeply grateful for the dedicated guidance and unwavering commitment. Under Mr Ang’s mentorship, my daughter not only grew tremendously in confidence but also achieved outstanding PSLE results, scoring AL1 for both Mathematics and Science, with an excellent overall AL5.

Beyond the results, what truly sets this tuition centre apart is how much my daughter genuinely enjoys learning here. She finds the lessons efficient, engaging, and highly effective, especially with Mr Ang’s clear, structured, and thoughtful teaching methods. Learning never felt stressful, but purposeful and motivating.

I strongly recommend Mr Ang without hesitation. He is not only an exceptional teacher who delivers results, but also someone who truly cares about his students’ character development and values. In all my years as a parent, I have never encountered a teacher like him. Thank you for believing in my daughter, pushing her to excel, and guiding her beyond academics. Your impact goes far beyond grades, and we are truly thankful for everything you have done.

place_name: ADJ Tuition Centre & Student Care (Jurong West)
review_text:
My daughter has been in studentcare since p3/4. In their previous studentcare, teachers don’t teach and plays more of a monitoring role (no tuition or teaching), and my daughters came home with some pages of homework they missed out doing.

But at ADJ, I never have to worry about homework or spelling (which are always done) because Mr Ang and teachers are very hands on.

In addition, kids are pushed further to their potential as Mr Ang gives them stretch assignments and tuition. I don’t teach my daughters at all or monitor them homework but they scored well. My younger daughter was an average student but she scored AL1 in math (she said the paper was easy compared to her practices at ADJ) and overall AL8 in PSLE this year which is a pleasant surprise.

My elder daughter has sec sch math tuition at ADJ and scored A1 in both a math and e math.

Most importantly my kids dun dread gg to ADJ as it’s fun and rewarding for them. As parents this really takes a huge load off our shoulders and we focus on spending quality family time together. The fees are reasonable and affordable as well.

place_name: ADJ Tuition Centre & Student Care (Jurong West)
review_text: When I first started, my chinese was very bad and when my teachers challenged me to speak chinese for a whole day, I struggled tremendously. Now, with the help of Mr Ang and their team of helpful teachers, my chinese improved widely and I am now more able to speak with my family at home with a language i was previously unfamiliar with. A great thanks to my teachers for making my learning enjoyable and effective at the same time. Not only did my Chinese improve but my Maths , Science and English too. When I joined Science tuition in primary 3, my science always scored around 60 but in the span of a month, I managed to score 95 in my end of year! Now, for PSLE, I scored AL2 for Science. After being tutored for 3 years, I am strong in all 4 subjects and scored remarkably well for my PSLE and I can finally qualify for my dream school! Mr Ang and all the teachers used unique and effective methods that made everything simpler.

place_name: ADJ Tuition Centre & Student Care (Jurong West)
review_text: My A Math results have improved significantly after attending tuition for less than a year. I went from a D7 to a B4, and I’m really grateful for the guidance and support provided. The tutor was patient and always took the time to explain methods in a way that made sense. Thanks to this tuition, I’ve gained confidence and a much stronger foundation in A Math. This small Bangladeshi shop below the HDB block near NTU has completely changed my life and has made me feel so much at home. This shop imports fish that are loved by all Bengalis in both Bangladesh and India, and if you're Bengali, you'll know how important it is to eat the right fish for your mental satisfaction. They import Bengali favorites like Rohu, Katla, Ilish and Chingri, but even has the dark horses like Pabda, Tengra, and Koi. The Rohu and Katla are of decent weight (above 3 Kg), and hence the fish tastes sweet, has longer bones and the belly (পেটি) has the right amount of fat. The Ilish quality is also very good. The mutton is also of very good quality and much cheaper than what you get in supermarkets. The owner is an extremely nice person, and he cleans the fish, along with the scales, and you can also ask him your preferred cut of mutton. If you're Bengali and live around Jurong West, do not give this shop a miss.

Stepping into the Omni-Theatre at Science Centre Singapore felt like entering a completely different world. This isn’t your usual cinema — the massive dome screen and immersive surround sound make you feel like you’re inside the movie, whether you’re flying through space or exploring nature. The visuals are incredibly sharp and engaging, and it’s a great mix of entertainment and learning for both kids and adults.
One big tip: visit on a weekday. That’s truly the best time to go. The crowd is much smaller, and it feels almost like you have the theatre to yourself, especially if you’re going with family. The experience is more relaxed, quieter, and way more enjoyable without the usual weekend rush.
Ticket prices are also very reasonable. Singaporeans and Permanent Residents pay around $8, while tourists pay about $14, which is excellent value considering how unique the experience is compared to a regular movie theatre.
What I also love is how convenient the location is. After the Omni-Theatre, you can easily spend more time nearby. KidsSTOP™ is perfect for younger children with hands-on learning and play, while Snow City is just next door if you want something fun and cooling. It’s easy to turn this into a full half-day or even full-day family outing.
Overall, the Omni-Theatre is a great place to slow down, learn something new, and enjoy quality time together. If you’re looking for something different to do in Singapore — especially on a weekday — this is definitely worth checking out.

Brought my kids here today. It was pleasant even though 20mins into the show, one fell asleep and the other one was looking forward to the “good part” as we were expecting a show about space. However it was more of a NASA showcase. Commented that she came to learn about space, not rockets 😅

Not all of the footage made use of the unique omni dome but this could be due to the film’s origin. Sound could be improved. We sat at the top near the exit. Because the back speakers were off, the show lacked impact. It was also warm as if the aircon was off.

Parking was full. Some cars had to park on the side road inside carpark. Understood from staff that this was unusual as it’s the last day of Robofest at Science Centre. Perhaps some traffic control is required for busy days such as today.

We’ll come again for other shows and hope that the management look into the above especially the show experience. Still think it’s a core memory opportunity for kids as it did for me when I visited the omni theatre back in my school days.

As part of SG60, there was a promotion to visit the science centre and the Omni theatre free.  It was a bit confusing though as although the ticket listed this two places as free, you would have to book separately for each movie slot.  I learned later that each admission ticket that day could book one movie.  However they opened up another queue for walk in admission.  And I believed anyone who used the walk in managed to go in.

I managed to watch all the films that day except for Stars Dreaming.  Animal Kingdom was the most interesting film with nice visuals and interesting facts.  Cities of the future was quite a boring one that covered a few cities around the world on the issues of environment sustainability.  Some audiences actually walked out of it.  Kitz the cat's Secret of the Space Station was the only film with a storyline and also made use of the Onimax effect to make you nauseous and dizzy in a few scenes.  It was an animation targeted at children although the theme can be quite dark.  It was still educational.  I believed it was a Korean production and some dialogues were quite awkward. Overall I would only recommend Animal Kingdom.

I found the dome screen rather dim though.  But all the pixels on the screen were working fine.  The images were not clear and the animal kingdom felt like a low Res film.  The theatre is digital 8k though according to the staff.

In terms of environment, you need to put up with talking children and eating.  Even the mother next to me kept using her hp and I need to tell her about it.  Later she started to provide narration to her child.  So be prepared to put up with the annoyances of these people.

There were a lot of stairs climbing up and down.  If you are mobility challenged you could seek help from the staff and gained access via a lift.

This theatre along with the science centre would be torn down for another new building.  Not sure would there be an Omni theatre in the future.

Long ago I watched other shows like Grand Canyon, Space walk and diving in the sea.  All were good documentaries.

Got vouchers and brought my kids (5, 10, 12 and 14yo) to watch "Ocean Paradise". The venue is very clean (no eating and drinking allowed), comfortable and has a magnificent dome screen. The movie length of 35min was perfect for my 5yo (not too long) but also not so short that the trip wasn't worth it for my 14yo. The images from this show were stunningly beautiful and the messaging was clear. Nothing scary whatsoever, which is great for my easily-scared kids, but still having a strong storyline to keep everyone engaged throughout. Volume of sounds were also not too loud (unlike movie theaters) but still clear. Do check out the highly rated Indian Food stall just outside the omnitheater, or adjourn to the Regale for some pasta (tts what we ate anyways) if spice is not an option. Bottom line: fantastic immersive movie experience for kids (and adults)!!

I think the omni is losing its touch. The documentary is very boring. People expect to feel " they are in the movie" and yet we only feel like it is looking at a documentary. A lot of people end up sleeping.

Nanyang Community Club is located near Pioneer MRT Station, and while I couldn't find specific details on its wheelchair accessibility, many community clubs in Singapore are designed to be accessible.

Here are some general accessibility features you might find in Singapore's community clubs ¹ ² ³:
- *Ramps and Lifts*: Many community clubs have ramps and lifts to facilitate easy access for people with mobility issues.
- *Accessible Toilets*: Community clubs often have toilets designed for people with disabilities, equipped with grab bars and lowered sinks.
- *Wide Pathways*: Some community clubs have wide pathways that allow for easy movement of wheelchairs.

To confirm Nanyang Community Club's accessibility features, you can:
- *Contact the Club Directly*: Reach out to Nanyang Community Club to inquire about their accessibility features.
- *Check with the People's Association*: The People's Association manages many community clubs in Singapore and may have information on accessibility features.

Nanyang Community Club's operating hours are from 10 am to 6 pm, daily. If you're planning to visit with your boss to take photography, consider calling ahead to confirm accessibility features ⁴.

Child Vaccination Center is located on 2nd floor of the community club. It is run by Raffles Medical and is well organized. Registration to vaccination to 30 minutes observation area and discharge area are all within the same air conditioned hall.

Very clean, handy free transport link options between beaches. Lots of activities and eateries.  Easy access for those with poor mobility, pushchairs, etc
Family friendly
Beautiful beach! There is a free shuttle bus around siloso beach.
A beautiful, clean, and safe beach. Filled with tourists and Singaporean locals alike. I once spent a quiet holiday here with someone I loved.
Blk 273A is a corner block unobstructed view facing main junction along jalan bahar. Love this block location!
Cool place

Transferring my child to this school was the best decision ever! She’s been at MapleBear Jurong West from Nursery all the way to K2 and we have absolutely no regrets.The school is amazing at balancing academics with holistic development. As parents who do not speak Mandarin at all, we're honestly wowed by how much her Chinese has improved. It’s given her a really strong foundation for Primary School. A huge thank you to Teacher Enci and Xiao Xi Lao Shi for all their love, patience, encouragement, and support ♥️. You've both played such a big part in her academic and social growth. The school environment is so warm and nurturing, and the teachers genuinely care about the kids. I highly recommend this school to any parent looking for a happy and supportive place for their child!😍

One thing we really like is that the school has its own kitchen and prepares fresh meals daily. It gives us peace of mind knowing what our child is eating. The teachers are caring and the overall environment is warm and welcoming.

This school is more about babysitting than education. Although My kid stayed there short term,  I found there’s no structured teaching or knowledge reinforcing system like other schools i toured. It’s substandard in Singapore education. If you want your kids to learn, it’s not the right place.   That explains why only this school has school bus—补充对在教育方面的缺陷吧。

Additionally, this school is a family run business. It does save costs but I find that so unprofessional!!

It could be the best kindergarten in Jurong region.  Their systematic teaching methods, dedicated team, responsible teachers, and clean&tidy campus environment have allowed my kid to spend three happy years here.  Highly recommend!

As another parent at this school, I see things differently:

1. Babysitting" vs. Learning?
My kid learns through play—problem-solving, sharing, exploring. No, they’re not drilling worksheets, but they’re excited to go every day and come home full of stories. That’s learning too!

2. Family-Run = Unprofessional?
Actually, it feels more like a community. The teachers know every kid, and the principal is always around. Big chains can feel impersonal—here, it’s like extended family.

3. School Bus = Red Flag?
Haha, if anything, the bus is a *perk*! Not many schools offer one. We chose this place becauseit’s chill and kid-friendly

Every family has different needs, but we love  here. Maybe it’s not “Singapore’s fanciest,” but it’s been great for us!

all my kids went to this childcare when they were young. they have great teachers who impacted my kids till this day. (they are always talking about teacher sabrina and 李老师) and also terrific lessons like teaching the kids basic math and all which really helped my kids when they entered primary school. (my kids had great achievements in primary school and even went to gep). really a very good preschool to send your child to
One of the best Child care! I like the rigor they put into their curriculum.

Overall, Millennia Institute serves as a valuable alternative pathway to the A-Levels. It is particularly suitable for students who prefer a steadier academic pace, appreciate a supportive environment, and are willing to take ownership of their learning over a longer period.

horrible school, horrible bio.dept, little to NO support to students due to lack of funds 🤑, they’re falling behind other jc.

the exam papers are so easy that they provide a sense of security for students, not preparing them for the actual examination ahead.

Worst place ever. So many Hdb are near it and so many events are happening there like sports day which they always need to shout plus Every Monday or Friday night a very big light will be flashed at the Hdb at 446A and 445A literally can u stop using a mic I could hear it on the forth floor and it made my baby cry. Like why the hell do you need to make the residents nearby mad?

As an alumni, this is a piece of advice I can give you. Only join if you think that you have the discipline and determination in order to excel in your A-levels. Over the course of three years, there is a possibility of getting distracted due to the relatively laid-back environment. Not to mention, you may also come across negative influences from your peers. While the school does provide the necessary resources to help you pass your exams, it ultimately depends on you to decide if you are willing to put in the extra effort and strive for excellence.

Was invited to give the valedictorian speech during 2007 institute day but another name ended up on the roll of honour. If you wanted someone to fulfil just that segment of the event, you could just record that ‘outstanding student’s speech and play it during the event. Shouldn’t have wasted my time.

I'm delighted to see how much more vibrant and shadier the garden has become since 2007, the year my wife and I had our wedding photos taken. It's reassuring that the core structures and pavilions are still standing, now part of a beautifully improved and transformed landscape.

Not just a garden but also the new entrance of current University. The location are not easy to access as it doesn’t locate near to MRT. The current “ Paifang (牌坊) “ who stand in the garden are the duplicate of the original who located near to Pioneer MRT. So for those who wish to visit the historical monuments may consider to visit the different site.

The place is more on greens. Little of flowers.
Is good for people who  likes quite and relaxing place. May favourite is their little house in the  center ( I don't know how to called that). So uniquely beautiful  😍. 👍👍

beautiful park to hangout with your buddies or just spacing out yourself. there are red gazebo that has few seats and table if you want to have a lunch or snack. very contrast with the park that full of green. also there is mini waterfalls. you could take mesmerizing picture over there.

Lovely garden. It's especially beautiful during golden hour. I enjoy sitting under the pavilions and hearing the cicadas. It's just a little unfortunate that the traffic sounds are quite loud even in the middle of the garden.
became a better place after renovation 😍 good for doing early morning and evening exercise at there 😘

I recommend Yunnan Park because
Yunnan Province is famous for its unique geographical environment and diverse vegetation, and Yunnan parks have beautiful flowers, lakes, gardens and so on.
The park contains elements that showcase local culture, history or art. Including sculpture, architecture, cultural activities, etc.
The park has perfect infrastructure, such as benches, public toilets, restaurants, amusement facilities and so on. The park is clean, tidy and safe. Good service and convenient facilities usually enhance the visitor experience.
There are various recreational activities in the park, such as musical performances.

Nice place to relax at night and also do some bodyweight exercises
The playground is suitable for children, exercise corner has many equipment.
There is a also a huge gate at the entrance. Probably has some cultural value, not sure. Looks classy though.
Its newly upgraded. Wait for the trees to grow fuller then it'll be really nice
Lovely park in the neighborhood, where one can exercise. They have some equipments to help you exercise like pull up bar, parallel bars, etc. Not crowded especially during this covid times.

Mr Andrew is an excellent tutor who found many different ways to keep the lessons fun, engaging and informative using lots of different animations. His teaching style helped improve my grades in math and science a bunch. So grateful for his help!!

Teacher Andrew is a great teacher who makes Science interesting. He also cares about the students wellbeing too. After studying there for 6 years, I achieved a A2 in the GCE O level Examinations. Thanks Teacher Andrew for being patient and teaching me well!

Andrew is an exceptional tutor. He is engaging and explains math and scientific concepts clearly, helping my daughter build real confidence. My daughter enjoys the lessons and has made noticeable improvements. Highly recommended!

Teacher Andrew is an amazing maths tutor! Before I started lessons with him, I found some maths topics really confusing and wasn’t very confident in exams. He explains everything in a really clear and simple way, and he’s very patient when going through questions step by step.
His lessons helped me understand the concepts much better. He also shared helpful tips on how to approach different types of questions.Thanks to his guidance and support, I was able to improve a lot and got an A in Maths! I’m really grateful for all the help and would definitely recommend Teacher Andrew!

Teacher Andrew is very knowledgeable and explains difficult topics in a simple and easy-to-understand way. My son’s grades improved and scored A in O level ,he is also very responsible and always well-prepared for lessons.

I frequent this bus stop as 179 is the only bus from Pioneer that enters NTU. The sign informs commuters of the bus services available at the bus stop

1. Code choice
English is the only language iterated on the sign. This could possibly be due to the limited space on the sign itself, leading to the need to pick a language a majority of commuters would understand.

2. Inscription
The bus sign incorporates mainly shades of green, black font, and yellow. The black font written against the green background highlights the numbers a lot more and the nature of the green background itself captures the attention of commuters.

3. Emplacement
The sign is located at the front of the bus stop and is double-sided so commuters from either side of the bus stop are able to read the information. Its location allows for the commuters to immediately know which bus services are available
***257 doesn't exist*** Thank fully was able to reroute with Gmaps! +1 star for that, but still oh em gee
Bus 255 no longer stops here. Please update the information.
Next to Pioneer train station. A food court is near by.
257 no longer exist on this route. Pls update!

This MRT Station is strategically located in Jurong East with two popular shopping malls in the area directly connected to it and with the IMM shopping mall and the Ng Teng Fong Community Hospital just a bit farther away from the aforementioned two malls. Therefore this MRT Station sees a heavy footfall. As I live in Hillview, I can easily get to Jurong East by taking bus number 184 followed by a one stop MRT ride from Clementi MRT Station to Jurong East MRT Station.

I have headed here in singapore to head to IMM mall. Metro ride was good. Im thrilled to see everyone using metro besides personal transport. Most of the people are very friendly giving directions when needed. Security team and staff are so helpful in showing the directions. I depended so much on them as I visited and took train for the first time. There was some confusion when taking the circle change in the station.

I love in the West. I've been using the JE MRst exchange since 1991. The ceiling plan hasn't changed at all since then.

Until recently, this roof wasn't washed much at all and even now it's hard to get rid ofnth grime and bird waste accumulating here. I understand that this is very difficult given the business of the interchange. But something has to be done.

I cannot believe my eyes! Totally different from 20 years ago.

I have not been to Jurong East MRT station for 20 years. When I stepped out of the train nowadays, I was shocked. It has changed so much!

20 years ago, this was just a normal station. Now, it looks like a whole new modern city. It is very interesting and well-developed. Here is why I was so impressed:

If you have not been here in a long time, you must visit. It is amazing to see how much Singapore has built and improved this area. It is a true transformation!

Jurong East MRT Station is a very convenient interchange station connecting both the North-South Line and East-West Line. It is well organized and makes travelling across Singapore easier, especially for people working or living in the west area.

The station is directly connected to nearby shopping malls like JEM and Westgate, which is very useful for shopping and dining after travel. Clean environment and clear signboards make transfers smooth even during busy hours. Highly recommended interchange station in Singapore.

It was my first time trying out this brand of mala. Such a disappointment, I bought dry mala for take away but when I got home & try, the taste was soooo bland and tasteless. 👎 Wasted my $15 and journey there.

My fave halal mala spot finally reached JURONGGGGGG!!! I will always make my way to the first outlet at Jalan Kayu and it’s always been consistent. Staffs are always friendly and im happy to say that the jurong outlet one hits exactly the same. There are variety of ingredients like my favourite smoked duck, fatty meat, quail egg, sweet potato noodles (ooo so delicious how it absorbs the mala flavour) andddd aplenty of seafood meat.

However, sad to see that tang tea’s business in this outlet is not the best here. Im hopeful it’ll flourish after Yisilan made its appearance here. I pray more customers come this way and make it lively in this quiet neighbourhood.

Hehe looking forward to more mala feasting 👍🏽👍🏽

Ordered from grab.. So happy to have another halal mala option in Jurong.Bought the dry mala.So much variety of ingredients to choose from.

Previously i took the Hot spicy level which need to add on $0.50 but i personally would recommend to just stick to the medium spicy if you’re a spicy lover as the hot spicy level is very spicy even for someone like me who have very high spice tolerance.

Total that i paid including delivery was only $21.93 which in my opinion very affordable as it come in a huge tub,4 variety of meat which can easily be eaten by 2 pax.  It’s very delicious too. And they separate the peanuts in a container so for those who don’t eat the peanut they don’t have to pick it out.

place_name: Wan Xing Mala Xiang Guo 万兴麻辣香锅
review_text:
We went there last week, and the hotpot shop is really nice. The meat quality is good, the soup is flavorful, and everything tastes fresh. The environment is quiet and comfortable, which makes it a great place to enjoy a meal.

We would definitely come back again.

One suggestion: it would be even better if there were more sauce options, such as a seafood sauce or ready-mixed sauces. That would really complete the experience.

place_name: Wan Xing Mala Xiang Guo 万兴麻辣香锅
review_text: Literally the worst mala ever. Mala for 2 pax total cost $30. Expensive compared to other places but not worth it at all. Mala was too dry and we gave the cashier lady to add in more sauce but it tasted even worse after re-cooking.  Could not even finish the food. Waste of money and time.

place_name: Wan Xing Mala Xiang Guo 万兴麻辣香锅
review_text: not bad, portions are most suitable for 2 so plan accordingly. Place opens until 2am so skip the dinner crowd for a more quiet and relaxed neighborhood atmosphere 😁

place_name: Wan Xing Mala Xiang Guo 万兴麻辣香锅
review_text: Ordered the chongqing grilled fish ($25) at mild spicy level, which turned out to be not spicy at all and very delicious! Portion was very big and I'd say it's suitable for 3pax. Also appreciated that we could order via the QR code on our table. Service was friendly too. Thumbs up!

place_name: Wan Xing Mala Xiang Guo 万兴麻辣香锅
review_text: Broccoli, cabbage, baby cabbage, shiitake mushroom, black fungus, 3 fishball, 4 crabsticks, pork belly, 2x noodles. Portion for about 1.5pax. $24. Not cheap for the quality. Meat is decent, tough. Flavorwise, it's below average compared to food court malas. No mala taste, only salt and oil. There's Aircon here.
Love the takoyaki, honey waffle and good portion for their mains. And they always have new snacks, very affordable price too. Definitely coming back again. 😬😬😬

Came across an extremely rude cashier today who was honestly not happy to serve customers. She looked visibly unhappy and answered in a rude tone when asked about what was available or if the food was ready (we did not rush her at all). Would not patronise again if it’s the same cashier.
Tasty food for good price , Friendly seller . Got chicken popcorn rice for 4$.

I ordered the Thai Crab Fried Rice at this food stall in the stadium, and it was honestly terrible. The crab meat wasn’t even cooked properly it had a strong unpleasant smell that made it impossible to eat. The dish was completely inedible and I ended up wasting over $7 for nothing. Very disappointed with the quality and hygiene. Definitely not coming back.

A quick and affordable hidden gem serving drinks and snacks, tucked on the second floor near the entrance to the swimming pool. Would come back to support when I'm  in the area for gym, swimming or a run.
Multiple varieties of Indian Groceries and Fresh Vegetables, Good Housekeeping. Easy assesibility. Location at Blk 153, Jalan Teck Whye.
Good shop to find lots of Indian products at reasonable rates. Friendly staff and well maintained shop

Good Indian Vegetable Shop in Jurong West!!!

AMMAN Vegetable Shop at Jurong West Street 91 Market is a very good place for fresh vegetables, especially for Indian groceries. The produce is fresh, well-stocked, and reasonably priced.

The shop is convenient for daily shopping, and you can find many Indian vegetables that are not easily available in other supermarkets.

Overall, a reliable and helpful Indian grocery shop in the neighborhood. Highly recommended!
Good, Indian provisions shop with fresh supplies and all grocery necessities. Even fish and chicken also available.
All India vegetables and fruits( fresh now) grocery items available

I frequent this specific 711 in my neighbourhood, and often times meet a friendly uncle, Kumar. He makes us feel welcome, and always brings conversation. He is a friendly face, and a kind uncle who ensures we get the best. Reminding us of promotions to receive discounts etc.
the cashier josephine ( ? ) a middle aged woman was an amazing cashier ! she guided my helper and i on how to cook he rice with patience ! best customer service i had in 7 - 11

Staff dunno how to cancel the payment. Cannot help customer solve problem. Coffee machine not available, staff dun want to clean. Therefore no value meal until staff happy and free to clean the machine.
Please train your staff before they can work alone in the shop.
Divya has a very good customer service

Disrespectful attitude service, when asked  can i help you from the counter sitting staff ,i reply in friendly tone that is ok i just looking around,but the staff immediately reply me by saying JUST SEEING THINGS ONLY HUH.....i speechless at that moment, thought maybe his style of talking doent meant anything,thn i approach to him asking some cashcard issue,thn he answer my question when sitting down,i cant see him for a moment due to high counter desk with short chair he sitting...felt like disturb his relaxing time during afternoon working time...really?seriously?
super fresh seafood!!! the salmon is sooo good seasoned with just salt. we also had the sotong and stingray which we cooked with sambal - delish & def will have them again!
Good service from Ding Xin. Fish delivered is fresh. Paper parcel baked lemon fish is still fresh and sweet after stored in freezer for 2 weeks. 👍

I’ve been getting my seafood from Ding Xin since the Covid days, and I’ve never looked back. They’re a lovely family-run business all the way in the west, while I stay in the east—but good service and fresh seafood are worth the distance. Always prompt, always reliable, and they really take pride in what they do. No frills, just good honest business with a personal touch. Found them through word of mouth, and I’ve been recommending them ever since. If you’re looking for quality seafood with service you can count on, this is the place 🥰

Just tried Ding Xin Seafood online for the first time and I'm impressed! We got a bunch of different seafood - salmon, red snapper, sea bass, and more - and it was all super fresh and delicious. The fish had no bones, not even tiny ones, and the prawns were sweet and firm. We cooked them in different styles - steam fish, with soup,  stir-fried, air-fried, and grill; and everyone loved them. Highly recommend Ding Xin Seafood to fellow seafood lovers! Thanks for the great experience!
Salmon was fresh and plump, prawns were sweet after BBQ-ing, Stingray was supple
